title: Single-chamber air strut for cross-axle commonisation
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace bespoke front and rear triple-chamber air struts with a commonised single-chamber, switchable-volume design sharing piston and rolling-lobe geometry across axles. Reduces unique part numbers, tooling sets and validation cycles while retaining levelling and comfort. Targeted at high-volume premium SUV platforms where chamber complexity drives cost without commensurate ride benefit on touring-biased variants.

ideaData.title: Single-chamber air strut for cross-axle commonisation
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline uses three-chamber air struts with axle-specific rolling-lobe diameters, driving 4+ unique struts. The change consolidates to one switchable single/dual-volume architecture with shared piston rod and bellows tooling, axle tuning via valve-block orifice and damper map. Strut body machined from 6082-T6 aluminium; alternatives include AlSi10MnMg HPDC for the upper mount, 6014-T4 for formed brackets, and forged AM60B magnesium for the spring perch to cut unsprung mass. Bellows reinforced with aramid vs baseline polyamide cord. Levelling retained through ride-height sensors and ECU calibration, decoupling hardware from tune.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates two bellows vulcanisation tools into one and shares the piston-rod forging die, cutting tooling spend ~40%. Alternative processes: HPDC for upper mounts vs machined billet, and roll-formed vs stamped lower brackets. Reduces unique strut part numbers from four to two, lowering assembly-line complexity, kitting errors and aftersales SKUs while keeping bellows weld/crimp count constant.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Part-number consolidation across axles cuts tooling and validation spend.

ideaData.riskNotes: Single-chamber struts give a flatter spring rate than triple-chamber, risking reduced high-frequency isolation and a firmer secondary ride. Mitigated by CDC damper retuning and switchable volume to preserve comfort/sport split. Levelling authority and load-bearing for heavy PHEV variants validated to full GVW; crash load paths through the dome mount unchanged. Off-road articulation maintained via extended bellows stroke; premium ride character preserved through ECU calibration.

title: Integrated single-stage compressor with on-strut accumulator
description: [ICE,MHEV] Replace remote twin-piston compressor plus separate steel reservoir with a single-stage compressor packaged with a polymer-lined accumulator and integrated dryer. Cuts air-line length, fittings and leak points while sustaining fill rate for kneel and levelling. Suited to ICE/MHEV touring SUVs where rapid simultaneous-corner lift is less critical than packaging and cost.

ideaData.title: Integrated single-stage compressor with on-strut accumulator
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline twin-piston compressor with separate ~5L steel reservoir needs long nylon air lines and many push-fit fittings. The change integrates a single-stage compressor, regenerative dryer and a 2L accumulator into one module mounted near the valve block, shortening lines. Housing options: AlSi10MnMg HPDC vs glass-filled PA66-GF35 polymer vs AE44 magnesium for the bracket; reservoir from blow-moulded HDPE with aluminium 6061-T6 end caps replacing deep-drawn steel. Reduced dead volume improves fill efficiency; physics favour shorter pneumatic paths lowering pressure drop and compressor duty cycle, improving durability.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: HPDC compressor housing vs machined billet, and blow-moulded polymer reservoir vs deep-drawn welded steel, removing two weld seams and a leak-test station. Alternative: AE44 Mg die-cast bracket. Integration cuts fittings ~30%, shortens nylon line BOM, and merges three sub-assemblies into one module, reducing assembly time and warranty leak claims.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Module integration removes fittings, lines and leak paths.

ideaData.riskNotes: Single-stage compressor reduces peak fill rate, slowing four-corner simultaneous lift, risking perceived sluggishness during off-road kneel and entry/exit. Mitigated by accumulator pre-charge buffering instantaneous demand. Polymer reservoir validated for fatigue across -40 to +85°C and burst margin to 3x working pressure. NVH addressed via isolated mounts; durability of regenerative dryer preserves moisture control, protecting valve block and preventing winter freeze-up.

title: Frequency-selective passive damper replacing base CDC
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] On comfort-trim SUVs, substitute electronically controlled CDC dampers with mechanical frequency-selective (amplitude/frequency-dependent) twin-tube dampers. Delivers most of the ride-comfort benefit passively, deleting the damper ECU, wiring, and solenoid valves. Targets high-volume base and mid trims where adaptive damping take-rate is low and cost dominates buyer decisions.

ideaData.title: Frequency-selective passive damper replacing base CDC
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline base-trim uses CDC continuously variable dampers with proportional solenoid and ECU. The change fits a frequency-selective twin-tube damper with an amplitude-sensitive bypass and an add-on hydraulic frequency valve that softens high-frequency low-amplitude inputs without electronics. Tube from cold-drawn E235 steel; piston rod hard-chrome alternatives include induction-hardened C45 or stainless 1.4057; valve discs from spring steel 51CrV4. Physics: a secondary flow path opens above a tuned frequency, decoupling damping from velocity to mimic adaptive comfort. Deletes solenoid, harness and control unit, preserving passive fail-safe behaviour.
ideaData.materialGrade: E235 tube / 51CrV4 valve steel / C45 rod
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Roll-formed/cold-drawn tube and stamped valve discs replace solenoid machining and coil winding; alternative processes: precision stamping vs fine-blanking for discs, and induction hardening vs hard-chrome plating for rods. Deletes solenoid sub-assembly and ECU, cutting electronic BOM, one harness branch and an EOL calibration step, simplifying corner assembly.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Passive frequency valve removes adaptive-damper electronics.

ideaData.riskNotes: Passive dampers cannot adapt to driver-selected modes, risking loss of body-control in sport driving and on undulating off-road terrain versus CDC. Mitigated by restricting deletion to comfort trims and pairing with anti-roll tuning. Frequency valve durability validated to 200k cycles; NVH harshness over sharp edges addressed by amplitude-sensitive bypass. Crash and load paths unaffected; premium secondary ride preserved on motorway surfaces.

title: 48V e-ARB roll stabilisation replacing hydraulic ARS
description: [MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace 48V/hydraulic active roll-stabilisation (hydraulic pump, lines, accumulators) with electromechanical 48V e-ARB actuators on front and rear bars. Removes the hydraulic circuit and its leak/warranty exposure while improving response latency and energy efficiency. Best fit for MHEV/PHEV/BEV platforms already carrying a 48V or HV supply for the actuators.

ideaData.title: 48V e-ARB roll stabilisation replacing hydraulic ARS
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline active anti-roll uses a hydraulic pump, valves, lines and rotary actuators drawing engine power. The change fits 48V electromechanical roll actuators with a planetary/strain-wave gearset splitting each anti-roll bar, controlled by torque demand from a lateral-dynamics ECU. Bar halves from forged 51CrV4 spring steel or tube hardened 34CrMo4; housing AlSi10MnMg HPDC vs machined 7075-T6 vs AE44 Mg for mass. Strain-wave flexspline from 1.4057 stainless. Physics: applying counter-torque proportional to lateral acceleration cancels body roll faster than hydraulics, with regenerative decoupling on straight roads saving energy.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Forged anti-roll bar halves and HPDC actuator housings replace hydraulic pump machining, hose crimping and accumulator fabrication; alternatives: strain-wave vs planetary gear, and HPDC vs forged housing. Deletes the entire hydraulic circuit BOM (pump, hoses, fluid, accumulators), removing fill/bleed stations and reducing assembly leak-test complexity and warranty fluid claims.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Electromechanical actuation deletes the hydraulic ARS circuit.

ideaData.riskNotes: e-ARB introduces a 48V high-current actuator with functional-safety (ASIL-D) demands; failure must default to a passive stabiliser bar for safe handling. Mitigated by mechanical fail-stiff coupling and redundant sensing. NVH from gear whine controlled by helical/strain-wave design and isolation. Off-road requires full-decouple articulation, validated for axle travel; crash loads through subframe unchanged; premium flat-cornering preserved with lower latency than hydraulics.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BMW X5 (2019) Active Roll Stabilisation programme

title: Monotube conversion for high-trim CDC corner dampers
description: [PHEV,BEV] Convert high-trim adaptive corner dampers from twin-tube to monotube CDC architecture with external solenoid. Better heat rejection for heavy PHEV/BEV masses on sustained downhill, plus reduced fade and a lighter, simpler tube assembly. Targets performance and towing variants where damper thermal duty is highest.

ideaData.title: Monotube conversion for high-trim CDC corner dampers
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV/BEV. Baseline twin-tube CDC traps heat between tubes, fading under repeated high-energy inputs from heavy battery-laden SUVs. The change adopts a monotube with a floating gas piston and externally mounted CDC valve, exposing the single wall directly to airflow for better cooling. Tube from seamless cold-drawn E355 vs welded E235; rod from induction-hardened C45 or 1.4057 stainless; gas piston from PA66-GF30 vs aluminium AlSi10MnMg. Physics: higher gas pressure prevents cavitation and the larger piston area gives more consistent damping force under thermal load, improving fade resistance for towing and downhill descents.
ideaData.materialGrade: E355 seamless tube / C45 rod / AlSi10MnMg piston
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Seamless cold-drawn tube and external valve machining replace inner-tube draw and internal foot-valve assembly; alternatives: monotube vs twin-tube tubing, and HPDC vs machined external valve body. Removes the inner tube and base-valve sub-assembly, cutting two components per damper and simplifying gas-charging while adding an external solenoid bracket, net part-count reduction.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Monotube deletes inner tube and base valve, saving mass and cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Monotube is more sensitive to side loads and tube denting, risking seizure off-road versus protected twin-tube. Mitigated by guard tube and increased rod guide bearing length. High gas pressure raises seal friction, addressed with low-friction PTFE seals to preserve secondary ride. Thermal benefit validated on Alpine descent cycle at GVW; NVH and crash mounting unchanged; premium body control preserved under sustained load.

title: Composite air-spring piston replacing aluminium casting
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace die-cast aluminium air-spring rolling pistons with glass-fibre-reinforced polymer (PA66-GF) pistons, cutting unsprung mass and machining. Maintains rolling-lobe contour and burst strength while reducing corrosion and cost. Applicable across all powertrains as a like-for-like air-strut component swap on existing platforms.

ideaData.title: Composite air-spring piston replacing aluminium casting
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline rolling-lobe piston is die-cast AlSi10MnMg requiring contour machining and anti-corrosion coating. The change moulds the piston from PA66-GF50 or PPA-GF40 high-temperature polymer, with alternatives including PA6-GF35 for lower-load corners and SMC for the upper closure plate. Physics: the rolling lobe rolls over the piston contour to set effective area and progressive rate; an injection-moulded contour holds tolerance and reduces unsprung mass ~40% per piston, improving wheel control. Glass loading provides hoop strength against internal pressure; integrated ribs replace machined features.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Injection moulding replaces HPDC plus CNC contour machining and coating, removing a machining cell and a coating line; alternatives: PA66-GF injection vs SMC compression moulding for the closure, and over-moulded vs pressed-in inserts. Net-shape moulding cuts secondary operations, scrap and tool wear, and lowers piston mass, reducing corner BOM and finishing steps.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Net-shape polymer piston removes machining and coating.

ideaData.riskNotes: Polymer creep and thermal expansion risk contour drift affecting spring rate and lobe wear over life. Mitigated by glass loading, rib design and validation across -40 to +90°C with UV/ozone exposure of the bellows interface. Burst and fatigue validated to 3x working pressure and 1M cycles. Unsprung-mass reduction improves ride and grip; NVH from lobe roll-off tuned via contour; premium ride-height accuracy preserved.

title: Consolidated 8-port valve block for four-corner levelling
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace discrete corner solenoid valves and manifolds with one integrated 8-port valve block carrying all levelling, residual-pressure and exhaust valves in a single HPDC body. Reduces fittings, lines and leak paths while centralising diagnostics. Applies across powertrains carrying four-corner air suspension.

ideaData.title: Consolidated 8-port valve block for four-corner levelling
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline distributes solenoid valves near each corner with individual manifolds and long air lines. The change centralises eight cartridge solenoids, residual-pressure valves and a central exhaust into one valve block. Body from AlSi10MnMg HPDC vs machined 6082-T6 billet vs PA66-GF40 polymer manifold; seals FKM vs HNBR; cartridges with 1.4305 stainless poppets. Physics: centralised galleries reduce dead volume and fitting count, lowering leak probability and improving levelling precision; integrated pressure sensor enables closed-loop height control and per-corner diagnostics, cutting no-fault-found returns.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: HPDC valve-block body with drilled galleries replaces multiple machined manifolds and crimped line junctions; alternatives: HPDC vs billet machining for the body, and pressed-in vs threaded cartridges. Consolidates ~6 discrete valve assemblies into one block, cutting fittings ~50%, shortening air-line BOM and removing several leak-test interfaces, simplifying assembly and diagnostics.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Manifold consolidation removes fittings, lines and leak paths.

ideaData.riskNotes: Centralising valves lengthens corner air lines, risking slower individual-corner response and added line mass. Mitigated by larger-bore lines and predictive levelling. Single-block failure could disable all corners, so residual-pressure valves retain ride height on fault. Burst and fatigue validated to 3x pressure; NVH from valve clicks isolated; off-road kneel and load-levelling for heavy variants preserved; crash routing unaffected.

title: Tapered tube coil springs replacing two-stage air on base BEV
description: [BEV] On entry BEV trims, delete four-corner air suspension and fit progressive-rate coil springs with self-levelling rear dampers to manage battery and payload mass. Removes compressor, reservoir, struts and valve block entirely, saving major cost where air-suspension take-rate is low. Self-levelling rear preserves loaded ride height.

ideaData.title: Tapered tube coil springs replacing two-stage air on base BEV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline base BEV carries full air suspension to offset ~600kg battery mass and payload swing. The change fits progressive-rate coil springs from tapered-wire 54SiCr6 or 60SiCrV7, with hydraulic self-levelling (Nivomat-type) rear dampers maintaining loaded height. Alternatives: oil-tempered 55Cr3 wire for fronts and side-load-tuned coils to reduce strut friction; lower spring seat from 6082-T6 vs AM60B Mg. Physics: variable pitch/diameter gives a soft initial then firm rate to carry GVW without air; self-levelling dampers internally pump to restore ride height under sustained load, replacing air levelling for the common laden case.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Hot/cold coiling and tapered-wire rolling replace strut machining and bellows vulcanisation; alternatives: tapered vs constant-wire coiling, and shot-peened vs stress-relieved finishing. Deletes the entire air-supply BOM (compressor, dryer, reservoir, valve block, air struts), removing pneumatic fill/leak-test stations and many fittings, a large net part-count and assembly-time reduction.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Deleting air suspension on base BEV removes the entire pneumatic system.

ideaData.riskNotes: Losing air suspension removes ride-height adaptability, risking reduced ground clearance for kerbs/ramps and a less premium variable ride. Mitigated by self-levelling rear for laden stability and progressive coils tuned to battery mass. Spring fatigue and sag validated to 1M cycles at GVW with corrosion protection. NVH from coil clash addressed by progressive pitch; crash load paths unchanged; entry-trim ride positioned below air variants intentionally.

evidenceSources.title: ES8 coil vs air variant teardown

title: Hollow stabiliser bar via flow-forming for unsprung mass cut
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace solid forged anti-roll bars with flow-formed hollow tubular bars at equal roll stiffness, cutting mass and material content. Lowers unsprung and sprung weight, aiding efficiency across powertrains, with biggest payoff on heavy PHEV/BEV variants. Equal torsional rate preserves handling.

ideaData.title: Hollow stabiliser bar via flow-forming for unsprung mass cut
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline solid anti-roll bars are forged 51CrV4 or 38MnSiVS6 bar stock. The change flow-forms hollow tubes from 34CrMo4 or 26MnB5 with tuned wall thickness, matching torsional stiffness at ~35% lower mass. Alternatives: seamless DOM tube vs flow-formed vs tailor-wall tube; end forging from 38MnSiVS6 micro-alloyed steel. Physics: torsional stiffness scales with the polar second moment; removing low-stress core material near the neutral axis preserves rate while cutting mass, reducing unsprung and roll-bar inertia and improving transient roll response.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Flow-forming and tube bending replace solid-bar forging and full-section heat treat; alternatives: flow-formed vs DOM tube, and friction-welded vs forged ends. Hollow construction reduces raw material ~35%, lowers furnace energy in heat treat, and cuts component mass, with end-forming integrated to retain bar-link attachment without added parts.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Hollow section removes core material at equal stiffness.

ideaData.riskNotes: Hollow bars are more sensitive to wall-thickness variation and internal corrosion, risking fatigue cracking if uncontrolled. Mitigated by ultrasonic wall inspection, internal wax coating and shot peening. Torsional fatigue validated to durability targets at full roll loading; NVH bushing rates retuned for lighter bar. Off-road articulation and end-link loads validated; premium flat cornering preserved by stiffness-matched design.

title: Commonised ride-height sensor across model range
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Standardise on a single contactless Hall-effect ride-height sensor and linkage interface across all air-suspension models, replacing multiple bespoke rotary potentiometric sensors. Cuts unique part numbers, calibration variants and inventory. Universal mounting bracket adapts to each corner geometry.

ideaData.title: Commonised ride-height sensor across model range
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline uses several rotary potentiometric height sensors with model-specific bodies and connectors. The change adopts one contactless Hall/inductive sensor with a common housing and connector, adapting via a bracket and link arm per corner. Housing from PA66-GF35 vs PBT-GF30 vs PPS-GF40 for high-temp corners; link arm from 6082-T6 Al or glass-filled polymer. Physics: contactless angle sensing removes wiper wear and contamination drift, improving levelling repeatability; commonisation moves variation into low-cost brackets and software calibration rather than sensor hardware.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Single injection-mould tool for the sensor body replaces multiple bespoke tools; alternatives: PPS vs PA66 moulding, and over-moulded vs assembled connector. Bracket variation handled by low-cost stamped or moulded adaptors, cutting unique sensor SKUs from five to one, reducing tooling, inventory and EOL calibration variants.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Single sensor across range collapses unique part numbers.

ideaData.riskNotes: A common sensor must cover the widest angular range and harshest corner environment, risking over-specification for some applications. Mitigated by software-defined measurement window and shared calibration. Contactless tech improves drift and durability versus potentiometric. Levelling accuracy validated across temperature and EMC; off-road mud/water ingress sealed to IP6K9K; premium auto-levelling precision preserved across all variants.

title: Crimped-end air line replacing threaded fittings
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace threaded brass air-line fittings with pre-assembled crimped quick-connect lines for the air-suspension circuit. Eliminates torque-controlled assembly steps and leak-test rework while improving sealing consistency. Applies to all air-suspension SUVs as a circuit-wide process change.

ideaData.title: Crimped-end air line replacing threaded fittings
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline uses brass push-in or threaded fittings requiring torque control and prone to under/over-torque leaks. The change supplies pre-cut nylon PA12 lines with factory-crimped quick-connectors and integrated O-rings. Line material PA12 vs PA11 (bio-based) vs PA6/66 multilayer for permeation; connectors from PA66-GF or 1.4305 stainless vs brass CW614N; seals HNBR vs FKM. Physics: a controlled crimp delivers a repeatable radial seal independent of operator torque, lowering leak rate and permeation; quick-connects allow tool-free assembly and service.
ideaData.materialGrade: PA12 line / PA66-GF connector / HNBR seal
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Factory crimping and laser cut-to-length replace in-plant threaded assembly and torque verification; alternatives: crimped vs push-in connectors, and PA12 vs multilayer barrier tube. Pre-assembled lines remove a torque-control station and reduce leak-test rework, cutting fitting count and assembly time while standardising connector SKUs.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Factory-crimped lines remove in-plant torque assembly and rework.

ideaData.riskNotes: Pre-crimped lines reduce routing flexibility, risking fitment issues with build variation and harder field repair. Mitigated by service-loop design and validated routing fixtures. Crimp integrity validated by pull-off and burst to 3x working pressure across temperature and vibration. Permeation controlled for cold-climate pressure retention; NVH from line resonance clipped; levelling response and leak-down preserved for premium ride-height stability.

title: Tailor-tempered coil springs replacing dual-rate stacks
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] Replace dual-rate coil stacks (main plus helper spring with isolators) with a single progressive tailor-coiled spring delivering the same rising rate. Removes the helper spring, upper isolator and seat, cutting parts and assembly. Applies to ICE/MHEV/PHEV coil-sprung variants carrying variable payload.

ideaData.title: Tailor-tempered coil springs replacing dual-rate stacks
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline rear uses a main spring plus a helper coil and rubber isolator to add rate under load. The change replaces the stack with one variable-pitch, variable-diameter progressive coil from oil-tempered 54SiCr6 or 60SiCrV7 wire, with alternatives 55Cr3 and side-load-optimised geometry. Physics: closely spaced coils at the inactive end progressively go solid as load rises, increasing effective rate without a separate helper; tailored wire diameter and pitch shape the force-deflection curve to match payload swing, eliminating the helper spring, its seat and isolator.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Variable-pitch CNC coiling and tapered-wire rolling replace assembly of two springs plus isolator; alternatives: tapered-wire vs constant-wire coiling, and induction vs furnace stress relief. Removing the helper spring, upper seat and isolator cuts three parts per corner and an assembly step, reducing BOM and stack-up tolerance while shot peening sustains fatigue life.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Single progressive coil deletes the helper-spring stack.

ideaData.riskNotes: Progressive coils risk coil clash NVH and uneven stress at the transition coils under high load. Mitigated by pitch optimisation, ground ends and peening to manage residual stress. Fatigue and sag validated to 1M cycles at GVW with corrosion protection. Loaded ride height and rate matched to outgoing dual-rate behaviour; off-road droop travel preserved; premium laden ride comfort maintained.

title: Shared 48V e-ARB actuator front-and-rear commonisation
description: [MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Commonise the 48V e-ARB roll actuator module so the same motor-gearbox unit serves both front and rear bars, with torque scaling in software and bar arms tuned to axle. Cuts unique actuator part numbers and tooling. For MHEV/PHEV/BEV platforms with electromechanical roll control.

ideaData.title: Shared 48V e-ARB actuator front-and-rear commonisation
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline carries axle-specific e-ARB actuators with different motors and gear ratios front and rear. The change defines one actuator module (motor, strain-wave gearset, ECU) used at both axles, with roll stiffness set by bar-arm length and software torque limits. Housing AlSi10MnMg HPDC vs machined 7075-T6 vs AE44 Mg; flexspline 1.4057; bar arms forged 51CrV4. Physics: required roll torque differs by axle, but a single oversized-but-derated actuator with software scaling covers both, moving variation into low-cost bar arms and calibration rather than unique motors and gearboxes.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One HPDC housing and one strain-wave gear tool serve both axles; alternatives: HPDC vs machined housing, and forged vs cast bar arms. Commonising the actuator halves unique motor/gearbox SKUs and tooling, simplifies supplier logistics and spares, with axle differentiation handled by inexpensive forged arms and software, reducing assembly variant complexity.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One actuator both axles collapses unique motor/gearbox parts.

ideaData.riskNotes: A common actuator must meet the higher-torque axle, risking over-specification and added mass at the lighter axle. Mitigated by software derating and shared-volume cost benefit outweighing mass. Functional safety must fail-stiff to a passive bar at both axles. NVH gear whine controlled by strain-wave design; off-road full-decouple articulation validated; premium roll control and latency preserved equally front and rear.

title: Polymer composite leaf-style transverse spring for rear BEV
description: [BEV] Replace two rear coil springs and lower control arms' spring function with a single transverse GFRP composite leaf spring spanning the axle, providing springing and some anti-roll. Cuts mass and part count under heavy BEV rear axles. Frees packaging around the structural battery pack.

ideaData.title: Polymer composite leaf-style transverse spring for rear BEV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline rear uses two steel coil springs plus a steel anti-roll bar. The change fits a single transverse glass-fibre/epoxy (GFRP) composite leaf spring mounted to the subframe at two points and to each knuckle, providing wheel-rate springing and partial roll stiffness. Material E-glass/epoxy GFRP vs hybrid glass/carbon CFRP vs SMC for lower-cost variants; mounts AlSi10MnMg HPDC. Physics: the transverse leaf deflects symmetrically in bump (springing) and asymmetrically in roll (anti-roll), combining two functions; composite's high specific strength cuts mass ~50% versus steel coils plus bar and frees vertical packaging above the battery.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Filament winding or compression moulding of the composite leaf replaces coil coiling and bar forging; alternatives: GFRP compression moulding vs prepreg layup, and bonded vs bolted mounts. Combining springing and anti-roll deletes two coil springs and the anti-roll bar plus links, cutting several parts and assembly steps, though composite tooling and bond/inspection add process content.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Dual-function composite leaf deletes coils and anti-roll bar.

ideaData.riskNotes: Composite leaf couples springing and roll, constraining independent tuning, and risks delamination/fatigue at mount interfaces under heavy BEV loads. Mitigated by tailored ply layup, bonded-bushing interfaces and ultrasonic NDT. Creep and temperature stability validated -40 to +90°C; impact/stone-chip protection added for off-road. NVH transmissibility tuned via bushings; crash load paths re-validated; premium ride retained through rate calibration.

evidenceSources.title: Transverse composite leaf spring with roll function

title: Reservoir delete via larger-volume air struts
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Delete the dedicated steel air reservoir by enlarging strut chamber volume and relying on the compressor for fast fills, using a closed-loop air system that recycles air between corners and an in-line buffer. Removes a heavy tank, bracket and lines. Closed-loop recovery sustains levelling speed.

ideaData.title: Reservoir delete via larger-volume air struts
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline carries a ~5L pressurised steel reservoir feeding rapid lifts. The change deletes the standalone reservoir, enlarges strut working volume and adopts a closed-loop air management system that transfers air between corners and a small in-line buffer, with the compressor topping up. Buffer from blow-moulded HDPE with 6061-T6 caps vs aluminium 6082-T6 vs PA66-GF; strut body 6082-T6. Physics: closed-loop air recovery reuses energy stored at high corners and reduces atmospheric venting, sustaining levelling rate without a large dead-weight tank; larger strut volume lowers spring rate slightly, retuned via valve.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Closed-loop air-management valve block (HPDC) and small buffer replace the deep-drawn welded steel reservoir and its bracket; alternatives: HPDC vs billet valve block, and blow-moulded vs extruded buffer. Deleting the reservoir removes welds, a bracket, mounting hardware and several fittings, cutting mass and a leak-test interface while adding closed-loop valve content.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Closed-loop recovery removes the dead-weight reservoir.

ideaData.riskNotes: Without a reservoir, simultaneous four-corner fast lift relies more on the compressor, risking slower kneel and higher compressor duty/heat. Mitigated by closed-loop air reuse and thermal-protected compressor. Larger strut volume must hold ride height under leak-down; validated for levelling speed and durability. Off-road kneel speed validated; NVH compressor cycling managed; load-levelling for heavy variants preserved via closed-loop transfer.

title: HPDC aluminium top mount replacing steel-rubber stack
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace stamped-steel strut top mounts and separate bearing plates with an integrated HPDC aluminium top mount carrying the bearing, isolator and sensor boss in one casting. Cuts parts, mass and corrosion protection. Applies to coil and air struts across powertrains.

ideaData.title: HPDC aluminium top mount replacing steel-rubber stack
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline strut top mount is a stamped-steel cup with separate thrust bearing, rubber isolator and bracketry. The change integrates the cup, bearing seat, isolator cavity and sensor/line boss into one HPDC AlSi10MnMg casting with an over-moulded elastomer isolator. Alternatives: AM60B or AE44 magnesium for further mass cut, 6082-T6 forged for highest-load corners. Physics: aluminium casting carries vertical strut load and locates the bearing while the bonded elastomer tunes NVH path stiffness; integration removes stack-up between steel parts, improving alignment and reducing corrosion-prone steel interfaces.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: HPDC with over-moulded isolator replaces multi-part steel stamping, welding and assembly; alternatives: HPDC vs forged aluminium, and bonded vs press-fit isolator. Integration consolidates ~4 steel parts into one casting, deleting welds and corrosion coating, reducing mass and assembly stack-up while the bearing seat is cast net-shape, cutting machining.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Casting integration deletes steel parts, welds and coating.

ideaData.riskNotes: Aluminium top mounts risk galvanic corrosion at steel fasteners and lower fatigue margin if porosity is uncontrolled. Mitigated by sealed fastener interfaces, vacuum-assisted HPDC and X-ray porosity checks. Isolator bonding validated for NVH path durability and pull-off. Vertical and lateral strut loads validated to crash and pothole cases; off-road impact durability confirmed; premium NVH isolation preserved via tuned elastomer.

title: Software-defined damping deletes mechanical mode selector
description: [MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Remove the hardware drive-mode rotary and dedicated suspension-mode wiring, controlling CDC damping and air-spring stiffness modes purely through the central drive-mode software and touchscreen. Cuts a switch, harness and trim part. For connected MHEV/PHEV/BEV vehicles with central HMI.

ideaData.title: Software-defined damping deletes mechanical mode selector
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline has a physical suspension-mode rotary/switch with dedicated wiring to the chassis ECU. The change removes the hardware control, exposing comfort/sport/off-road damping and ride-height modes through the central drive-mode controller and touchscreen, with the chassis ECU reading mode over the vehicle bus. No new material grade is added at the corner; reuses existing CDC valves and air struts. The console blank panel uses PC-ABS vs PP-T20 vs PA6-GF; harness copper deleted. Physics: damping and stiffness selection is already an ECU calibration set; the physical switch is redundant signalling that the bus already carries.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes a switch sub-assembly and a harness branch; alternatives: injection-moulded blank vs shared trim panel, and bus signalling vs discrete wiring. Removing the rotary and its connector cuts an electronic part, console machining for the aperture and an assembly/test step, with mode logic moved entirely to software, simplifying variant wiring.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Software mode selection removes the hardware switch and wiring.

ideaData.riskNotes: Removing the physical control risks slower mode access and driver-distraction concerns when changing damping on the move. Mitigated by steering-wheel shortcut and voice control, preserving glance-free operation. No structural, crash or durability impact as it is a control-path change. Off-road mode remains accessible via dedicated tile; premium configurability preserved while NVH and ride tunes are unchanged in hardware.

title: Rolling-lobe bellows gauge optimisation via FEA
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Optimise air-spring bellows wall and cord construction via FEA, reducing rubber and reinforcement plies where stress is low while retaining burst and fatigue margins. Cuts material per bellows across the air-suspension range. Tailored ply layup matches local stress.

ideaData.title: Rolling-lobe bellows gauge optimisation via FEA
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline bellows use a uniform two-ply construction with conservative rubber gauge. The change uses FEA-driven zonal gauge and cord-angle optimisation, thinning the rubber cover and matching ply count to the local hoop/bending stress over the rolling lobe. Elastomer options: EPDM vs natural-rubber blend vs HNBR for heat; reinforcement aramid vs polyamide vs polyester cord. Physics: bellows stress concentrates at the rolling fold; tailoring cord angle near the neutral wind angle and reducing cover thickness where pressure load is low cuts material while sustaining burst pressure and flex fatigue at the lobe.
ideaData.materialGrade: EPDM cover / aramid cord / HNBR (heat zones)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Zonal calendering and tailored ply winding replace uniform-gauge build; alternatives: aramid vs polyester cord, and continuous vs spliced ply. Reducing rubber mass and ply content cuts material per bellows and curing energy, with vulcanisation tooling reused, lowering BOM cost without adding parts; tighter process control needed on ply placement.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: FEA-tailored gauge removes over-specified rubber and cord.

ideaData.riskNotes: Thinning the bellows risks reduced abrasion resistance and earlier ozone/UV cracking, plus fatigue at the rolling fold. Mitigated by protective sleeve, antiozonant compounding and validation to 1M flex cycles and burst at 3x pressure. Cold-climate flexibility validated to -40°C. Levelling accuracy and leak-down preserved; off-road stone/debris protection retained; premium ride-height stability and durability maintained.

title: Dual-rate CDC valve commonised across damper sizes
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Standardise a single proportional CDC solenoid valve across all damper bore sizes, with damping characteristics set by disc stacks and software, not bespoke valves. Cuts unique valve part numbers, tooling and inventory across the adaptive-damper portfolio. Applies to all CDC-equipped variants.

ideaData.title: Dual-rate CDC valve commonised across damper sizes
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline uses bore-specific CDC solenoid valves across front/rear and model variants. The change adopts one common proportional solenoid valve body with flow tuning via interchangeable disc stacks and software current maps. Valve body from machined 11SMnPb30 free-cutting steel or AlSi10MnMg HPDC; poppet 1.4305 stainless; disc stack 51CrV4. Physics: continuously variable damping depends on solenoid-controlled orifice area; a single valve sized for the largest flow can be derated by current map and disc selection for smaller dampers, moving variation into low-cost discs rather than unique solenoids.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One solenoid machining/winding line and tool serve all bores; alternatives: HPDC vs machined valve body, and stamped vs fine-blanked discs. Commonising the valve cuts unique solenoid SKUs across the portfolio, reducing tooling, coil-winding variants and inventory, with damper differentiation handled by inexpensive disc stacks and calibration, simplifying supply and EOL setup.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Single CDC valve across bores collapses unique solenoid parts.

ideaData.riskNotes: A valve sized for the largest bore may be slightly over-specified for small dampers, marginally affecting low-speed control resolution. Mitigated by current-map tailoring and disc selection. Common solenoid hysteresis and response validated across flow ranges. NVH valve noise controlled; durability to thermal and contamination validated; sport body-control and comfort isolation preserved through software tuning across all variants.

title: Integrated air-strut damper-piston module for assembly cut
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Combine the air-spring module and CDC damper into one pre-assembled, pre-charged strut delivered as a single serviceable unit, replacing in-plant stacking of bellows, damper and mount. Cuts assembly time and corner stations. Plug-and-play corner reduces line complexity.

ideaData.title: Integrated air-strut damper-piston module for assembly cut
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline assembles bellows, piston, CDC damper, top mount and sensor at the vehicle line. The change supplies a Tier-1 pre-assembled, pre-charged air strut with integrated CDC valve, bearing and harness pigtail as one serviceable corner. Damper tube E355 steel; air-spring piston PA66-GF50; top mount AlSi10MnMg HPDC; alternatives AM60B Mg mount, 6082-T6 spring seat. Physics: factory pre-charge and integrated bearing remove vehicle-line gas filling and stack-up alignment; a single quick-connect air and electrical interface lets the corner install in one operation, shifting complexity to a controlled supplier cell.
ideaData.materialGrade: E355 tube / PA66-GF50 piston / AlSi10MnMg mount
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Supplier sub-assembly and pre-charge replace in-plant stacking, gas filling and alignment; alternatives: pre-charged module vs line-fill, and quick-connect vs threaded interfaces. Module delivery removes multiple corner stations and a gas-charge process from the OEM line, cutting assembly time and tolerance stack-up, with one electrical/air interface per corner reducing fitting count.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Pre-assembled corner removes line stacking and gas-fill stations.

ideaData.riskNotes: Pre-charged modules risk transit pressure loss and reduced in-plant inspection visibility, plus higher single-part replacement cost in service. Mitigated by sealed transport caps, incoming leak checks and service-exchange pricing. Module durability and pre-charge retention validated over shelf life and vibration. Off-road and crash mounting unchanged; NVH from integrated bearing tuned; premium ride and levelling preserved by controlled supplier assembly.

title: Steel-to-aluminium spring seat for unsprung mass
description: [MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace stamped-steel lower spring seats and isolators with HPDC aluminium seats integrating the isolator pocket, cutting unsprung mass for ride and efficiency on heavy electrified SUVs. Applies to coil-sprung corners across MHEV/PHEV/BEV variants. Corrosion-free aluminium removes coating.

ideaData.title: Steel-to-aluminium spring seat for unsprung mass
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline lower spring seats are stamped steel with separate rubber isolators and corrosion coating. The change casts the seat in AlSi10MnMg HPDC with an integrated isolator pocket and over-moulded elastomer pad. Alternatives: AM60B or AE44 magnesium for the lowest mass, 6082-T6 forged for high-load corners. Physics: the seat reacts spring preload into the control arm; aluminium's lower density cuts unsprung mass ~40% at this part, improving wheel control and reducing road-induced harshness, while the cast pocket locates the isolator precisely to control spring seating noise.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: HPDC with cast isolator pocket replaces steel stamping plus coating and separate isolator retention; alternatives: HPDC vs forged seat, and over-moulded vs clipped isolator. Casting removes the corrosion-coating line and a retention feature, cuts unsprung mass and consolidates the isolator interface, with net-shape pocket reducing machining and assembly steps.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Aluminium seat cuts unsprung mass and deletes coating.

ideaData.riskNotes: Aluminium seats risk galvanic corrosion against steel springs and fatigue if porosity is uncontrolled. Mitigated by isolating pad, sacrificial coating at contact and vacuum HPDC with X-ray checks. Spring-seat fatigue validated to durability targets at GVW and pothole loads. Off-road impact and debris resistance validated; NVH spring-seat noise tuned via isolator; premium ride benefit from reduced unsprung mass realised.

title: Closed-loop air dryer regeneration extends desiccant life
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Optimise air-dryer regeneration through closed-loop exhaust routing so the desiccant cartridge is purged on every vent, extending life and downsizing the cartridge. Reduces desiccant volume and warranty moisture failures across air-suspension SUVs. Smaller, longer-life dryer cuts cost.

ideaData.title: Closed-loop air dryer regeneration extends desiccant life
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline dryers regenerate only partially during venting, oversizing the desiccant to guarantee life. The change routes exhaust air back through the desiccant on every corner-lower event, fully regenerating the cartridge each cycle and allowing a smaller bed. Cartridge housing PA66-GF40 vs PPS-GF40 vs AlSi10MnMg; desiccant molecular-sieve vs silica-gel vs activated-alumina. Physics: dry exhaust air strips adsorbed moisture from the desiccant during depressurisation; ensuring full purge flow maintains adsorption capacity, so a smaller bed sustains dew-point control over life, preventing valve freeze and corrosion.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Revised valve-block porting for closed-loop purge replaces an oversized cartridge; alternatives: molecular sieve vs silica gel, and moulded vs machined housing. Downsizing the desiccant bed cuts material and housing size, with regeneration handled by porting rather than added hardware, reducing cartridge cost and warranty moisture-related returns without new parts.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Full-purge regeneration allows a smaller, longer-life desiccant.

ideaData.riskNotes: Under-purged regeneration in low-use vehicles risks moisture breakthrough, valve freeze and corrosion. Mitigated by ECU-managed purge cycles and dew-point monitoring. Desiccant attrition and dusting controlled by retention screens. Cold-climate dryness validated to -40°C; levelling reliability preserved. No structural or crash impact; off-road water-fording valve protection retained; premium ride-height availability sustained through reliable moisture control.

title: Magnetorheological delete for cost-led adaptive damping
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace magnetorheological (MR) dampers with conventional solenoid CDC dampers delivering comparable adaptive range without MR fluid and specialised coils. Removes costly MR fluid and magnetic-circuit content while keeping continuously variable damping. Suited to volume trims where MR premium isn't perceived.

ideaData.title: Magnetorheological delete for cost-led adaptive damping
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline MR dampers use iron-particle MR fluid and an electromagnetic piston coil to vary apparent viscosity, an expensive fluid and magnetic circuit. The change fits a conventional CDC solenoid valve damper achieving continuously variable damping by orifice control with standard hydraulic oil. Tube E355 steel; valve body AlSi10MnMg HPDC vs machined 11SMnPb30; discs 51CrV4; seals FKM. Physics: CDC varies orifice area electronically to set force, covering most of the MR comfort-to-sport range at much lower cost, trading nanosecond MR response for millisecond solenoid response acceptable on touring SUVs.
ideaData.materialGrade: E355 tube / AlSi10MnMg HPDC valve / 51CrV4 discs
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Standard damper machining and solenoid assembly replace MR fluid handling and magnetic-piston winding; alternatives: HPDC vs machined valve, and standard mineral oil vs MR fluid. Removing MR fluid and the magnetic circuit deletes specialised fill and coil processes, cutting high-cost material content and a hazardous-fluid station while retaining a conventional damper BOM.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: CDC replaces costly MR fluid and magnetic-circuit content.

ideaData.riskNotes: CDC's slower response than MR risks reduced body-control crispness over sharp inputs valued by enthusiasts. Mitigated by predictive road-preview control and fast solenoids closing most of the gap. Durability improves without MR fluid settling/abrasion. NVH valve noise controlled; off-road and towing damping range validated; premium ride breadth preserved on volume trims while reserving MR for flagship variants.

title: Self-levelling rear damper deletes air on PHEV tow trims
description: [PHEV] On PHEV towing trims, replace rear air struts with hydromechanical self-levelling dampers (Nivomat-type) plus coil springs to handle tongue and battery load, retaining laden ride height without the rear air circuit. Cuts rear compressor demand and air content. Self-pumping levels under load.

ideaData.title: Self-levelling rear damper deletes air on PHEV tow trims
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline rear uses air struts to level under heavy PHEV battery plus towing tongue loads. The change fits coil springs with hydromechanical self-levelling (Nivomat) dampers that pump internally from road motion to restore ride height under sustained load, deleting the rear air struts and rear-circuit valves. Damper tube E355; coil 54SiCr6 tapered; reservoir AlSi10MnMg vs 6061-T6 vs PA66-GF. Physics: vertical oscillation drives an internal pump that pressurises a gas chamber, raising the laden rear to design height within a few hundred metres, providing automatic load-levelling without compressor energy or air-line content.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Self-levelling damper assembly and coil coiling replace rear air-strut and bellows vulcanisation; alternatives: Nivomat self-levelling vs air strut, and HPDC vs machined reservoir. Deleting rear air struts and rear valve-circuit content removes bellows, pneumatic fittings and a leak-test interface, reducing rear-corner air BOM while adding a self-contained hydromechanical damper.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Self-levelling damper removes the rear air circuit on tow trims.

ideaData.riskNotes: Self-levelling dampers need vehicle motion to pump up, risking delayed levelling when stationary-loaded then driven, and offer no on-demand ride-height raise. Mitigated by quick pump-up calibration and front air retention for clearance. Towing stability and laden height validated to GVW and max tongue load. NVH from pumping controlled; off-road droop preserved; premium laden ride height delivered automatically.
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN/ECE R55 towing / R13H loaded stability

title: 48V e-ARB recovers energy to cut MHEV duty cycle
description: [MHEV] On 48V MHEV SUVs, run the e-ARB actuators in regenerative decouple on straight roads, recovering energy and reducing parasitic load, allowing a smaller 48V DC-DC and downsized actuator cooling. Cuts power-electronics and cooling content. Energy recovery downsizes supporting hardware.

ideaData.title: 48V e-ARB recovers energy to cut MHEV duty cycle
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: MHEV. Baseline e-ARB actuators draw continuous 48V current and need cooling sized for sustained load. The change calibrates the actuators to decouple and back-drive (regenerate) on straight, low-roll roads, returning energy to the 48V net and lowering average current, permitting a smaller DC-DC allocation and reduced actuator cooling. Actuator housing AlSi10MnMg HPDC vs 7075-T6 vs AE44 Mg; windings copper vs aluminium hairpin. Physics: roll actuators only need torque during cornering; in steady state they can free-wheel and recover suspension energy, cutting thermal duty so cooling and power-electronics headroom can be downsized.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC / 7075-T6 Al / Cu hairpin winding
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Software-led duty reduction lets the cooling circuit and DC-DC be downsized rather than re-engineered; alternatives: copper vs aluminium windings, and passive vs active actuator cooling. Smaller cooling and power-electronics content reduces BOM and assembly plumbing, with the change largely in calibration plus a downsized heat-exchanger, lowering thermal hardware cost.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Energy recovery downsizes actuator cooling and power electronics.

ideaData.riskNotes: Aggressive decouple risks a brief response lag re-engaging roll control at corner entry, affecting body-control confidence. Mitigated by predictive engagement using steering and navigation preview. Downsized cooling must still cover sustained mountain-pass cornering, validated to thermal worst case. Functional safety fail-stiff retained; NVH re-engagement smoothed; premium flat cornering preserved while parasitic load drops.

title: Monoblock HPDC damper mount integrates knuckle interface
description: [MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Integrate the lower damper clevis and knuckle bracket into one HPDC aluminium monoblock, replacing a bolted steel clevis plus bracket. Cuts fasteners, mass and machining at the corner. For electrified SUVs prioritising unsprung-mass reduction. Net-shape casting removes assembly.

ideaData.title: Monoblock HPDC damper mount integrates knuckle interface
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline uses a stamped/forged steel damper clevis bolted to a separate knuckle bracket. The change casts a single AlSi10MnMg monoblock combining the damper clevis, knuckle interface and brake-hose/sensor bosses. Alternatives: forged 6082-T6 for highest loads, AE44 magnesium for mass, or squeeze-cast aluminium for fatigue-critical zones. Physics: the lower damper load reacts through the clevis into the knuckle; casting both as one net-shape part removes the bolted joint, its fasteners and stack-up, lowering unsprung mass and improving stiffness/alignment at the wheel for better damper control.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: HPDC monoblock replaces steel clevis plus bracket assembly and bolting; alternatives: HPDC vs squeeze casting vs forging, and net-shape vs machined bosses. Integration deletes two-to-three fasteners and a bolted interface, cuts unsprung mass and corrosion coating, and reduces machining via cast bosses, lowering corner part count and assembly time.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Monoblock casting deletes the bolted clevis-bracket joint.

ideaData.riskNotes: Integrating the clevis and knuckle interface raises fatigue and crash-load demands on a single casting; porosity could cause cracking. Mitigated by squeeze casting in critical zones, X-ray inspection and generous fillets. Lateral, vertical and braking loads validated to durability and crash cases. Galvanic isolation at steel fasteners; off-road impact resistance validated; premium damper control improved by stiffer interface.

title: Skateboard-shared corner module across BEV SUV variants
description: [BEV] Commonise a complete suspension corner module (control arms, damper, spring/air strut, knuckle) across short- and long-wheelbase BEV SUV variants on the skateboard platform, with track/height set by subframe shims. Maximises volume and tooling reuse. One corner spans the model family.

ideaData.title: Skateboard-shared corner module across BEV SUV variants
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline differentiates corner hardware across wheelbase and trim variants on the skateboard. The change defines one common corner module (upper/lower arms, knuckle, damper, air or coil strut) shared across the family, with geometry adjusted by subframe mounting shims and software ride-height/damping. Arms forged 6082-T6 or AlSi7Mg0.3 cast; knuckle AlSi10MnMg HPDC vs 7075-T6 forged; bushings tuned per variant. Physics: suspension hardpoints can be held common while track and ride height shift via subframe interface, so kinematics differences are absorbed by inexpensive shims and bushings rather than unique arms and knuckles.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Shared forging and casting tools across variants replace variant-specific corner tooling; alternatives: forged vs cast arms, and HPDC vs forged knuckle. Commonising the corner module reuses high-cost tooling across the family, slashing unique part numbers and validation, with differentiation via low-cost shims, bushings and software, cutting tooling and inventory dramatically.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One corner module across variants maximises tooling reuse.

ideaData.riskNotes: A common corner must satisfy the heaviest, longest variant, risking over-design and mass on lighter ones, and constrains per-variant kinematic tuning. Mitigated by shim/bushing tuning and shared-volume cost outweighing mass penalty. Loads validated to worst-case GVW and crash for all variants. Off-road geometry and on-road comfort tuned via bushings and software; premium ride differentiation preserved without unique hardware.

title: Roll-formed C-section subframe replacing HPDC casting
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] Replace a large HPDC aluminium front subframe with a roll-formed and MIG-welded high-strength steel subframe at lower tooling cost for mid-volume programmes, accepting marginal mass for major piece-price and investment savings. Carries suspension and anti-roll mounts. Lower investment suits mid volumes.

ideaData.title: Roll-formed C-section subframe replacing HPDC casting
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline front subframe is a large HPDC AlSi10MnMg casting with high die cost. The change fabricates a roll-formed and MIG-welded subframe from HSLA S420MC or DP780, with reinforced nodes from 22MnB5 hot-stamped 1500MPa boron at suspension hardpoints. Alternatives: DP980 for crash rails, hydroformed tube for the cross-member, 6082-T6 brackets. Physics: closed C-sections and tubes give high bending/torsional stiffness for suspension and anti-roll mounts; steel's lower tooling investment suits mid volumes despite a mass penalty offset by selective AHSS gauge reduction.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Roll forming, hydroforming and MIG welding replace large-tonnage HPDC; alternatives: roll-formed vs hydroformed members, and hot-stamped vs cold-formed nodes. Steel fabrication slashes die investment versus a giga-casting-scale die, trading added weld/part count for far lower tooling, with tailor-welded blanks placing AHSS only where needed, improving cost at mid volume.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Steel fabrication cuts subframe tooling investment for mid volume.

ideaData.riskNotes: Welded steel subframes risk added mass, weld-fatigue sites and corrosion versus a monolithic casting, affecting unsprung/sprung mass and NVH path stiffness. Mitigated by hot-stamped nodes, robotic weld control, e-coat and tuned bushings. Suspension and anti-roll mount stiffness validated for handling and crash load paths. Off-road durability and skid loads validated; premium NVH isolation preserved via bushing tuning.

evidenceSources.title: Steel vs cast subframe cost study

title: Air-spring top cap consolidates sensor and line ports
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Integrate the air line port, pressure sensor boss and height-sensor link mount into the air-spring top cap as one moulded part, replacing separate fittings and brackets. Cuts parts and leak interfaces at the strut top. Applies across air-suspension SUVs.

ideaData.title: Air-spring top cap consolidates sensor and line ports
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline strut top cap uses a separate threaded air port, a bolted pressure-sensor adaptor and a riveted height-sensor link bracket. The change moulds these into one PA66-GF40 cap with an integrated quick-connect air port, sensor cavity and link-arm boss. Alternatives: PPA-GF40 for heat, PPS-GF40 for chemical resistance, or AlSi10MnMg for metal interface needs. Physics: consolidating ports into one moulded cap removes threaded joints, each a potential leak path, and locates the sensor and link precisely, improving levelling accuracy while reducing assembly operations at the strut top.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Single injection-moulded cap with integrated ports replaces multiple fittings, adaptors and brackets; alternatives: PA66-GF vs PPA-GF moulding, and over-moulded insert vs threaded boss. Integration deletes two-to-three fasteners and threaded joints, removing leak interfaces and an assembly step, with the quick-connect port standardising the air interface and cutting strut-top part count.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Moulded cap consolidates ports, sensor and link mount.

ideaData.riskNotes: Concentrating ports in one polymer cap risks creep-induced leakage at the air interface and sensor-cavity sealing over life and temperature. Mitigated by glass loading, metal threaded inserts and validation across -40 to +90°C with burst to 3x pressure. Leak-down and levelling accuracy validated. No crash or off-road structural change; NVH unaffected; premium ride-height stability preserved through reliable sealing.

title: Bidirectional structural-pack mounts share damper tower load
description: [BEV] On structural-battery BEV SUVs, route rear damper-tower loads partly into the structural pack case, allowing a lighter, smaller rear shock tower casting. Shares load between body and pack to cut chassis mass and material. Structural pack reduces tower content.

ideaData.title: Bidirectional structural-pack mounts share damper tower load
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline rear shock towers carry full damper and spring loads into the body, sizing a large HPDC casting. The change ties the tower to the structural battery case via shear-capable mounts so the pack reacts part of the suspension load, permitting a lighter, smaller tower casting. Tower AlSi10MnMg HPDC vs AE44 Mg vs 6082-T6 forged node; pack case AlSi10MnMg with steel DP980 reinforcements; mounts forged 42CrMo4. Physics: sharing vertical and lateral damper loads between body and the stiff structural pack reduces peak stress in the tower, allowing thinner walls and lower mass while maintaining stiffness for damper control.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Smaller HPDC tower plus shear mounts replace a large standalone casting; alternatives: HPDC vs forged tower node, and bolted vs structurally bonded pack interface. Sharing load downsizes the tower casting, cutting aluminium content and mass, while standardised shear mounts add modest content; net material and weight reduction with revised crash-load routing through the pack.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Load-sharing with the structural pack downsizes the shock tower.

ideaData.riskNotes: Routing suspension loads into the battery case risks pack-mount fatigue, NVH transmission into the cabin and crash-load interaction with cells. Mitigated by isolating shear mounts, cell-standoff zones and validated crash load paths keeping cells outside crush. Durability validated to GVW and pothole at the pack interface. Off-road impact isolation maintained; premium NVH preserved via tuned mounts; damper control stiffness retained.

evidenceSources.title: Suspension load sharing with battery structure

title: Anti-roll bar link commonisation with ball-stud reuse
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Standardise anti-roll bar drop links on a common ball-stud and housing across front and rear and across models, with length set by a common-end tube cut to size. Cuts unique link part numbers and tooling. Applies across all stabiliser-bar SUVs.

ideaData.title: Anti-roll bar link commonisation with ball-stud reuse
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline uses model- and axle-specific drop links with different ball studs, housings and lengths. The change standardises one ball-stud, socket and housing design, varying only a swaged steel tube cut to length per application. Ball stud forged 41Cr4 or 42CrMo4; housing PA66-GF50 vs zinc die-cast vs forged steel; bearing POM/PTFE liner. Physics: drop links transmit axial roll-bar loads; the ball joint and housing are interchangeable while only the connecting tube length changes, so commonisation moves all variation into a cheap cut-to-length tube rather than unique forged studs and housings.
ideaData.materialGrade: 42CrMo4 ball stud / PA66-GF50 housing / POM liner
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One ball-stud forging die and one housing mould serve all applications; alternatives: forged vs cast housing, and swaged vs welded tube ends. Commonising studs and housings cuts unique link SKUs across the range, reducing tooling and inventory, with length variation by a low-cost tube cut, simplifying supplier logistics and assembly part presentation.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Common ball-stud and housing collapse unique link parts.

ideaData.riskNotes: A common ball-stud must meet the highest axial load of any application, risking slight over-specification on lighter axles. Mitigated by shared-volume savings exceeding mass penalty. Ball-joint durability, articulation and pull-out validated to worst-case roll loads. NVH lash controlled by preloaded liner; off-road articulation and corrosion sealing validated; premium roll response and link durability preserved across applications.

title: Predictive air-levelling cuts compressor and valve cycling
description: [MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Use navigation and camera road-preview to pre-position ride height and minimise reactive compressor/valve cycling, allowing a downsized compressor and longer-life valves. Cuts air-supply duty and warranty wear across connected air-suspension SUVs. Preview reduces hardware duty.

ideaData.title: Predictive air-levelling cuts compressor and valve cycling
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline air systems react to load and road events, cycling the compressor and solenoids frequently and sizing hardware for that duty. The change fuses navigation, GPS and forward camera/lidar preview to pre-set ride height ahead of speed humps, ramps and load changes, smoothing demand and cutting reactive cycling. This permits a downsized single-stage compressor and lower-duty solenoids. Compressor housing AlSi10MnMg vs PA66-GF; valve poppets 1.4305; seals FKM. Physics: anticipating height changes reduces peak and total actuation, lowering compressor thermal duty and solenoid cycle count, enabling smaller, longer-life hardware.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Software preview lets the compressor and valves be downsized rather than redesigned; alternatives: single-stage vs twin-piston compressor, and standard vs high-cycle solenoid. Reduced duty allows smaller compressor and lower-rated valves, cutting hardware cost and warranty wear, with the change mainly in calibration plus a smaller-displacement pump, lowering air-supply BOM.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Predictive levelling reduces actuation duty, downsizing hardware.

ideaData.riskNotes: Preview-based pre-positioning risks errors from inaccurate maps or sensor occlusion, causing mistimed height changes affecting comfort or clearance. Mitigated by reactive fallback control and conservative confidence thresholds. Downsized compressor must still cover worst-case loaded lift, validated to GVW kneel time. Off-road manual height override retained; NVH from cycling reduced; premium adaptive ride preserved with lower hardware duty.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Li Auto L9 (2022) air suspension programme

evidenceSources.title: Predictive air suspension with road preview

title: Eddy-current passive damping supplement for BEV secondary ride
description: [BEV] Add a compact passive eddy-current damping element at the strut to supplement hydraulic damping for high-frequency battery-mass control, allowing a simpler base hydraulic damper without adaptive valves. Trades adaptive hardware for a maintenance-free magnetic element. For heavy BEV secondary-ride refinement.

ideaData.title: Eddy-current passive damping supplement for BEV secondary ride
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline relies on adaptive CDC valves to control high-frequency secondary ride under heavy battery sprung mass. The change adds a compact passive eddy-current damper element, a conductor moving through a permanent-magnet field, supplementing the hydraulic damper at high frequencies, permitting a simpler non-adaptive base damper. Conductor copper C11000 vs aluminium 6101-T6; magnets NdFeB N42SH vs ferrite; yoke from electrical steel M250-35A. Physics: relative motion induces eddy currents whose Lorentz force opposes velocity, giving frequency-dependent damping that rises with speed, complementing hydraulic damping at high frequency without valves, fluid wear or electronics.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Magnet assembly and conductor machining add a passive element while deleting adaptive valve/solenoid content on the base damper; alternatives: NdFeB vs ferrite magnets, and copper vs aluminium conductor. The eddy-current module is maintenance-free with no fluid; net change removes adaptive electronics from base trims at the cost of magnet content, simplifying the corner.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Passive eddy-current damping supplements ride without adaptive valves.

ideaData.riskNotes: Eddy-current damping is velocity-proportional and cannot be switched off, risking firmness at low speed and added unsprung mass from magnets. Mitigated by tuning the magnetic gap and pairing with a soft base hydraulic curve. Thermal demagnetisation of NdFeB validated with SH-grade magnets to high temperature. Off-road durability and debris sealing validated; NVH eddy-element silent; premium secondary ride under battery mass improved passively.

title: Hot-stamped boron anti-roll bar mounts for crash-load reuse
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace thick mild-steel anti-roll bar and subframe brackets with thin hot-stamped boron (22MnB5) brackets at higher strength, cutting gauge and mass while maintaining stiffness and crash-load capability. Applies to stabiliser and suspension mounting brackets across powertrains. Gauge-down at equal strength.

ideaData.title: Hot-stamped boron anti-roll bar mounts for crash-load reuse
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline stabiliser and suspension brackets are thick mild or HSLA steel stampings. The change hot-stamps brackets from 22MnB5 1500MPa boron, allowing a thinner gauge at equal or higher strength and crash-load capacity. Alternatives: DP980 cold-formed for less safety-critical brackets, DP780 for medium load, or 7075-T6 aluminium where mass dominates over crash duty. Physics: the very high yield strength of press-hardened boron lets section thickness drop while preserving bending and buckling resistance for anti-roll mount loads and crash-load transfer, cutting steel mass per bracket.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Hot stamping (press hardening) replaces thick cold stamping; alternatives: hot-stamped boron vs DP980 cold forming, and tailor-welded blank vs uniform gauge. Gauge reduction cuts steel mass and coil consumption, with press-hardening adding furnace and die-cooling content; tailor-welded blanks place boron only at high-load nodes, optimising cost and weight while retaining mounting-point stiffness.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Press-hardened boron enables gauge-down at equal strength.

ideaData.riskNotes: Thin boron brackets risk reduced fatigue margin at welds and hydrogen-embrittlement/corrosion sensitivity if coatings fail. Mitigated by AlSi-coated boron, controlled welding and e-coat. Anti-roll mount stiffness and crash-load transfer validated to durability and frontal/side cases. NVH path stiffness preserved via section design; off-road impact and skid loads validated; premium handling precision retained through stiff, light mounts.

title: HPDC AlSi10MnMg front lower control arm replacing forged steel
description: [BEV] Convert front lower control arm from forged S355 steel to high-pressure die-cast AlSi10MnMg for skateboard BEV SUV. Reduces unsprung mass ~2.1kg/corner improving ride and energy efficiency, deletes phosphate/e-coat steps and a sub-bracket weld. Net cost down via part integration despite higher Al price, validated against Volvo EX90 front axle architecture.

ideaData.title: HPDC AlSi10MnMg front lower control arm replacing forged steel
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline is a forged S355/42CrMo4 steel L-arm with welded ball-joint bracket, e-coated, ~5.8kg. The change adopts vacuum-assisted HPDC AlSi10MnMg (T7) integrating the ball-joint housing and two bush eyes as one casting at ~3.7kg. Physics: lower unsprung mass cuts wheel-hop and improves contact-patch control, while cast Al stiffness is recovered through optimised rib topology and section modulus. Alternatives evaluated: forged 6082-T6 wrought Al (higher cost, better fatigue), AM60B Mg HPDC (corrosion/cost risk), and 7075-T6 machined billet (cost-prohibitive). AlSi10MnMg chosen for castability, weldability to nodes and ductility (>10% elongation T7) supporting crash and pothole strain energy.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces forging + MIG welding + e-coat with single vacuum HPDC plus T7 ageing and CNC of joint faces. Alternative low-pressure permanent-mould casting evaluated for porosity control but slower cycle. Deletes one sub-bracket, two welds and phosphate line; BOM drops two fasteners. New die tooling (~£480k) offsets by part-count and finishing reduction; assembly torque stations cut from 4 to 3.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Part integration and finishing deletion outweigh Al price premium.

ideaData.riskNotes: Cast Al fatigue life under cyclic pothole loading is the key risk; mitigated by T7 temper raising elongation, FEA-driven rib placement and shot-peening of ball-joint boss. NVH preserved via hydrobush tuning compensating lower material damping. Crash: arm designed to retain wheel without intruding battery enclosure. Off-road jounce abuse covered by 2.0g vertical and 3.5g longitudinal block-load durability tests; premium ride quality maintained through unsprung-mass reduction sharpening primary ride.

title: Forged 6082-T6 steering knuckle replacing cast iron
description: [BEV] Swap GGG60 nodular cast-iron front steering knuckle for forged 6082-T6 aluminium on BEV skateboard SUV. Cuts ~3.4kg unsprung mass per corner, improving range, steering response and brake-by-wire feel. Forging porosity-free microstructure supports caliper and strut loads; benchmarked to BMW iX front knuckle aluminium architecture.

ideaData.title: Forged 6082-T6 steering knuckle replacing cast iron
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline EN-GJS-600 (GGG60) ductile-iron knuckle ~6.9kg carries hub bearing, caliper and strut/damper interface. Change forges 6082-T6 Al to ~3.5kg with machined bearing bore and caliper bosses. Physics: forging aligns grain flow around stress risers giving high fatigue strength at 40% lower density, lowering unsprung mass to improve range and reduce road-induced steering disturbance. Alternatives: AlSi7Mg0.3 (A356-T6) low-pressure cast (lower fatigue), 7075-T6 forged (stress-corrosion risk needs T73), and HPDC AlSi10MnMg (porosity at bearing bore). 6082-T6 selected for fatigue, corrosion resistance and machinability of the bearing seat to b-by-wire tolerance.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces sand-casting + heat treat + machining with closed-die forging, T6 solution/age and CNC. Alternatives: low-pressure permanent-mould casting (cheaper tooling, lower fatigue) and squeeze casting (intermediate). Forging die ~£360k; deletes risering/feeders and reduces machining stock. Bearing bore concentricity improves first-pass yield; corrosion e-coat removed, replaced by chromate-free seal.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Unsprung-mass and range benefit with neutral piece cost via scrap reduction.

ideaData.riskNotes: Bearing-seat fretting and galvanic corrosion at steel hub interface are primary risks; mitigated with steel insert sleeve and dielectric coating. Crash: knuckle must shear predictably in side-pole to manage wheel intrusion, validated by FMVSS 214 sub-system test. Steering precision and brake-by-wire latency preserved by tight bore tolerance and stiffness-matched strut boss. Durability proven over 300k-cycle multi-axial rig; off-road impact resilience checked at 4g lateral kerb-strike.

title: HPDC gigacast rear subframe replacing stamped-welded assembly
description: [BEV] Replace 9-piece stamped-welded steel rear subframe with single HPDC AlSi10MnMg gigacasting on BEV skateboard. Eliminates ~38 welds and 6 stampings, integrating e-axle and trailing-arm mounts. Cuts assembly time and mass while improving dimensional stability for structural battery interface; benchmarked to Tesla/NIO megacast practice.

ideaData.title: HPDC gigacast rear subframe replacing stamped-welded assembly
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline rear subframe is 9 stamped HSLA S420/DP600 panels MIG/spot-welded into a cradle ~31kg supporting e-motor, links and bushings. Change casts one AlSi10MnMg (or Tesla-grade self-annealing AlSiMnMg) HPDC ~24kg integrating motor mounts, five-link pickups and bush eyes. Physics: single casting removes weld-induced distortion and stack tolerance, giving stiffer load paths and better NVH isolation. Alternatives: AlSi7Mg low-pressure cast cradle (size limits), hydroformed Al tube space-frame (more joints), and DP980 stamped-welded lightweight steel (less integration). Gigacast chosen for part-count collapse and stiffness-to-mass on skateboard packaging.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC / self-annealing AlSiMnMg / DP980 baseline
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces 6 stamping dies, spot/MIG weld cells and a geometry fixture with one 6,100t HPDC machine plus T7 and CNC of mount faces. Alternatives: tailor-welded-blank stamped cradle and hydroformed tube frame both retain joining. Deletes ~38 welds and sealer; assembly stations drop from 7 to 2. Megacast die/cell capex high (~£6.5M) but per-vehicle conversion and scrap fall sharply at volume.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Collapsing weld/stamp operations into one casting slashes conversion cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Cast repairability and crash energy management of a monolithic cradle are key risks; mitigated by designed crush initiators, local wall-thickness tuning and a serviceable bolt-on motor sub-bracket. NVH improves from removal of weld variance but requires retuned hydrobushes to avoid harshness. Durability and battery-interface fatigue validated by 500k-cycle multiaxial and salt-spray testing; off-road articulation loads checked at 3g vertical with no link-mount yielding, preserving premium refinement.

title: PHEV rear subframe reinforcement via tailor-welded blanks
description: [PHEV] Reinforce rear subframe for PHEV battery and e-axle mass using laser tailor-welded DP780/DP980 blanks instead of uniform-gauge plate plus bolted doublers. Puts thickness only where e-axle torque and battery cradle loads peak, cutting mass and deleting reinforcement parts on Audi Q7-class plug-in hybrid.

ideaData.title: PHEV rear subframe reinforcement via tailor-welded blanks
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline rear cradle uses uniform 2.5mm DP600 stampings with bolted-on reinforcement doublers to carry the rear e-axle reaction torque and added battery cradle mass (~140kg pack).
