Change uses laser tailor-welded blanks combining 1.8mm DP780 in low-stress zones with 3.0mm DP980 at motor-mount and link pickups. Physics: gauge placed by FE stress map raises local stiffness and fatigue margin without global mass penalty. Alternatives: hot-stamped 22MnB5 1500MPa local patches, HPDC AlSi10MnMg cradle (cost), and roll-formed HSLA S420 closed sections. TWB chosen to delete doublers, fasteners and an assembly station while meeting PHEV duty cycle.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces uniform stamping + bolted doublers with laser-welded blank then single draw. Alternatives: hot stamping for patches and roll forming for rails. Deletes 4 doublers, 12 fasteners and one weld station; blank welding adds a laser cell but nets fewer parts. Spot-weld count down ~14%; sealer and corrosion-coat scope reduced.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Gauge-where-needed deletes doublers and trims mass simultaneously.

ideaData.riskNotes: Weld-line fatigue under PHEV e-axle torque reversals is the main concern; mitigated by placing the laser seam outside peak-stress bands and HAZ hardness control. NVH preserved as stiffer local mounts reduce e-axle whine transmission. Crash: rear cradle must manage offset-rear loads without pack intrusion, validated to UN R94 and pack-retention tests. Durability proven on 350k-cycle four-poster representing loaded plug-in mass; premium ride retained through tuned bush rates.

title: Hydroformed front subframe rails replacing stamped twin-shell
description: [ICE,MHEV] Replace stamped twin-shell welded front subframe rails with hydroformed HSLA S420 closed tubes on ICE/48V SUV. Closed section boosts torsional stiffness and crash load path while removing the longitudinal seam weld, cutting weld count and sealer. Benchmarked to Mercedes GLE front cradle architecture.

ideaData.title: Hydroformed front subframe rails replacing stamped twin-shell
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline front cradle rails are two stamped DP600 half-shells spot-welded into a closed box with a continuous flange seam, ~16kg/pair. Change hydroforms HSLA S420 tube into variable-section closed rails ~13.5kg, no seam flange. Physics: seamless closed section maximises polar moment for torsional stiffness and gives a cleaner axial crush path for frontal impact. Alternatives: roll-formed HSLA with laser-welded seam, HPDC AlSi10MnMg cradle (cost/repair), and DP980 stamped twin-shell (retains welds). Hydroforming chosen to delete flange material, seam welds and improve stiffness-to-mass for 48V mild-hybrid front-end packaging.
ideaData.materialGrade: Hydroformed HSLA S420 / DP600 baseline / roll-formed DP780
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces twin-shell stamping + ~26 spot welds with tube pre-bend, hydroforming and end machining. Alternatives: roll forming plus laser seam, and tailor-welded tube. Deletes flange seam, sealer bead and two weld fixtures; part count for rails halves. Hydroform press and tooling capex (~£1.9M) amortised by conversion and corrosion-coat savings on the now-sealed cavity.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Seamless tube removes weld/sealer operations and flange scrap.

ideaData.riskNotes: Hydroform wall-thinning at tight bends risks local crush-mode change; mitigated by pressure-sequence tuning and thickness mapping into crash FE. NVH improves with stiffer closed rails reducing front-end shake. Crash: axial crush stability validated to UN R94 and US NCAP frontal, ensuring stable fold without peak-force spikes. Durability and corrosion proven via cavity-wax and 240k-cycle road-load; premium steering precision retained from higher cradle stiffness improving toe control.

title: Commonised five-link rear upper control arm across ICE/BEV
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Commonise the rear five-link upper control arm across ICE, PHEV and BEV variants using one forged 6082-T6 arm with shared bush eyes and length. Removes three variant part numbers, consolidating forging dies and validation. Benchmarked to BMW X7 multi-powertrain rear axle commonisation.

ideaData.title: Commonised five-link rear upper control arm across ICE/BEV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline carries three distinct upper-link part numbers tuned per powertrain ride height and rate, each its own forging die and homologation. Change defines one forged 6082-T6 arm sized to the BEV (highest sprung-mass) load case with shared 14mm bush eyes and common length, ride differences handled via bush rate not geometry. Physics: worst-case BEV load envelope bounds all variants, so a single arm carries ICE/PHEV with margin. Alternatives: A356-T6 cast arm (lower fatigue), HPDC AlSi10MnMg (porosity), and stamped DP780 fabricated arm (heavier). Forged 6082-T6 chosen for fatigue margin enabling one design across all duty cycles.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates three forging dies and machining programs into one; alternatives considered were cast and stamped fabrication. Deletes two part numbers, two sets of validation tooling and reduces spares complexity. One die set (~£300k) replaces three; bush-rate variants handled by elastomer change only, no new metalwork. Logistics and line-side kitting simplified.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One arm across powertrains slashes tooling and validation spend.

ideaData.riskNotes: Over-design for ICE/PHEV adds slight mass; justified by tooling and validation savings and negligible ride penalty. NVH preserved by powertrain-specific bush tuning rather than arm geometry. Durability bounded by BEV worst-case so ICE/PHEV inherit margin, validated by shared 400k-cycle rig. Crash and kinematics unchanged as hardpoints are common; premium ride differentiation retained through calibrated bush rates and damper tuning, not metal changes.

title: Hydrobush optimisation deleting one rear link bushing variant
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] Rationalise five-link rear bushings from five distinct rates to four by retuning a single hydrobush to cover two link positions. Deletes one bushing part number and its validation while preserving axle compliance and NVH. Benchmarked to Lexus RX rear suspension refinement targets.

ideaData.title: Hydrobush optimisation deleting one rear link bushing variant
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline rear five-link uses five unique elastomer/hydraulic bushings, two of which have closely matched radial rates but different part numbers. Change retunes a single hydrobush (natural-rubber/NR-EPDM blend with glycol fluid) and a revised void profile to serve both positions within compliance targets. Physics: voided bush geometry sets radial/conical rate independently, letting one part cover both stations. Alternatives: solid NR bush (less decoupling), polyurethane bush (harsher), and FKM-fluid hydrobush (cost). NR/glycol hydrobush chosen for ride-isolation and durability across ICE/PHEV duty while enabling part deletion.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Removes one bush variant from moulding tooling and the assembly kit. Alternatives: solid-rubber and PU bushings evaluated. Deletes one mould cavity set, one part number and one validation stream; injection-mould cycle unchanged. Assembly poka-yoke simplified as one fewer look-alike part reduces mis-build risk and rework warranty.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Tuning one bush to two roles deletes a variant and its overhead.

ideaData.riskNotes: Compromise rate could blunt either ride isolation or handling precision; mitigated by void-profile optimisation keeping both stations inside compliance and toe-control windows. NVH validated by transfer-path analysis confirming no boom/harshness regression. Durability proven via 1.2M-cycle bush fatigue at elevated temperature. Crash and kinematics unaffected; premium on-road comfort and steering linearity preserved, confirmed by subjective ride sign-off on representative road loads.

title: Heavy-duty forged 42CrMo4 lower arm for ICE off-road SUV
description: [ICE] Replace cast-iron heavy-duty front lower arm with forged 42CrMo4 (4140) on body-on-frame ICE off-roader. Forging cuts ~1.8kg while raising fatigue strength for high-articulation kerb and rock loads, reducing section without losing capacity. Benchmarked to Toyota Land Cruiser 300 front axle durability.

ideaData.title: Heavy-duty forged 42CrMo4 lower arm for ICE off-road SUV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE. Baseline heavy-duty lower control arm is EN-GJS-700 ductile iron ~9.2kg sized conservatively for off-road impact. Change forges 42CrMo4 (quenched & tempered) at ~7.4kg with grain flow oriented along principal load paths, allowing reduced section at equal capacity. Physics: forged martensitic-bainitic structure raises fatigue and fracture toughness, so material can be removed while retaining rock-crawl and kerb-strike margin. Alternatives: microalloyed 38MnSiVS6 forging (no quench, lower cost), 6082-T6 Al forging (rejected for abrasion/impact on hardcore off-road), and HPDC AlSi10MnMg (impact ductility risk). 42CrMo4 chosen for impact toughness under severe off-road duty.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces sand casting + machining with closed-die forging, quench-temper and CNC. Alternatives: air-cooled microalloyed forging skips heat treat; cast remains heaviest. Deletes feeders/risers and reduces machining stock; forging die ~£340k. Part is more robust to off-road abuse, cutting field-failure warranty and rework. Shot-peening added for fatigue, offset by reduced casting scrap.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Forging enables section reduction at equal off-road capacity.

ideaData.riskNotes: Reduced section must not compromise rock-strike resistance; mitigated by impact FE, shot-peening and a skid-load proof test (5g vertical, 4g longitudinal). Corrosion of 4140 needs robust e-coat plus sacrificial provisions for stone-chip. NVH unchanged at this hardpoint. Off-road capability preserved or improved via higher toughness and unsprung-mass reduction aiding wheel articulation. Durability validated on Baja-profile rig and 250k-cycle abuse; premium on-road ride retained through unchanged kinematics.

title: Cast-iron to forged-Al rear knuckle for PHEV e-axle
description: [PHEV] Convert rear steering/hub knuckle from GGG60 cast iron to forged 6082-T6 on PHEV with rear e-axle. Reduces unsprung mass offsetting added battery and motor weight, improving ride and regen brake feel. Forged Al supports e-axle reaction and toe-link loads; benchmarked to Audi Q8 e-axle rear corner.

ideaData.title: Cast-iron to forged-Al rear knuckle for PHEV e-axle
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline rear knuckle is EN-GJS-600 iron ~5.6kg carrying hub bearing, toe link and e-axle reaction. Change forges 6082-T6 Al to ~3.1kg with machined bearing bore and link bosses. Physics: PHEV adds ~150kg of pack and motor mass, so removing unsprung mass restores ride frequency balance and improves regenerative-brake modulation feel. Alternatives: A356-T6 low-pressure cast (lower fatigue at bearing boss), AlSi10MnMg HPDC (porosity at bore), and 7075-T73 forged (cost/SCC). 6082-T6 chosen for fatigue, weldability and corrosion in the wet rear environment while meeting e-axle torque reaction.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces casting + machining with closed-die forging, T6 and CNC of bore/bosses. Alternatives: low-pressure casting and squeeze casting evaluated. Forging die ~£350k; deletes risers and cuts machining stock. Steel bearing-sleeve insert added to avoid fretting; e-coat replaced by sealed dielectric finish, reducing line scope.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Unsprung-mass cut offsets PHEV mass with neutral piece cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Galvanic corrosion at the steel hub/Al knuckle interface and bearing-bore fretting are key risks; mitigated by stainless sleeve and dielectric coating. Crash: rear corner must retain wheel and protect pack in rear impact, validated to UN R94. NVH and regen feel preserved by stiff toe-link boss and reduced unsprung mass. Durability proven on multiaxial rig at PHEV loaded mass over 320k cycles; premium ride and quiet refinement maintained.

title: Integrated wheel hub-bearing module deleting separate components
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Adopt Gen-3 integrated wheel hub-bearing unit combining hub flange, bearing and ABS encoder, replacing pressed-in Gen-1 bearing plus separate hub. Cuts assembly steps and warranty from press-fit errors across all powertrains. Benchmarked to Porsche Cayenne front corner module.

ideaData.title: Integrated wheel hub-bearing module deleting separate components
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline uses a Gen-1 double-row angular-contact bearing press-fit into the knuckle with a separate forged hub and bolted ABS ring. Change adopts a Gen-3 flanged hub-bearing unit (case-hardened 100Cr6/SAE52100 races, forged C45 flange) pre-greased and sealed with integrated magnetic encoder. Physics: factory-set preload removes field press-fit variation, improving bearing life and run-out for brake-by-wire and stable ABS signal. Alternatives: Gen-2 (flange on outer ring only), ceramic-hybrid bearing (cost), and tapered-roller unit (drag). Gen-3 chosen to delete components, fix preload and cut warranty across every powertrain corner.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces bearing press, hub forging line and ABS-ring assembly with a single bolt-on module. Alternatives: Gen-2 unit and tapered-roller designs evaluated. Deletes press station, two parts and one gauging op; assembly torque-only fit improves first-pass yield. Module is sealed-for-life, removing grease station and reducing warranty from contamination and incorrect preload.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Sealed preset module deletes press-fit steps and warranty.

ideaData.riskNotes: Sealed-for-life module shifts robustness onto supplier preload and seal quality; mitigated by 100% preload measurement and dual-lip seal validation. NVH and brake feel improve from controlled run-out. Crash: hub retention to knuckle validated under lateral kerb-strike. Durability proven via L10 bearing life at premium SUV loads and contaminated-seal salt/mud endurance; off-road sealing checked by wade and dust ingress tests, preserving premium quiet refinement and steering precision.

title: BEV skateboard front subframe HPDC integrating motor mounts
description: [BEV] Replace stamped-welded front subframe with HPDC AlSi10MnMg casting integrating front e-motor mounts, link pickups and steering-rack bosses on BEV skateboard. Collapses ~7 stampings and 30 welds into one part, improving stiffness for steer-by-wire. Benchmarked to Xpeng G9 front structure.

ideaData.title: BEV skateboard front subframe HPDC integrating motor mounts
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline front cradle is 7 stamped DP600/HSLA S420 panels welded into a box ~27kg with bolted rack and motor brackets. Change casts AlSi10MnMg HPDC ~21kg integrating front-drive-unit mounts, lower-link and tension-strut pickups and steering-rack bosses. Physics: monolithic casting removes weld compliance, raising local stiffness for crisp steer-by-wire response and stable crash load path into the skateboard. Alternatives: low-pressure AlSi7Mg cast (size/cycle), extruded-Al bonded space-frame (joint count), and DP980 stamped-welded (less integration). HPDC chosen for part-count collapse and stiffness-to-mass critical to by-wire steering precision.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC / AlSi7Mg low-pressure / DP980 baseline
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces 7 stamping dies and ~30 welds with one HPDC machine plus T7 and CNC of mount faces. Alternatives: bonded extrusion frame and low-pressure casting. Deletes spot/MIG weld cells, sealer and a geometry fixture; assembly stations drop from 6 to 2. Die/cell capex (~£4.2M) offset by conversion, scrap and corrosion-coat reductions on the single Al part.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Integrating mounts into one casting removes weld/stamp conversion.

ideaData.riskNotes: Monolithic casting repairability and crash crush control are primary risks; mitigated by designed deformation zones, serviceable bolt-on rack bracket and local wall tuning. NVH improves via stiffer motor mounts cutting drive-unit whine, retuned with hydromounts. Crash validated to UN R94 frontal and pole, ensuring controlled crush without pack intrusion. Durability and steer-by-wire stiffness verified on 450k-cycle multiaxial; premium handling precision preserved by reduced cradle compliance.

title: Hollow forged anti-roll-bar links replacing solid steel
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace solid 38MnSiVS6 anti-roll-bar drop-links with hollow forged/flow-formed tube links across the five-link axle. Cuts ~0.6kg unsprung per corner with no stiffness loss, improving ride and roll response. Applicable across all powertrains; benchmarked to Mercedes GLS roll-control hardware.

ideaData.title: Hollow forged anti-roll-bar links replacing solid steel
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline drop-links use solid 38MnSiVS6 microalloyed steel rod with forged ball-stud ends. Change uses a hollow flow-formed/forged tube link (DP780 or 34MnB5) with friction-welded ball studs, removing low-stress core material. Physics: axial/bending loads concentrate near the surface, so a hollow section retains stiffness and buckling capacity at lower mass, cutting unsprung weight. Alternatives: solid forged baseline, 6082-T6 Al link (lower stiffness, larger section), and GFRP composite link (joint integrity risk). Hollow steel chosen for stiffness, fatigue and low cost across all powertrain variants.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces solid forging with tube flow-forming plus friction/projection-welded ball studs. Alternatives: solid forging and Al forging. Tube process reduces raw mass and machining; ball-stud weld adds a cell but nets lower material cost. Corrosion handled by sealed-tube e-coat; minimal tooling change (~£140k) for the welding fixture.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Removing inert core mass cuts unsprung weight at equal stiffness.

ideaData.riskNotes: Tube-to-ball-stud weld fatigue and internal corrosion are the main risks; mitigated by friction-weld qualification, NDT and sealed cavity wax. Stiffness and buckling validated by FE and physical to match solid link, preserving roll response and steering feel. NVH unaffected as ball-joint isolation is unchanged. Durability proven on 1M-cycle link rig and corrosion cycling; premium handling and flat cornering retained, off-road articulation loads checked at full droop.

title: Stamped-welded to gigacast rear cradle for BEV three-motor
description: [BEV] Convert high-power BEV rear cradle from stamped-welded DP980 to HPDC gigacast AlSi10MnMg supporting dual rear motors and torque-vectoring loads. Integrates motor, link and damper mounts in one part, deleting ~40 welds. Improves stiffness for high-torque launch; benchmarked to Yangwang U8 quad-motor chassis.

ideaData.title: Stamped-welded to gigacast rear cradle for BEV three-motor
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline is a DP980/DP600 stamped-welded rear cradle ~34kg with bolted dual-motor brackets carrying high torque-vectoring reactions. Change casts AlSi10MnMg HPDC ~26kg integrating both motor mounts, five-link pickups and damper towers. Physics: monolithic Al cradle removes weld compliance under high launch torque, stabilising toe/camber and stiffening the load path for torque vectoring. Alternatives: hot-stamped 22MnB5 reinforced steel cradle (heavier), low-pressure AlSi7Mg (size), and bonded extrusion frame (joints). Gigacast chosen for stiffness-to-mass and part-count collapse under extreme drive torque.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces stamping dies and ~40 welds with one 6,100t HPDC plus T7 and CNC. Alternatives: hot stamping and bonded extrusion. Deletes weld cells, sealer and fixturing; assembly stations drop from 7 to 2. Megacast capex (~£6.8M) offset by conversion, scrap and corrosion savings; serviceable motor sub-bracket retained for repair.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One casting absorbs torque-vectoring loads and deletes weld conversion.

ideaData.riskNotes: High-torque fatigue at motor mounts and casting repairability are key risks; mitigated by local wall tuning, serviceable bolt-on motor bracket and HCF testing at peak vectoring loads. NVH improves from stiffer mounts cutting drive whine, retuned with hydromounts. Crash to UN R94 with pack retention validated. Durability over 500k torque-reversal cycles; premium launch stability and refinement preserved through reduced cradle compliance and controlled toe.

title: Roll-formed HSLA rear link replacing stamped fabrication
description: [ICE,MHEV] Replace two-shell stamped-welded rear trailing link with roll-formed HSLA S420 closed section on ICE/48V SUV. Continuous roll forming raises material utilisation and deletes the seam-weld station while improving bending stiffness. Benchmarked to Volvo XC90 rear axle link design.

ideaData.title: Roll-formed HSLA rear link replacing stamped fabrication
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline rear trailing link is two stamped DP600 shells spot-welded into a box ~3.9kg with flange seam. Change roll-forms HSLA S420 strip into a closed section ~3.3kg with a single laser-welded longitudinal seam. Physics: continuous forming gives uniform wall and high section modulus for bending/buckling with less scrap than blanking two shells. Alternatives: stamped twin-shell baseline, hydroformed tube (capex), and 6082-T6 Al extrusion (cost/stiffness). Roll forming chosen for material utilisation, throughput and stiffness on the 48V mild-hybrid rear link.
ideaData.materialGrade: Roll-formed HSLA S420 / DP600 baseline / 6082-T6 extrusion
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces blank/stamp/spot-weld with roll forming plus inline laser seam and end machining. Alternatives: hydroforming and Al extrusion. Material utilisation rises from ~62% to ~88%; deletes a weld fixture and reduces flange scrap. Roll-form tooling (~£260k) amortised by scrap and conversion savings; continuous process lifts throughput.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Continuous forming lifts utilisation and deletes a weld station.

ideaData.riskNotes: Laser-seam fatigue and springback-driven length tolerance are the main risks; mitigated by seam placement away from peak stress, weld NDT and inline laser cut-to-length. Bending stiffness and buckling validated to match or exceed stamped link, preserving toe control and ride. NVH unchanged. Durability proven over 400k-cycle road-load; corrosion handled by sealed-section wax, off-road jounce loads verified at full bump.

title: Commonised steering knuckle across front/rear via mirrored casting
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Design one A356-T6 knuckle casting machined into left/right and front/rear variants from a common raw casting, replacing four unique knuckles. Cuts casting tooling four-fold and simplifies logistics across all powertrains. Benchmarked to Cadillac Escalade corner commonisation.

ideaData.title: Commonised steering knuckle across front/rear via mirrored casting
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline uses four unique knuckle part numbers (front L/R, rear L/R) each with its own casting tool. Change defines one symmetric A356-T6 (AlSi7Mg0.3) low-pressure raw casting with all bosses present, then machines position-specific features to create each variant. Physics: a superset casting carries every hardpoint; only finish machining differentiates handedness and axle, bounded by the most-loaded corner. Alternatives: forged 6082-T6 superset (machining cost), HPDC AlSi10MnMg (porosity at bores), and GGG60 iron (mass). A356-T6 chosen for castability of the superset and adequate fatigue across all corners.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates four casting tools into one superset mould; differentiation moves to CNC. Alternatives: forged superset and HPDC. Deletes three casting tools and reduces raw-part SKUs to one, simplifying foundry scheduling and logistics. Machining adds a few features per variant but net tooling and inventory fall sharply; one mould (~£420k) replaces four.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Superset casting plus machining replaces four unique tools.

ideaData.riskNotes: Superset casting carries unused bosses adding minor mass and machining; justified by four-fold tooling and SKU reduction. Each variant must still meet its corner load case, validated by the worst-corner envelope. NVH and steering precision preserved as machined bores hold tight tolerance. Crash retention per corner validated to FMVSS 214 and UN R94. Durability bounded by most-loaded corner over 350k cycles; premium handling consistency maintained across axles.

title: PHEV front subframe local hot-stamp patch for battery mass
description: [PHEV] Reinforce front subframe motor/engine cradle for PHEV mass using local hot-stamped 22MnB5 1500MPa patches instead of thicker uniform DP780 plus bolted plates. Targets stiffness at mount nodes, cutting mass and deleting reinforcement brackets. Benchmarked to BMW X5 xDrive45e front cradle.

ideaData.title: PHEV front subframe local hot-stamp patch for battery mass
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline front cradle uses uniform 2.8mm DP780 with bolted reinforcement plates to carry combined ICE plus front-motor and battery-routing mass. Change applies tailor-welded hot-stamped 22MnB5 1500MPa boron patches at the engine-mount and link nodes over thinner 2.0mm DP780 base. Physics: ultra-high-strength patches add local stiffness and yield margin precisely where node loads peak, removing global over-gauge. Alternatives: full-cradle 22MnB5 (formability/cost), HPDC AlSi10MnMg cradle (capex), and DP980 cold-stamped patch (less strength). Boron patch chosen for highest strength-to-mass at the critical mounts under PHEV duty.
ideaData.materialGrade: 22MnB5 1500MPa boron patch / DP780 base / DP980 alt
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces uniform stamping plus bolted plates with patchwork tailor-welded blank then press-hardening. Alternatives: full hot-stamp and HPDC. Deletes 3 reinforcement plates and 10 fasteners; adds a press-hardening cell but removes a bolting station. Spot-weld count down; corrosion-coat scope reduced as bolted joints disappear.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Boron patches delete bolted plates and trim global gauge.

ideaData.riskNotes: Patch-to-base weld and HAZ softening under boron press-hardening are key risks; mitigated by laser-weld qualification, tempering control and placing seams off peak-stress. NVH preserved as stiffer mounts cut powertrain shake. Crash: cradle must manage frontal crush with added PHEV mass, validated to UN R94 and US NCAP. Durability over 350k-cycle road-load at loaded plug-in mass; premium ride and isolation maintained through retuned mounts.

title: BEV lightweight forged-Al lower link for skateboard efficiency
description: [BEV] Replace HPDC AlSi10MnMg front lower link with weight-optimised forged 6082-T6 on BEV skateboard to cut unsprung mass and extend range. Forging allows thinner web sections at higher fatigue strength. Net mass down ~1.4kg/corner; benchmarked to Li Auto L9 air-suspension front axle.

ideaData.title: BEV lightweight forged-Al lower link for skateboard efficiency
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline front lower link is HPDC AlSi10MnMg ~4.2kg with conservative wall thickness for cast soundness. Change forges 6082-T6 to ~2.8kg using thinner optimised webs enabled by porosity-free forged microstructure and higher fatigue strength. Physics: forging removes the porosity penalty, so sections shrink to a stress-driven minimum, cutting unsprung mass to improve range and primary ride. Alternatives: HPDC baseline, A356-T6 cast (lower fatigue), and 7075-T73 forged (SCC/cost). 6082-T6 chosen for fatigue, corrosion and formability allowing aggressive lightweighting on the air-suspension skateboard.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces HPDC with closed-die forging, T6 and CNC of joint faces. Alternatives: low-pressure casting and squeeze casting. Forging die ~£330k; deletes casting porosity-rework and reduces scrap. Thinner sections cut raw mass; air-spring bracket integrated into the forging removing one bolted part and two fasteners.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Forged thin webs cut unsprung mass and integrate a bracket.

ideaData.riskNotes: Thin forged webs risk buckling under kerb-strike and air-spring side loads; mitigated by buckling FE, rib placement and shot-peening. NVH preserved via hydrobush tuning compensating reduced material damping. Crash: link retains wheel without pack intrusion, validated to UN R94. Durability over 350k-cycle multiaxial including air-spring loads; off-road and pothole abuse at 3g vertical verified, preserving premium air-suspension ride and range benefit.

evidenceSources.title: Forged vs cast link mass study

title: Press-fit to bolt-on ball joint in lower arm for serviceability
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] Convert press-fit ball joint in front lower arm to a bolt-on cartridge, deleting press tooling and enabling field replacement instead of whole-arm scrap. Cuts warranty and service cost across ICE/hybrid SUVs. Benchmarked to Jeep Grand Cherokee front suspension serviceability.

ideaData.title: Press-fit to bolt-on ball joint in lower arm for serviceability
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline presses a ball joint into the forged-steel arm eye; wear means scrapping the whole arm. Change uses a 3-bolt flanged ball-joint cartridge (forged 41Cr4 housing, hardened stud, PTFE-lined seat) bolted to a machined boss. Physics: bolted interface gives defined preload and serviceability without compromising stud-load capacity. Alternatives: press-fit baseline, riveted joint (non-serviceable), and integral cast joint (scrap whole arm). Bolt-on cartridge chosen to enable field replacement, delete the press operation and cut warranty across ICE/hybrid duty.
ideaData.materialGrade: Forged 41Cr4 housing, hardened 16MnCr5 stud, PTFE seat
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces ball-joint press station with a machined boss and torque-fasten. Alternatives: press-fit and riveting. Deletes the press and its gauging; adds a CNC boss face and three fasteners. Serviceability cuts dealer whole-arm replacement, lowering warranty and spares cost; assembly becomes torque-only improving first-pass yield.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Bolt-on cartridge enables service and deletes the press op.

ideaData.riskNotes: Bolted joint must hold preload under reversed stud loads without loosening; mitigated by patch-lock fasteners, defined torque-angle and joint-fatigue test. Stud articulation and sealing validated for mud/water ingress on off-road duty. NVH and steering feel unchanged as joint stiffness matches press-fit. Durability proven over 1M-cycle ball-joint rig plus loosening test; premium steering precision and service economics preserved.

title: Mg AM60B steering knuckle pilot for ultimate BEV mass cut
description: [BEV] Pilot AE44/AM60B magnesium HPDC rear knuckle on premium BEV to cut unsprung mass below aluminium, extending range and sharpening ride. Requires galvanic isolation and corrosion strategy; net mass ~1.1kg below forged Al. Benchmarked to AITO M9 lightweight rear corner.

ideaData.title: Mg AM60B steering knuckle pilot for ultimate BEV mass cut
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline rear knuckle is forged 6082-T6 Al ~3.1kg. Change pilots AE44 (or AM60B) magnesium HPDC ~2.0kg with steel bearing-sleeve insert and full galvanic isolation. Physics: Mg density (~1.8 g/cc) cuts unsprung mass further than Al, improving range and wheel control, while AE44's creep resistance suits the loaded bearing boss. Alternatives: forged 6082-T6 (heavier), AlSi10MnMg HPDC (heavier), and CFRP knuckle (cost/joint risk). AE44/AM60B chosen for lowest mass with adequate elongation and creep, accepting corrosion-management cost on a premium low-volume BEV.
ideaData.materialGrade: AE44 / AM60B Mg HPDC / forged 6082-T6 baseline
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces forging with hot-chamber/cold-chamber Mg HPDC plus T-temper and CNC; steel sleeve pressed at bore. Alternatives: Al forging and Al HPDC. Mg die (~£500k) and protective coating line add cost, offset partly by mass-driven range value and integration of sensor bosses. Stringent isolation (e-coat, dielectric washers, stainless fasteners) added.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Magnesium beats aluminium on unsprung mass, adding range value.

ideaData.riskNotes: Galvanic corrosion at steel/Mg interfaces and creep at the bearing boss are the dominant risks; mitigated by AE44 creep grade, steel sleeve, dielectric isolation and conversion coating. Crash: knuckle must retain wheel and protect pack in rear impact, validated to UN R94 with Mg ductility characterised. NVH preserved by stiff boss design. Durability over 320k multiaxial cycles plus accelerated salt-spray; premium ride and range gains validated, off-road exposure limited by trim strategy.

title: Hydroformed rear axle beam replacing welded twist-beam
description: [ICE,MHEV] Replace stamped-welded twist-beam cross-member with a hydroformed HSLA S500 tube tuned for torsional roll stiffness on entry premium ICE/48V SUV. Single tube deletes shell welds and tunes rate via section profile. Benchmarked to Ford Bronco rear suspension cross-member.

ideaData.title: Hydroformed rear axle beam replacing welded twist-beam
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline twist-beam cross-member is two stamped DP600 V-shells welded together, ~9kg, with seam-controlled torsional rate. Change hydroforms HSLA S500 tube with a varying closed-to-open profile to set roll stiffness directly, ~7.6kg. Physics: open-section mid-span gives controlled torsion while closed ends carry bending; hydroforming achieves the transition seamlessly without welds. Alternatives: stamped twin-shell baseline, roll-formed beam with welded seam, and 6082-T6 extruded beam (rate tuning limits). Hydroforming chosen to delete seam welds and tune torsional rate by geometry on the 48V mild-hybrid rear axle.
ideaData.materialGrade: Hydroformed HSLA S500 / DP600 baseline / 6082-T6 extrusion
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces twin-shell stamping and seam welding with tube pre-bend and hydroforming. Alternatives: roll forming and extrusion. Deletes ~20 welds and a fixture; profile transition removes a separate stiffness-tuning bracket. Hydroform tooling (~£1.5M) amortised by weld and bracket deletion plus reduced corrosion sealing on the closed ends.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Seamless profile sets roll rate and deletes shell welds.

ideaData.riskNotes: Open-section fatigue and wall-thinning at the torsion zone are key risks; mitigated by pressure-sequence control, thickness mapping and HCF testing of the transition. Roll stiffness and toe-compliance validated to match target, preserving handling and ride. NVH unchanged or improved by removing weld variance. Durability over 400k torsional cycles; corrosion handled by closed-end wax and coating, off-road articulation loads checked at full wind-up.

title: Commonised front subframe casting across BEV and PHEV
description: [PHEV,BEV] Design one HPDC AlSi10MnMg front subframe serving both PHEV and BEV by including motor and engine-mount provisions in a common casting, machined per variant. Deletes a second subframe tool and validation stream. Benchmarked to Hongqi E-HS9 multi-energy platform.

ideaData.title: Commonised front subframe casting across BEV and PHEV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV/BEV. Baseline runs two front subframes: a PHEV steel-reinforced cradle and a BEV Al casting. Change defines one AlSi10MnMg HPDC superset including both front-motor mounts and PHEV engine/transmission-mount bosses, machined to expose the relevant features per variant. Physics: a common casting carries the bounding load set (PHEV combined ICE+motor torque vs BEV motor torque), with finish machining selecting mounts. Alternatives: separate steel PHEV cradle and Al BEV cradle (two tools), full forging (cost), and bonded extrusion (joints). Common HPDC chosen to delete a tool and validation while meeting both powertrains' mount loads.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC superset / DP980 steel PHEV / AlSi7Mg cast
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates two subframe tool sets into one HPDC superset die; differentiation by CNC. Alternatives: separate steel and Al cradles, and forging. Deletes one die set, one weld line (the steel PHEV cradle) and a validation program. Megacast die (~£5.5M) replaces two toolings; logistics simplified to one raw casting feeding both lines.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One superset casting serves both energies, deleting a tool and line.

ideaData.riskNotes: Superset casting carries unused mount bosses and must satisfy the worst-case PHEV torque path; mitigated by bounding-load FE and local wall tuning. NVH tuned per variant via mount elastomers, not metal. Crash: both variants validated to UN R94 with pack/engine retention as applicable. Durability bounded by PHEV combined-torque case over 450k cycles; premium refinement preserved through variant-specific mount calibration, repair via serviceable sub-brackets.

title: ICE heavy-duty cast-iron arm to ADI for weight and wear
description: [ICE] Upgrade heavy-duty rear control arm from GGG60 to austempered ductile iron (ADI) on body-on-frame ICE SUV, raising strength to allow section reduction and ~1.6kg cut while improving wear at the bush bore. Benchmarked to Jeep Wrangler solid-axle link durability.

ideaData.title: ICE heavy-duty cast-iron arm to ADI for weight and wear
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE. Baseline rear link is EN-GJS-600 ductile iron ~7.8kg sized for solid-axle off-road loads. Change uses austempered ductile iron (ADI grade EN-GJS-1050-6) ~6.2kg, whose ausferrite structure roughly doubles strength and improves wear, allowing section thinning. Physics: higher yield and fatigue let the section shrink while retaining articulation and impact capacity; ADI wear resistance benefits the bush bore. Alternatives: forged 42CrMo4 (cost), 6082-T6 Al (impact/abrasion on off-road), and GGG60 baseline. ADI chosen for strength-to-cost and toughness under severe solid-axle ICE off-road duty.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces standard ductile-iron casting with as-cast plus austempering heat treat and machining. Alternatives: forging and Al forging. Austempering cell added but section reduction cuts raw mass and machining; no new pattern needed beyond minor section change (~£90k). ADI bore wear resistance reduces bush-fretting warranty.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: ADI strength enables section cut at equal off-road capacity.

ideaData.riskNotes: Austemper process control and thinner sections under rock-strike are the main risks; mitigated by tight bath/time control, impact FE and a 5g skid-load proof test. ADI machinability is harder, addressed by tool selection and as-near-net casting. NVH unchanged at this hardpoint. Off-road articulation and durability validated on Rubicon-profile rig over 250k cycles; premium on-road ride retained via unchanged kinematics and reduced unsprung mass.

title: Wheel hub material downgrade from forged to cast for BEV rear
description: [BEV] Replace forged C45 wheel-hub flange with near-net low-pressure cast EN-GJS-700 on lightly loaded BEV rear corner where bending loads permit, cutting machining and material cost. Loads verified lower than driven front. Benchmarked to NIO ES7 rear hub assembly.

ideaData.title: Wheel hub material downgrade from forged to cast for BEV rear
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline rear hub flange is forged C45 steel, conservatively specified to match the driven front. Change uses near-net low-pressure cast EN-GJS-700 ductile iron for the non-driven or low-torque rear hub where bending dominates over torsion. Physics: rear-corner duty has lower cyclic torque, so ductile iron's fatigue suffices with a modest section increase, trading a little mass for lower cost and machining. Alternatives: forged C45 baseline, forged microalloyed 38MnSiVS6, and A356-T6 Al hub (galvanic/bearing concerns). Cast GGG70 chosen for cost where loads permit, validated against measured rear-corner spectra.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces forging and heavy machining with near-net low-pressure casting plus light CNC of bearing/bolt faces. Alternatives: forging and Al casting. Casting cuts raw cost and machining stock; pattern tooling (~£120k) modest. Applies only to rear corner where load spectra are validated lower, keeping forged hub on driven front.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Cast iron suffices on low-torque rear, cutting forge and machining cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Casting porosity at the bolt-flange and slightly higher mass are the risks; mitigated by low-pressure process control, X-ray sampling and load-spectrum validation confirming rear duty is below forged-front levels. Bearing run-out and brake feel preserved by machined seat tolerance. Crash hub retention validated to UN R94. Durability over L10 plus 300k-cycle rear-corner spectra; premium quiet refinement and brake modulation maintained, no off-road exposure penalty.

evidenceSources.title: Cast vs forged hub cost data

title: Five-link to four-link rear architecture reducing part count
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] Re-architect rear suspension from five-link to an optimised four-link with integrated toe-control geometry, deleting one link, two bushings and four fasteners per side while preserving kinematics. Benchmarked to Mercedes GLE rear axle simplification.

ideaData.title: Five-link to four-link rear architecture reducing part count
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline rear axle uses five links per side to independently set camber, toe and longitudinal compliance. Change adopts an optimised four-link where a wider-based upper control arm (forged 6082-T6) absorbs the deleted link's toe-control role via geometry and bush rate. Physics: kinematic synthesis recovers the elastokinematic toe/camber curves with one fewer link, trading slight tuning freedom for part reduction. Alternatives: keep five-link (baseline), integral-link (more parts), and trailing-blade axle (compliance limits). Four-link chosen to delete a link, two bushings and fasteners while preserving premium handling and ride.
ideaData.materialGrade: Forged 6082-T6 upper arm / DP780 link / NR hydrobush
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes one link forging/stamping, two bush mouldings and four fasteners per side, plus their assembly stations. Alternatives: integral-link and blade designs. Removes two part numbers and a sub-fixture; rear-axle assembly cycle shortens. Tooling for the revised upper arm (~£300k) offset by deleted link and bushing tooling and reduced line labour.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Removing a link and its bushings cuts parts, mass and assembly.

ideaData.riskNotes: Reduced links cut elastokinematic tuning freedom risking toe-under-load and ride compromise; mitigated by kinematic optimisation, bush-rate tuning and extensive vehicle-dynamics sign-off. NVH validated by transfer-path analysis ensuring no harshness from stiffer bushes. Crash and rear-impact load paths re-validated to UN R94. Durability over 400k-cycle rig; premium handling precision and ride comfort preserved by carefully synthesised geometry, off-road articulation maintained.

title: Bushing commonisation across front and rear lower arms
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Standardise the large compliance bushing to one part across front and rear lower arms by aligning eye diameter and rate window, deleting one variant and its tooling. Applies across all powertrains. Benchmarked to Audi Q7 suspension bushing rationalisation.

ideaData.title: Bushing commonisation across front and rear lower arms
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline uses distinct large compliance bushings for front and rear lower arms with different eye diameters and rates. Change aligns both arm eyes to a common 58mm bore and tunes one voided NR/EPDM hydrobush whose rate window covers both stations. Physics: matching the arm eye geometry lets one bush serve both, with required rate differences absorbed by the elastic compliance envelope. Alternatives: keep two bushings (baseline), solid NR bush (less isolation), and PU bush (harshness). Common hydrobush chosen to delete a variant and its mould across every powertrain while keeping ride and handling targets.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Requires a minor arm-eye redesign on one arm to common bore, then deletes one bush mould cavity and part number. Alternatives: solid and PU bushings. Removes a moulding tool (~£110k saved) and a kit SKU; reduces look-alike mis-build warranty. Injection-mould cycle unchanged; logistics and line kitting simplified.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One bush across two arms deletes a mould and a SKU.

ideaData.riskNotes: Common rate window may slightly compromise front or rear tuning; mitigated by void-profile design keeping both inside compliance targets and confirmed by ride/handling sign-off. NVH validated via transfer-path analysis for no boom or harshness regression. Crash and kinematics unaffected as hardpoints unchanged. Durability over 1.2M-cycle bush fatigue; premium ride isolation and steering linearity preserved across all powertrains and load cases.

title: BEV rear subframe HPDC with integrated structural pack interface
description: [BEV] Cast rear subframe as HPDC AlSi10MnMg integrating the structural-battery-pack rear mounting plane and motor mounts, deleting a separate pack-mount cross-member and ~30 welds. Improves stiffness for cell-to-chassis pack. Benchmarked to NIO EC7 structural pack integration.

ideaData.title: BEV rear subframe HPDC with integrated structural pack interface
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline uses a stamped-welded rear subframe plus a separate bolted cross-member forming the rear pack-mount plane. Change casts AlSi10MnMg HPDC rear subframe integrating the structural-pack rear mounting plane, motor mounts and link pickups in one part. Physics: integrating the pack interface removes a bolted joint stack, raising stiffness and stabilising the cell-to-chassis load path for torsional rigidity. Alternatives: stamped-welded cradle plus cross-member (baseline), extruded-Al bonded frame (joints), and low-pressure casting (size). HPDC chosen to integrate the pack plane, delete a cross-member and ~30 welds while supporting structural-battery stiffness.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC / DP980 stamped baseline / extruded 6082
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces stamped cradle, separate cross-member and ~30 welds with one HPDC plus T7 and CNC of the pack-mount plane. Alternatives: bonded extrusion and low-pressure casting. Deletes a part, a weld line and a bolting station; pack-plane flatness machined to seal tolerance. Megacast die (~£5.8M) offset by conversion, part and weld deletion.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Integrating the pack plane deletes a cross-member and weld line.

ideaData.riskNotes: Pack-plane flatness, sealing and casting repairability are key risks; mitigated by CNC of the mating plane, gasket strategy and serviceable motor sub-bracket. NVH improves from stiffer pack interface cutting structure-borne noise, retuned with mounts. Crash: rear-impact pack retention validated to UN R94/R100 with controlled crush. Durability over 500k-cycle multiaxial plus pack-load fatigue; premium refinement and torsional rigidity preserved by integrated structural path.

title: Forged-Al to HPDC integrated knuckle-carrier for BEV cost
description: [BEV] Replace forged 6082-T6 front knuckle plus bolted strut carrier with one HPDC AlSi10MnMg integrated knuckle-carrier on high-volume BEV, deleting a bracket and two fasteners and cutting machining. Loads moderate enough for cast. Benchmarked to Xpeng G9 front corner.

ideaData.title: Forged-Al to HPDC integrated knuckle-carrier for BEV cost
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline front knuckle is forged 6082-T6 with a separately bolted strut/damper carrier bracket. Change casts AlSi10MnMg HPDC (T7) integrating the knuckle and strut carrier as one ~3.6kg part. Physics: where front-corner loads are moderate (lower-power BEV variant), cast T7 ductility and integrated ribbing meet fatigue while deleting the bolted joint. Alternatives: forged 6082-T6 baseline, A356-T6 low-pressure cast (lower fatigue), and GGG60 iron (mass). HPDC chosen to integrate the carrier, cut forging plus machining cost and reduce part count on the volume BEV front corner.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces forging plus bolted-bracket assembly with one vacuum HPDC, T7 and CNC of bearing bore and strut bosses. Alternatives: forging and low-pressure casting. Deletes one bracket, two fasteners and a bolting station; net machining lower than forged baseline. HPDC die (~£460k) offset by part integration and reduced finishing.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Casting integrates the carrier, deleting a bracket and machining.

ideaData.riskNotes: Cast fatigue at the integrated strut boss and bearing-bore porosity are the risks; mitigated by vacuum HPDC, T7 temper, FE-driven ribbing and bore X-ray sampling. Applied only to moderate-load BEV variant; high-power variant keeps forged knuckle. NVH and steering precision preserved via tight bore and stiff carrier. Crash retention validated to UN R94 and pole. Durability over 350k multiaxial cycles; premium handling maintained, off-road exposure limited on this variant.

title: PHEV rear subframe gauge optimisation via topology and TWB
description: [PHEV] Topology-optimise the PHEV rear subframe and deploy DP780/DP980 tailor-welded blanks to carry battery and e-axle loads with minimum gauge, cutting ~3.2kg without bolted reinforcements. Benchmarked to Volvo XC90 Recharge rear cradle.

ideaData.title: PHEV rear subframe gauge optimisation via topology and TWB
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline rear cradle is uniform 2.6mm DP600 with bolted reinforcements to handle the rear e-axle reaction and battery cradle mass. Change runs topology and gauge optimisation, realised as DP780/DP980 tailor-welded blanks placing 3.0mm at the e-axle and pack nodes and 1.8mm elsewhere. Physics: stress-driven thickness distribution carries the loaded-PHEV duty at minimum mass, deleting bolted doublers. Alternatives: hot-stamped 22MnB5 patches (cost), HPDC AlSi10MnMg cradle (capex), and uniform DP780 (heavier). TWB topology chosen for mass-optimal load carrying and reinforcement deletion under PHEV battery and torque loads.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces uniform stamping plus bolted reinforcements with laser-welded blanks and a single draw. Alternatives: hot-stamp patches and HPDC. Deletes 4 reinforcement plates, 12 fasteners and one station; laser-weld cell added but nets fewer parts. Spot-weld count and sealer scope fall; corrosion-coat simplified by removing bolted laps.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Topology-driven TWB carries loads at minimum gauge, deleting doublers.

ideaData.riskNotes: Weld-line fatigue at gauge transitions under e-axle torque reversals is the key risk; mitigated by seam placement off peak stress, HAZ control and HCF validation. NVH preserved as stiffer nodes reduce e-axle whine transmission. Crash: rear cradle manages offset-rear loads with pack retention, validated to UN R94. Durability over 360k-cycle road-load at loaded plug-in mass; premium ride and quiet refinement maintained through tuned bushes.

title: ICE front subframe stamped-welded utilisation and weld reduction
description: [ICE,MHEV] Re-engineer the stamped-welded steel front subframe to raise blank utilisation and convert ~30% of spot welds to structural adhesive bonding, cutting weld energy, flange material and distortion. Benchmarked to Lexus LX body-on-frame front cradle.

ideaData.title: ICE front subframe stamped-welded utilisation and weld reduction
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline front subframe is DP600/HSLA S420 stampings joined by ~120 spot welds with wide weld flanges and modest blank nesting. Change re-noses blanks for higher nesting utilisation and substitutes ~30% of spot welds with crash-durable epoxy structural adhesive plus reduced spot pitch. Physics: continuous bonded joints distribute load better than discrete spots, allowing narrower flanges and fewer welds at equal stiffness. Alternatives: full spot-weld baseline, laser-welded seams (heat/distortion), and self-pierce riveting (mixed-material only). Adhesive-hybrid chosen to cut weld count, flange scrap and distortion on the 48V mild-hybrid steel cradle.
ideaData.materialGrade: DP600/HSLA S420 stampings, crash-durable epoxy adhesive
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Retains stamping but improves nesting (utilisation ~70%→84%) and adds an adhesive-dispense station while removing ~35 spot welds. Alternatives: laser welding and SPR. Narrower flanges cut blank size and weld-fixture complexity; adhesive improves stiffness and sealing, reducing separate sealer. Weld-energy and electrode cost fall; minor tooling rework (~£180k).

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Adhesive-hybrid joining cuts welds, flange scrap and distortion.

ideaData.riskNotes: Adhesive cure robustness, e-coat oven compatibility and long-term bond durability are the risks; mitigated by crash-durable epoxy qualification, weld-bonding (spot+adhesive) for redundancy and accelerated humidity/thermal cycling. NVH improves from continuous bonded seams reducing buzz. Crash: bonded joints validated to UN R94 with weld-bond redundancy ensuring no peel failure. Durability over 300k-cycle road-load plus corrosion; premium refinement and structural integrity preserved.

title: Commonised wheel hub-bearing across front and rear corners
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Standardise one Gen-3 hub-bearing unit sized to the driven-front load case for use at all four corners, deleting three hub SKUs across the range. Applies across all powertrains. Benchmarked to BMW X7 corner module commonisation.

ideaData.title: Commonised wheel hub-bearing across front and rear corners
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline uses up to four hub-bearing variants (driven/non-driven, front/rear) with different bearing sizes and flange PCDs. Change standardises one Gen-3 flanged unit (52100/100Cr6 races, forged C45 flange, common PCD and bolt circle) sized to the worst-case driven-front load for all corners. Physics: the bounding driven-front L10 life envelope covers lighter rear and non-driven duty with margin, allowing one part everywhere. Alternatives: keep four variants (baseline), Gen-2 units (less integration), and tapered-roller (drag). Common Gen-3 chosen to delete SKUs across every powertrain corner while assuring bearing life.
ideaData.materialGrade: 100Cr6/52100 races, forged C45 flange, common PCD
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates four hub SKUs into one, deleting three part numbers and their gauging and logistics. Alternatives: Gen-2 and tapered-roller designs. One bearing line and one fixture serve all corners; volume on the single unit lowers piece price. Common PCD requires aligned knuckle/hub-carrier bores, a minor machining change.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One hub unit at all corners deletes three SKUs and adds volume scale.

ideaData.riskNotes: Worst-case sizing adds slight mass and cost at light rear corners; justified by SKU deletion, volume pricing and warranty simplification. Bearing L10 life bounded by driven-front duty covers all corners, validated by spectrum testing. NVH and brake feel preserved by controlled run-out. Crash hub retention validated per corner to UN R94. Durability over L10 plus 350k cycles at all corner spectra; premium quiet refinement and steering precision maintained.

title: BEV skateboard subframe-to-pack integral mounting deleting brackets
description: [BEV] Integrate front and rear subframe pack-mount interfaces directly into HPDC castings on a skateboard, deleting eight bolted bracket assemblies and standardising the pack-to-chassis bolt pattern. Cuts parts and improves pack stiffness. Benchmarked to NIO EL7 skateboard architecture.

ideaData.title: BEV skateboard subframe-to-pack integral mounting deleting brackets
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline mounts the battery pack to the skateboard via eight bolted bracket sub-assemblies welded to stamped subframes. Change integrates the pack-mount bosses directly into the front and rear AlSi10MnMg HPDC subframes and standardises one bolt pattern. Physics: casting the mount points into the structural subframe removes a bolted joint stack, raising pack-to-chassis stiffness and torsional rigidity while cutting hardware. Alternatives: bolted brackets (baseline), welded steel brackets on stamped cradle (parts/welds), and bonded mounts (service/peel). Integral cast bosses chosen to delete eight assemblies and standardise the pack interface across the skateboard.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC integral bosses / DP980 bracket baseline
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Casts mount bosses into the subframes, deleting eight bracket sub-assemblies, their welds and fasteners. Alternatives: welded steel brackets and bonded mounts. Removes a welding sub-line and a bolting station; standard bolt pattern enables one pack-mount fixture. CNC of boss faces to seal/locate tolerance added; net part and weld count fall sharply.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Casting mounts into the subframe deletes eight bracket assemblies.

ideaData.riskNotes: Cast boss fatigue, locating-plane flatness and serviceability of an integral mount are the risks; mitigated by FE-driven boss design, CNC of mating planes and a replaceable threaded insert for service. NVH improves from stiffer pack interface reducing structure-borne noise. Crash: pack retention in front/rear/side impact validated to UN R94/R95/R100. Durability over 500k-cycle multiaxial plus pack-load fatigue; premium torsional rigidity and refinement preserved.

title: ICE/MHEV upper control arm cast-Al to stamped-steel cost play
description: [ICE,MHEV] On cost-led ICE/48V trims, replace cast A356-T6 upper control arm with a stamped-welded DP780 fabricated arm where mass target is relaxed, cutting piece cost via cheaper material and high-utilisation stamping. Benchmarked to Lexus RX upper-arm variants.

ideaData.title: ICE/MHEV upper control arm cast-Al to stamped-steel cost play
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline upper control arm is A356-T6 cast aluminium chosen for mass on premium trims. Change offers a stamped-welded DP780 two-shell steel arm on cost-led ICE/48V trims where the unsprung-mass target is relaxed. Physics: a closed stamped steel section meets stiffness and fatigue at lower piece cost, trading ~0.9kg mass for material and process savings. Alternatives: A356-T6 cast (baseline), forged 6082-T6 (cost), and HPDC AlSi10MnMg (capex). Stamped DP780 chosen for lowest piece cost where the application tolerates the modest mass increase on mainstream variants.
ideaData.materialGrade: Stamped DP780 / A356-T6 cast baseline / forged 6082-T6
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces casting + machining with blanking, two-shell stamping and spot/MIG welding. Alternatives: forging and HPDC. High blank utilisation and cheap steel cut material cost; deletes the casting tool and machining ops. Stamping dies (~£280k) lower than casting tooling overall; e-coat replaces foundry finishing. Suited to high-volume cost trims only.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Stamped steel undercuts cast Al where mass target is relaxed.

ideaData.riskNotes: Added unsprung mass mildly affects ride and efficiency; mitigated by limiting to cost trims and retuning dampers/bushes. Steel arm corrosion managed by e-coat and sealed weld seams. Crash: arm retains wheel without intrusion, validated to UN R94. NVH and durability validated over 350k-cycle rig; premium handling on these trims preserved within target, with cast-Al retained on top trims for the lightest unsprung mass and sharpest dynamics.

title: Hollow-cast HPDC knuckle with salt-core for mass and cost
description: [BEV] Introduce hollow-section HPDC AlSi10MnMg front knuckle using lost salt-core to remove inert mass and material, cutting ~0.8kg below a solid casting while meeting strut and brake loads. Benchmarked to Li Auto L9 front corner lightweighting.

ideaData.title: Hollow-cast HPDC knuckle with salt-core for mass and cost
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline front knuckle is a solid-section AlSi10MnMg HPDC (or forged 6082-T6) carrying hub, caliper and strut loads. Change uses a hollow-section casting formed with a soluble salt-core (lost-core HPDC) to remove material from low-stress interiors, cutting ~0.8kg and Al consumption. Physics: thin-walled closed sections give high section modulus per mass, so hollowing the web retains stiffness while saving material. Alternatives: solid HPDC (baseline), forged 6082-T6 (heavier/cost), and A356-T6 low-pressure with sand core (slower). Salt-core HPDC chosen for the best mass-and-material outcome where loads permit hollow sections.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC, soluble salt-core / forged 6082-T6 / A356-T6
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces solid HPDC with lost-salt-core HPDC plus core wash-out, T7 and CNC. Alternatives: forging and sand-core low-pressure casting. Salt-core handling adds a core-set and dissolution step but removes ~15% Al mass and reduces material cost. Die (~£520k) and core tooling higher, offset by per-part Al saving at volume; bore X-ray sampling retained.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Hollow salt-core sections cut Al mass and material per knuckle.

ideaData.riskNotes: Salt-core positioning, wall-thickness control and fatigue at thin hollow sections are the risks; mitigated by core-print design, process monitoring, CT inspection and FE-driven wall mapping. NVH and steering precision preserved by stiff closed sections and tight bearing bore. Crash: knuckle shears predictably in side-pole, validated to FMVSS 214 and UN R94. Durability over 350k multiaxial cycles including pothole; premium handling and range benefit maintained, off-road exposure limited.

title: Dual-pinion EPS replaces hydraulic rack on luxury SUV
description: [ICE,MHEV] Replace engine-driven hydraulic power steering (pump, high-pressure hoses, fluid cooler) with a dual-pinion column-assist EPS. Eliminates parasitic pump drag (~0.3 l/100km), deletes hydraulic circuit BOM and fill-test station, enables lane-keep without separate actuator, and cuts warranty leak claims on a premium SUV platform.

ideaData.title: Dual-pinion EPS replaces hydraulic rack on luxury SUV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline is a belt-driven vane pump hydraulic rack with cooler and reservoir. The change adopts dual-pinion EPS: a second pinion drives a recirculating-ball nut via a brushless DC motor and ball-screw, sized for 12-13kN rack force. Housing options: AlSi10MnMg HPDC motor housing, 6082-T6 extruded rack tube, or AM60B Mg bracket for mass. Torsion bar sensor uses non-contact MR sensing. Pinion gears in 16MnCr5 case-hardened steel; ball-screw in 100Cr6. Deletes pump, lines, fluid, cooler. Enables software-defined assist curves and ADAS torque overlay, removing a standalone steering actuator on L2+ trims.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Process shifts from hydraulic line bending, crimping and hot-oil fill (HPDC housing, 6082-T6 extrusion, gear hobbing) to dry sub-assembly. Deletes the pressure-decay leak-test station and oil-fill cell, removing ~6 line minutes. BOM loses pump, two high-pressure hoses, cooler and 0.9 l fluid; adds ECU and motor. Assembly weld/joint count drops; end-of-line flush eliminated, cutting scrap.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Deleting the hydraulic circuit removes parts, fill process and leak warranty.

ideaData.riskNotes: Premium steering feel must be preserved: torque ripple from the BLDC motor is masked via 12-pole sinusoidal commutation and active damping, keeping on-centre feel and off-road kickback control comparable to hydraulic. Rack force capacity validated for full-lock manoeuvres at GVW on 21in tyres. Functional safety to ISO 26262 ASIL-D with redundant sensing preserves fail-operational assist; NVH benchmarked against the hydraulic baseline for refinement.

title: APA rack-parallel EPS commonised across SUV variants
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] Standardise an axle-parallel (APA) belt-drive EPS across short- and long-wheelbase SUV variants by sharing rack tube, motor and ECU, tuning only via software calibration. Cuts unique part numbers, consolidates tooling and supplier volume, and reduces validation spend while preserving variant-specific steering feel through parameter sets.

ideaData.title: APA rack-parallel EPS commonised across SUV variants
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline runs three rack variants with bespoke motors. The change defines one APA EPS: belt and ball-screw transmit motor torque parallel to the rack, with shared 6082-T6 rack tube, AlSi10MnMg HPDC motor housing and a single ECU. Differentiation moves to firmware: assist maps, friction compensation and return-to-centre tables. Alternative housings considered: AM60B Mg for -0.4kg, or A356-T6 low-pressure cast for fatigue margin. Ball-screw 100Cr6, belt HTD carbon-cord. Rack force capped at 14kN covers PHEV front-axle mass. One sensor cluster (dual MR) spans all variants for ASIL-D.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6082-T6 Al / AlSi10MnMg HPDC / A356-T6 low-pressure cast
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates three machining programmes into one extrusion-plus-HPDC line; alternatives A356-T6 low-pressure casting or AM60B Mg die-casting evaluated for the housing. Single belt-tension and ball-screw press-fit cell replaces three. Unique part numbers drop from 11 to 4, shrinking tooling spend and slashing PPAP and crash re-validation. Assembly fixtures commonised; supplier volume leverage lowers piece price.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One rack across variants drives tooling amortisation and volume price.

ideaData.riskNotes: Long-wheelbase variant has higher front-axle load and steering inertia; calibration must restore its heavier, more deliberate premium feel versus the agile short-wheelbase tune, validated on-road for on-centre build-up and parking effort. Belt-drive APA adds NVH risk under cold-start; carbon-cord belt and tuned tensioner suppress whine. Durability validated to 1.5M cycles at max rack force; off-road impact loads checked for ball-screw brinelling.

title: Steer-by-wire feedback actuator cost-down for BEV SUV
description: [BEV] Re-architect the steer-by-wire hand-wheel feedback actuator using a single-stage planetary and integrated MR sensing to delete the mechanical backup column on a BEV SUV. Removes intermediate shaft, U-joints and firewall seal, freeing crash and packaging space while cutting hardware via dual-redundant electronics instead of mechanical fallback.

ideaData.title: Steer-by-wire feedback actuator cost-down for BEV SUV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline steer-by-wire retains a clutched mechanical backup column. The change qualifies a fully decoupled system: a road-wheel actuator (RWA) and a hand-wheel feedback unit (HWA) with single-stage planetary in 16MnCr5 case-hardened gears, AlSi10MnMg HPDC housing, and dual three-phase BLDC windings for fail-operational torque. Sensing uses redundant non-contact MR position sensors. Deleting the column allows a 7075-T6 collapsible support replaced by a lighter 6014-T4 trim bracket. Alternative housings: AM60B Mg or A380 die-cast. ASIL-D with two power supplies and two ECUs; no clutch, no intermediate shaft, no firewall pass-through seal.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Removes column assembly: intermediate-shaft swaging, U-joint press, firewall grommet seal and clutch sub-assembly. Housing via HPDC; alternatives A380 die-cast or AM60B Mg. Adds redundant wiring harness and second ECU. Net part count down ~14; weld/seal joints at firewall eliminated, improving sealing quality and cutting NVH foam. Assembly de-risked by removing precise column-to-rack alignment.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Deleting mechanical backup column removes hardware and packaging cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Removing mechanical fallback demands proven fail-operational architecture: dual windings, dual ECUs, dual power nets and dual sensing sustain steering after any single fault, validated to ISO 26262 ASIL-D and UN/ECE R79 SbW provisions. Premium feel synthesised via high-fidelity torque maps reproducing on-centre build-up, road texture and end-stop. Crash energy management revalidated without column collapse; redundancy fault-injection tested. Off-road kickback rendered for haptic authenticity.
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN/ECE R79 steer-by-wire amendment, ISO 26262 ASIL-D

title: Brake-by-wire one-box integrated braking for BEV SUV
description: [BEV] Replace vacuum booster plus separate ESC unit with a one-box brake-by-wire actuator integrating master cylinder, motor-driven pressure source and ABS/ESC. Deletes the vacuum pump (no engine source on BEV), shortens hydraulic routing, and enables seamless regen blending up to 0.3g without pad drag, cutting parts and energy.

ideaData.title: Brake-by-wire one-box integrated braking for BEV SUV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline pairs a vacuum booster, electric vacuum pump and standalone ESC modulator. The change is a one-box integrated brake control: a BLDC motor drives a ball-screw onto the master-cylinder piston, with integrated ABS/ESC valve block in A380 die-cast aluminium housing, AlSi10MnMg HPDC pump body, and 100Cr6 ball-screw. Pedal feel is decoupled via a pedal-feel simulator with progressive spring and damping. Blends regen (axle e-motor) and friction so the driver sees one deceleration curve. Alternatives: AlSi9Cu3 die-cast block or machined 6082-T6 manifold. Dual-ECU ASIL-D; brake fluid DOT4 LV; deletes vacuum pump and its harness.
ideaData.materialGrade: A380 die-cast Al / AlSi10MnMg HPDC / 100Cr6 ball-screw
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates booster, vacuum pump and ESC into one HPDC/die-cast unit; alternatives AlSi9Cu3 die-casting or 6082-T6 machined manifold. Deletes vacuum-line routing and pump mount weld studs. Single vacuum-free fill-and-bleed cycle replaces two. BOM loses pump, check valve, vacuum hose and sensor; part count down ~18. Tooling consolidated to one valve-block casting die; assembly stations reduced by two.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Integrating booster, pump and ESC removes parts and a fill process.

ideaData.riskNotes: Blended braking must be transparent: pressure source and regen torque arbitration hand over without pedal-feel discontinuity at the regen-to-friction transition, validated for split-mu, ABS and trailer modes. Pedal-feel simulator tuned to premium firm-progressive character. Fail-operational backup via direct hydraulic push-through on total electrical loss meets UN/ECE R13-H and Euro NCAP AEB. Thermal and durability validated for 21in brakes at GVW on long descents; ASIL-D fault handling.
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN/ECE R13-H, Euro NCAP AEB, ISO 26262 ASIL-D

title: Electromechanical rear brakes delete rear hydraulics on BEV
description: [BEV] Fit dry electromechanical brakes (EMB) at the rear axle, deleting rear calipers' hydraulic lines, EPB motors and fluid volume. With strong rear regen on a BEV SUV, friction demand is low; EMB enables faster, individually modulated clamp force and removes the integrated parking-brake actuator, simplifying corner modules.

ideaData.title: Electromechanical rear brakes delete rear hydraulics on BEV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline rear corners use hydraulic floating calipers with separate EPB spindle motors. The change adopts dry EMB: a BLDC motor and planetary plus ball-ramp/ball-screw generate clamp force on the rear pads with integrated parking latch. Caliper body in A356-T6 low-pressure cast, motor housing AlSi10MnMg HPDC, ball-screw 100Cr6, gears 16MnCr5. Rear hydraulic lines, EPB motors and one-third of fluid volume deleted; front axle stays hydraulic for capacity. Alternatives: forged 6082-T6 caliper bracket, AM60B Mg motor cover. CAN/FlexRay clamp-force control, ASIL-D, with mechanical self-lock for park.
ideaData.materialGrade: A356-T6 cast Al / AlSi10MnMg HPDC / 100Cr6 ball-screw
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces rear hydraulic line bending/flaring and EPB motor press with electrical connector assembly. Caliper via low-pressure casting; alternatives 6082-T6 forging or A380 die-cast bracket. Deletes rear brake-line fill/bleed at the corner, simplifying EOL fill to front circuit only. BOM removes rear lines, unions, EPB actuators; adds EMB motor module. Corner-module pre-assembly improves at supplier, cutting line stations.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Dry rear corners delete hydraulics, EPB motors and fill labour.

ideaData.riskNotes: Rear EMB must guarantee fail-safe clamp and park retention without hydraulics: dual power supplies, energy-buffer cap for last-clamp, and mechanical self-locking ramp hold the vehicle on a 30% grade at GVW. Thermal derating managed since BEV regen carries most rear duty, limiting EMB energy. Dynamic stability preserved by fast individual clamp modulation for ESC/yaw. Validated to UN/ECE R13-H park and dynamic; off-road wading sealing IP6K9K; ASIL-D fault handling.

title: Regen-blending recalibration cuts MHEV rear pad wear
description: [MHEV] Retune the 48V regen-friction blending map to shift more low-g deceleration to the belt-starter-generator, downsizing rear brake rotor diameter and pad area on an MHEV SUV. Lower thermal duty allows thinner rotors and cheaper pad compound while preserving fade resistance for towing and descents.

ideaData.title: Regen-blending recalibration cuts MHEV rear pad wear
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: MHEV. Baseline 48V BSG provides modest coast regen but friction handles most braking, sizing rear rotors for full duty. The change recalibrates the blending algorithm to extend BSG regen torque into the 0-0.2g band, reducing rear friction energy ~25%. This permits a smaller-diameter grey-iron rotor (GG20/GG25 flake graphite) or a thinner vented disc, and a lower-cost NAO pad versus low-met. Alternatives for further mass: aluminium-MMC rotor or ADC12 hat with iron friction ring. Calibration only at front; rear hardware downsized. ESC blending arbitration ensures smooth handover.
ideaData.materialGrade: GG25 grey iron rotor / NAO friction pad / ADC12 hat (option)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Primarily a calibration change (no new tooling for software) plus a downsized cast-iron rotor: alternative processes are MMC casting or bi-metal ADC12 hat assembly versus monobloc grey-iron casting. Smaller rotor reduces machining time and raw iron mass. Pad compound change is drop-in. BOM cost falls on rotor and pad; no assembly station change. Validation cost dominates, not tooling.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Shifting low-g braking to regen downsizes rear friction hardware.

ideaData.riskNotes: Reduced rear rotor mass lowers thermal capacity, so towing and Alpine descent duty cycles are revalidated for fade and judder; friction stays predominantly front-biased, preserving stability. NAO pad maintains cold-bite and low-noise premium refinement; rotor coning checked at GVW. Regen-to-friction handover tuned to avoid pedal-feel step at low speed when BSG drops out near standstill. Validated to UN/ECE R13-H fade type-I/II and Euro NCAP.

title: Flow-formed alloy wheel replaces low-pressure cast
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Convert 21in low-pressure cast alloy wheels to flow-formed (rim-rolled) construction. Work-hardening the spun rim raises strength, allowing thinner sections that cut ~2.5kg per wheel of unsprung mass, lowering material cost per wheel and improving ride and efficiency across the SUV range.

ideaData.title: Flow-formed alloy wheel replaces low-pressure cast
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline is a fully low-pressure cast A356-T6 wheel with thick rim for porosity margin. The change low-pressure casts only the centre, then flow-forms (spins) the rim under heated rollers, refining grain and raising yield strength ~25%, enabling thinner barrel walls. Alloys: A356.2-T6, A357-T6 for higher elongation, or 6061-T6 in a fully forged alternative. Per-wheel mass drops ~2.5kg unsprung. Surface finish ready for diamond-cut or paint. Alternatives compared: 2-piece bolted rim and rotary-forged monobloc. Lower aluminium content per wheel offsets added spin-forming cycle time.
ideaData.materialGrade: A356.2-T6 cast + flow-formed rim / A357-T6 / 6061-T6 (forged option)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Adds a flow-forming spin station after low-pressure casting; alternatives are full forging or 2-piece bolted construction versus monobloc casting. Thinner rim cuts aluminium mass ~12% per wheel and shortens machining. Heat-treat and diamond-cut unchanged. Tooling adds spin rollers but reuses casting dies. Scrap from porosity falls as the rim is wrought, improving X-ray pass rate and reducing rework.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Work-hardened thinner rim cuts aluminium mass and cost per wheel.

ideaData.riskNotes: Thinner flow-formed rim must pass radial fatigue, impact (kerb-strike) and 13-degree cornering-fatigue per regulation at full SUV load rating; work-hardening provides the margin. Unsprung mass reduction sharpens turn-in and improves secondary ride, a premium dynamic benefit, while bead-seat integrity and air retention are validated. Off-road kerb and pothole impact energy checked; corrosion of thinner section managed by coating. Balance and runout held for refinement at high speed.
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN/ECE R124 wheels, GB/T impact & fatigue

title: Rotary-forged monobloc wheel for flagship BEV SUV
description: [BEV] Adopt rotary-forged (forged-then-spun) 22in monobloc wheels on the flagship BEV SUV to recover range-critical unsprung mass. Forged grain flow plus rim spinning cuts ~3kg per wheel versus cast, reducing rotational inertia and aero-cover dependence while consolidating to one premium wheel process.

ideaData.title: Rotary-forged monobloc wheel for flagship BEV SUV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline gravity- or low-pressure-cast 22in wheel is heavy, eroding range. The change forges a 6061-T6 billet preform then rotary-spins the rim, giving continuous grain flow and ~30% higher fatigue strength, allowing the lightest compliant section. Alloys: 6061-T6, 6082-T6, or 7050-T74 for peak strength. Per-wheel mass falls ~3kg, cutting unsprung and rotational inertia for measurable WLTP range gain. Alternatives: flow-formed cast and 2-piece forged. Aero-optimised spoke geometry reduces add-on plastic aero covers. Machined and diamond-cut finish; load rating sized for >2.8t GVW BEV.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Process moves from casting to forging plus rotary spin-forming; alternatives are 2-piece forged bolted or flow-formed cast versus gravity casting.
