Higher billet and press cost is offset by ~15% mass cut and deleted aero covers. Forging die and spin tooling add capital but raise yield (no porosity scrap). Machining time rises slightly for forged hardness; finishing unchanged. Consolidates premium tiers to one process.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Forged-spun mass cut adds range and deletes aero covers.

ideaData.riskNotes: Lightest section must still clear radial-fatigue, impact and cornering-fatigue at heavy BEV GVW; forged grain flow supplies durability margin. Reduced unsprung and rotational inertia improve ride isolation and acceleration feel, premium attributes, while bead retention and runtflat-ready bead seat are validated. Range claim verified on WLTP with the lighter rotating mass. Pothole/kerb impact energy and corrosion of machined surfaces checked; NVH balance held at autobahn speed.

title: 2-piece bolted wheel for repairable premium SUV rims
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Offer 22in 2-piece bolted wheels (forged centre, spun rim) on accessory and large-diameter trims so a kerbed rim is replaced as a half, not a whole wheel. Cuts warranty and accessory cost, allows mix-and-match finishes, and consolidates centre castings across rim widths.

ideaData.title: 2-piece bolted wheel for repairable premium SUV rims
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline monobloc wheels scrap the whole part on rim damage. The change bolts a forged 6082-T6 centre to a flow-formed 6061-T6 outer rim with a sealed bead and through-bolts in 10.9 zinc-flake fasteners. One centre design serves multiple rim widths and offsets, and finishes can differ between centre and rim. Alternatives: monobloc forged, low-pressure cast, or 3-piece for accessory tier. Rim-only replacement after kerb damage cuts service cost. Bolt-circle and bead seal validated for air retention; load rating sized to SUV GVW.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6082-T6 forged centre / 6061-T6 spun rim / 10.9 zinc-flake bolts
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Splits manufacturing into forged centre plus flow-formed rim joined by a bolt-and-seal station; alternatives monobloc forging or low-pressure casting. Adds assembly and leak-test versus a one-piece wheel, but commonises the centre casting across widths, cutting unique tooling. Service replaces a rim half, slashing accessory and warranty BOM. Sealant application and torque-to-yield bolting add line steps offset by tooling reuse.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Rim-half replacement and common centres cut warranty and tooling.

ideaData.riskNotes: Bolted joint and bead seal must hold air and survive fatigue at SUV GVW: the through-bolt interface is the critical fatigue and leak path, validated by cornering- and radial-fatigue plus pressure-decay leak test. Premium appearance preserved with mixed finishes; corrosion at the dissimilar-finish interface managed by sealant and coating. Kerb-strike replaceability confirmed; bolt-torque retention and re-use limits specified for service. Balance/runout held after rim replacement.

title: Low-rolling-resistance tyre spec for PHEV SUV efficiency
description: [PHEV] Specify a low-rolling-resistance (LRR) tyre with silica-rich tread and lighter casing for the PHEV SUV to extend electric range and cut CO2/CAFE exposure. Reduced rolling resistance lowers energy use; lighter construction trims unsprung mass while preserving wet grip via functionalised silica compound.

ideaData.title: Low-rolling-resistance tyre spec for PHEV SUV efficiency
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline tyre uses a grip-biased carbon-black tread with heavy reinforced casing for load. The change specifies a silica-rich, functionalised-silane tread cutting hysteresis ~20% (RR label class improvement), a lighter high-tensile steel belt and aramid cap-ply, and an optimised contour reducing casing mass. Compounds: S-SBR/silica tread, NR/BR sidewall, aramid vs nylon cap-ply alternatives. Range gain reduces battery cycling. HL-rated load index retained for PHEV kerb mass. Alternatives: foam-acoustic LRR and self-seal LRR variants. Wet grip preserved by silica dispersion chemistry; tread depth and wear validated.
ideaData.materialGrade: S-SBR/silica tread / aramid cap-ply / high-tensile steel belt
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Tyre build process unchanged (extrusion, calendering, building drum, curing) but compound and cap-ply material swap; alternative processes include foam-acoustic insertion or self-seal layer application versus standard build. Lighter casing cuts rubber and steel mass per tyre. No OEM tooling change; supplier compound qualification dominates. BOM and logistics improve slightly via lower mass; range benefit reduces fuel/charge cost claims.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Lower hysteresis and lighter casing cut energy and rubber mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: Cutting rolling resistance must not erode wet braking or aquaplaning margin, the key safety trade-off: functionalised silica restores wet grip while lowering hysteresis, validated to UN/ECE R117 wet-grip and RR labelling. Lighter casing checked for load durability at PHEV GVW and high-speed (HL) rating. Premium ride and low cabin noise preserved with tuned tread pattern; off-road and snow not targeted for this trim. Wear life confirmed to avoid warranty exposure.
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN/ECE R117 rolling resistance & wet grip

title: Foam-acoustic tyre deletes Helmholtz wheel resonator on BEV
description: [BEV] Use polyurethane foam-acoustic tyres to absorb cavity resonance, allowing deletion of the bolt-on Helmholtz resonators in the alloy wheel rim. On a quiet BEV SUV where road noise dominates, in-tyre foam cuts the 200-250Hz boom and removes wheel-side hardware and assembly complexity.

ideaData.title: Foam-acoustic tyre deletes Helmholtz wheel resonator on BEV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline suppresses tyre-cavity resonance with tuned Helmholtz resonators riveted into the wheel barrel plus standard tyres. The change bonds a contoured open-cell polyurethane foam ring inside the tyre, absorbing the ~220Hz cavity mode, so the wheel-mounted resonators are deleted. Wheel reverts to a plain A356-T6 or 6061-T6 forged barrel. Alternatives: dual-mass damper, increased liner mass, or active road-noise cancellation. Foam adds ~0.5kg/tyre but removes resonator hardware and the rim machining/bonding for it. Balance and high-speed durability of the foam ring validated; quiet BEV cabin benefits most.
ideaData.materialGrade: PU open-cell foam liner / A356-T6 or 6061-T6 plain barrel
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Adds an in-tyre foam-bonding step at the tyre supplier but deletes the wheel-side resonator riveting and the rim's resonator-mounting machining; alternative processes are rim-cast resonator pockets or active noise cancellation. Wheel barrel simplifies, cutting machining time and unique rim tooling. BOM removes resonator hardware; tyre BOM adds low-cost foam. Net assembly stations on the wheel line reduced.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: In-tyre foam replaces wheel resonators, simplifying the rim.

ideaData.riskNotes: Foam ring must survive high-speed heat and centrifugal load without delamination, the main durability risk, validated to UN/ECE R30 endurance/high-speed; balance and uniformity held for refinement. Cavity-noise reduction verified to match or beat the deleted resonators in the 200-250Hz band, preserving the premium quiet BEV cabin. Repairability (puncture/plug) and run-flat compatibility assessed. Added rotating mass ~0.5kg checked against ride and range; no off-road sealing impact.

title: Indirect TPMS via wheel-speed deletes direct sensors
description: [ICE,MHEV] Replace four battery-powered direct TPMS sensors with indirect TPMS using ABS wheel-speed plus spectral vibration analysis on volume ICE/MHEV SUV trims. Deletes valve-mounted sensor modules, their batteries and the RF receiver, removing service replacement cost and a recurring warranty driver.

ideaData.title: Indirect TPMS via wheel-speed deletes direct sensors
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline uses four direct TPMS modules (MEMS pressure sensor, lithium cell, RF transmitter) on the valve and an RF receiver. The change implements indirect TPMS (iTPMS): the ESC ECU detects deflation via rolling-radius change in wheel-speed ratios plus tyre-natural-frequency spectral analysis, flagging low pressure to UN/ECE R64. Deletes sensor modules, batteries, RF receiver and valve electronics; valve reverts to a standard rubber/snap-in. Alternatives: keep direct for run-flat/high trims, or hybrid. No new hardware; software in existing ABS/ESC ECU. Calibrated per tyre size for detection sensitivity.
ideaData.materialGrade: n/a software TPMS / standard snap-in valve / EPDM seal
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes four electronic sensor modules and the RF receiver from BOM and the sensor-pairing/relearn station at EOL; alternative is partial retention for run-flat trims. Valve assembly reverts to a simple snap-in rubber valve, simplifying tyre mounting. No new tooling; ESC software adds calibration. Service no longer replaces sensors at tyre change, cutting parts logistics and warranty.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Software TPMS deletes four sensor modules and the receiver.

ideaData.riskNotes: Indirect TPMS must meet UN/ECE R64 detection thresholds for single- and multi-tyre deflation, the key compliance risk, including the slow-leak case via natural-frequency analysis; sensitivity revalidated for SUV mass, large rims and varied loads/trailers. No individual pressure readout is a feature trade-off; reset-after-inflation procedure defined. Premium trims with run-flats may retain direct sensors. Off-road low-pressure airing-down handled by driver reset; no safety regression at GVW.

title: Single-piston floating front caliper from forged aluminium
description: [ICE,MHEV] Replace a heavy cast-iron front fixed caliper on lower SUV trims with a forged-aluminium twin-piston floating caliper. Forging cuts ~1.8kg unsprung per corner, removes machining of fixed-caliper bridges, and lowers material cost while retaining stopping power for non-performance trims.

ideaData.title: Single-piston floating front caliper from forged aluminium
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline lower trims carry an iron fixed caliper sized like the performance trim. The change fits a forged 6082-T6 aluminium floating caliper with a sliding-pin guide and twin pistons, matched to a vented GG25 rotor. Forged aluminium cuts ~1.8kg/corner and resists corrosion. Alternatives: A356-T6 cast caliper, AlSi10MnMg HPDC bracket, or composite-piston phenolic versus steel piston. Anti-rattle clips and ceramic shims preserve NVH. Guide pins in 1.0727 with PTFE boots. Sized for non-performance braking energy; performance trims keep the fixed monobloc.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6082-T6 forged Al caliper / GG25 rotor / phenolic piston (option)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Process shifts from iron casting plus heavy machining of a fixed bridge to aluminium forging with lighter machining; alternatives A356-T6 casting or AlSi10MnMg HPDC for the bracket. Floating design halves piston count and bore machining. BOM cost drops on raw material and machining minutes. Bracket and guide-pin sub-assembly adds a small step; corrosion e-coat simplified for aluminium.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Forged-aluminium floating caliper cuts mass, pistons and machining.

ideaData.riskNotes: Floating caliper must avoid taper-wear and pull, the main feel/durability risk: guide-pin friction and seal roll-back tuned for even pad wear and crisp pedal. Aluminium body thermal capacity validated for trailer and descent duty on this trim; not applied to performance variants needing fixed-caliper rigidity. NVH (groan, judder) controlled by shims and chamfered pads. Corrosion of guide pins sealed; stopping distance meets UN/ECE R13-H at GVW. Unsprung-mass cut aids ride.

evidenceSources.title: Forged Al floating caliper mass study

title: Aluminium-MMC front rotor cuts unsprung mass on BEV
description: [BEV] Replace grey-iron front rotors with aluminium metal-matrix-composite (AlSiC) rotors on the BEV SUV where regen carries most braking energy. Low friction duty makes the lighter MMC rotor viable, cutting ~4kg unsprung per axle, reducing corrosion bloom on rarely-used rotors and aiding range.

ideaData.title: Aluminium-MMC front rotor cuts unsprung mass on BEV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline grey-iron GG25 rotors are heavy and rust-bloom from low usage under strong regen. The change uses an aluminium MMC (A359 + 20% SiC particulate, AlSiC) rotor, optionally with a hard ceramic-sprayed friction surface, exploiting the low BEV friction duty. Mass falls ~4kg/axle, improving range and ride. Alternatives: carbon-ceramic (C/SiC) for flagship, ADC12 bi-metal hat with iron ring, or coated grey iron. Pad compound matched to the MMC surface to control wear. Thermal capacity sized for the rare high-energy event (failed regen, long descent) with derating logic.
ideaData.materialGrade: A359/SiC MMC rotor / C/SiC (flagship option) / coated GG25
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Process moves from grey-iron casting to MMC squeeze/stir casting plus surface coating; alternatives carbon-ceramic CVI or bi-metal ADC12 hat assembly. MMC casting and coating raise piece cost but mass and corrosion-warranty savings plus range value offset. Machining of MMC needs PCD tooling. BOM swaps rotor; pad compound change drop-in. Validation and coating are the cost drivers, not tooling volume.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Light MMC rotor cuts unsprung mass and corrosion warranty on BEV.

ideaData.riskNotes: MMC rotor thermal limit is the critical risk: under regen-loss or sustained descent it must not exceed aluminium softening, managed by a coated friction surface, oversized vent and brake-temperature-aware regen-derating that keeps friction energy bounded. Stopping distance and fade validated to UN/ECE R13-H for the worst case at GVW. Reduced corrosion bloom improves premium appearance and feel; judder/NVH with the matched pad checked. Off-road wading and grit abrasion of the coating assessed.

evidenceSources.title: AlSiC MMC brake rotor with coating
BEV brake duty and rotor sizing

title: Variable-ratio EPS pinion deletes rear-wheel-steer on trim
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] Use a progressive variable-ratio rack and tuned EPS to deliver low-speed agility on mid SUV trims, removing the rear-wheel-steer actuator from those trims. Variable pinion geometry shrinks the turning circle enough to drop the costly rear-axle steering hardware where it is not a performance differentiator.

ideaData.title: Variable-ratio EPS pinion deletes rear-wheel-steer on trim
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline mid trims inherit rear-wheel-steer (RWS) with its actuator, ECU and rear toe links from the flagship. The change cuts the front rack with a progressive variable-ratio tooth profile (quicker off-centre) and a stronger EPS motor, improving low-speed manoeuvrability so RWS can be deleted on mid trims. Rack in 16MnCr5 induction-hardened with variable pitch; EPS motor uprated; ECU calibrated. Alternatives keep RWS on flagship only. Deletes rear actuator (AlSi10MnMg housing, ball-screw, ECU) and rear sub-link. Turning circle target met within ~0.4m of the RWS car.
ideaData.materialGrade: 16MnCr5 variable-ratio rack / AlSi10MnMg (deleted actuator) / uprated EPS
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Rack machining adopts a variable-pitch tooth profile (gear shaping/hobbing) but deletes the entire rear-steer actuator sub-assembly and its harness; alternatives retain RWS only on flagship. BOM removes rear motor, ball-screw, ECU and rear toe-link hardware. Assembly deletes a rear-axle station. Front rack tooling updated; net part count and weld/fastener count fall significantly on mid trims.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Variable-ratio front steering removes rear-steer hardware on mid trims.

ideaData.riskNotes: Deleting RWS must not regress low-speed agility or high-speed stability that premium buyers expect: variable-ratio geometry restores turning circle and parking ease, while stability-control tuning maintains lane-change confidence at speed, validated on-road. Steering effort and on-centre feel re-tuned for the quicker ratio to avoid nervousness. Flagship retains RWS as differentiator. Off-road low-speed crawl steering checked; ISO 26262 and UN/ECE R79 unaffected by the simpler architecture.

title: Hollow forged steering tie-rods trim front unsprung mass
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace solid steel tie-rods and steering arms with hollow forged or tube-formed designs. Removing mass from steered, unsprung components sharpens response and cuts steel content per vehicle while maintaining buckling and fatigue strength through closed-section geometry.

ideaData.title: Hollow forged steering tie-rods trim front unsprung mass
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline tie-rods and inner/outer links are solid 41Cr4/C45 steel. The change uses hollow forged or internally-high-pressure-formed (IHU) tubes in 34MnB5/26MnB5 with upset ends for the ball-joint threads, cutting ~30% mass at equal buckling resistance. Alternatives: forged 6082-T6 aluminium link, tailor-wall tube, or seamless DOM tube. Ball-joint housings stay 16MnCr5. Closed section preserves column buckling and lateral fatigue. Steering arm optionally aluminium-forged. Mass off the steered axle improves on-centre response and ride. Threads rolled, not cut, for fatigue.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Process moves from solid bar forging/machining to hollow forging or internal-high-pressure forming with end-upsetting; alternatives DOM tube with welded ends or aluminium forging. Less steel mass per part; rolled threads add fatigue life. Tooling for IHU/hollow forging is new capital, offset by material savings at volume. Machining minutes drop. Joint press-fit and boot assembly unchanged.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Hollow steered links cut unsprung mass at equal strength.

ideaData.riskNotes: Closed-section links must match solid-bar buckling under full-lock kerb-impact loads, the governing failure mode, validated by axial buckling, lateral fatigue and corner-impact testing at SUV GVW. Ball-joint upset ends checked for wall thinning and thread fatigue. Reduced steered mass sharpens turn-in and secondary ride, premium benefits, without adding kickback. Corrosion of internal tube surface coated/sealed. Off-road rock-strike and articulation loads verified; no NVH penalty from hollow ring modes.

title: Common brake corner module across SUV wheelbase variants
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Standardise a single front and rear brake corner module (caliper, rotor diameter, knuckle interface) across short- and long-wheelbase and ICE-to-BEV SUV derivatives. Commonising clamp hardware and rotor part numbers consolidates supplier tooling and volume, with brake force tuned by software and pad compound.

ideaData.title: Common brake corner module across SUV wheelbase variants
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline carries distinct caliper/rotor combos per wheelbase and powertrain mass. The change defines one over-specified corner module: a forged 6082-T6 caliper, a single vented GG25 rotor diameter, and a common knuckle bolt pattern, with differentiation by pad compound (NAO/low-met/ceramic) and ABS/EBD calibration. Heavier BEV/long-wheelbase variants use higher-mu pads and software bias; lighter ICE uses economy pads. Alternatives: two rotor diameters max, or aluminium-hat bi-metal rotor. Consolidates part numbers across the SUV family, maximising tooling amortisation and purchasing leverage.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6082-T6 forged caliper / GG25 common rotor / tiered pad compounds
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Collapses multiple caliper and rotor castings/forgings into one each, slashing unique tooling and PPAP; alternative limits to two rotor sizes. Knuckle machining commonised to one bolt pattern. Differentiation via pad and software needs no hardware tooling. Assembly fixtures and torque programmes unified; supplier volume per part number rises sharply, lowering piece price and inventory complexity.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One corner module across variants maximises tooling and volume leverage.

ideaData.riskNotes: Over-specifying for the heaviest variant risks excess mass on light trims, accepted for commonisation; braking performance per variant is tuned by pad mu and ABS/EBD bias, validated to UN/ECE R13-H stopping and fade across the GVW range including BEV and towing. Pedal feel and NVH harmonised so all variants feel premium despite shared hardware. Thermal margin sized for worst-case descent. Off-road and trailer modes verified; corner-module sealing common (IP rating) across builds.

evidenceSources.title: GLE vs GLS brake hardware comparison

title: Brake-by-wire pedal-feel simulator commonised steer/brake ECU
description: [BEV] Integrate the brake-by-wire pedal-feel simulator control and steer-by-wire feedback control into one redundant chassis-motion domain controller on the BEV SUV. Sharing ASIL-D compute, power supplies and housing across steer and brake-by-wire deletes a duplicate ECU and harness, cutting cost and wiring mass.

ideaData.title: Brake-by-wire pedal-feel simulator commonised steer/brake ECU
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline runs separate ECUs for brake-by-wire (pedal simulator, pressure source) and steer-by-wire (feedback unit, RWA). The change consolidates motion control into one redundant chassis domain controller with two lockstep MCUs, dual power inputs and one AlSi10MnMg HPDC housing, hosting both pedal-feel and steering-feel synthesis. Alternatives: A380 die-cast housing, or zonal-ECU hosting. Deletes one ECU, one connector set and shared harness runs. Pedal-feel simulator uses a progressive spring/MR damper; steering feel via torque maps. ASIL-D fail-operational with partitioned software for steer and brake.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC ECU housing / A380 die-cast (option)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Merges two ECU housings into one die-cast/HPDC unit and one wiring connector family; alternatives A380 die-casting or zonal integration. Deletes a controller, its bracket and a harness branch, cutting assembly clips and routing time. Conformal-coat and seal one box not two. BOM removes duplicate compute and connectors; software partitioning adds verification cost, not tooling.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One redundant motion ECU replaces separate steer and brake controllers.

ideaData.riskNotes: Co-hosting steer- and brake-by-wire on one controller raises common-cause-failure risk, the central safety concern: freedom-from-interference via hardware partitioning, lockstep cores, dual power nets and independent watchdogs sustains fail-operational steering and braking to ISO 26262 ASIL-D and UN/ECE R79/R13-H. Pedal and steering feel synthesised independently to preserve premium character. Thermal load of combined compute managed; fault-injection and FMEDA validate isolation. EMC and sealing of the single box verified.

title: Regen-braking tuning downsizes MHEV front rotor thickness
description: [MHEV] Optimise the 48V regen-braking torque ramp and one-pedal-lite mapping to reduce front friction thermal duty, enabling a thinner vented front rotor and lighter pad backplate on the MHEV SUV. Lower friction energy preserves fade margin while trimming iron mass and pad cost.

ideaData.title: Regen-braking tuning downsizes MHEV front rotor thickness
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: MHEV. Baseline 48V regen is conservative, leaving the front rotor sized for full friction duty. The change tunes the BSG regen torque ramp and adds a lift-off 'coast-capture' map, raising recuperated energy and cutting front friction duty ~15%. This allows a reduced rotor effective thickness (GG25 vented) and a thinner steel pad backplate, or an aluminium-hat bi-metal rotor. Alternatives: smaller rotor diameter, MMC rotor, or NAO pad swap. Mostly software with a downsized cast rotor. ESC blending keeps pedal feel linear and handover smooth near standstill.
ideaData.materialGrade: GG25 thinner vented rotor / bi-metal ADC12 hat / NAO pad
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Mainly a calibration change plus a thinner cast-iron rotor: alternative processes are bi-metal aluminium-hat assembly or MMC casting versus monobloc grey iron. Thinner rotor cuts iron mass and grinding stock. Lighter backplate is a drop-in stamping change. No new EOL station; supplier rotor tooling adjusted for thickness. Validation, not tooling, dominates cost.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: More front regen lets the rotor and backplate shed mass and cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Thinner rotor reduces thermal mass, so fade, judder (DTV growth) and Alpine-descent duty are revalidated; front bias and stability are preserved since regen supplements rather than replaces friction. Coast-capture mapping tuned to avoid intrusive deceleration that would hurt premium drivability, with smooth regen-off handover near standstill. Towing and high-speed-stop duty checked at GVW. Meets UN/ECE R13-H fade type-I/II; NVH (cold groan) controlled by pad compound.

evidenceSources.title: MHEV regen energy and rotor duty

title: Hydraulic-rack delete on BEV via 12V-source EPS pump-off
description: [BEV] On the BEV SUV, replace a carried-over electro-hydraulic power steering (EHPS) pump pack with a pure EPS rack, deleting the electric pump, reservoir and cooler. EHPS made sense only on ICE carry-over; the BEV with high-voltage architecture is better served by a direct-drive EPS, cutting parts and a fluid-fill process.

ideaData.title: Hydraulic-rack delete on BEV via 12V-source EPS pump-off
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline is an electro-hydraulic rack (electric pump, reservoir, cooler, lines) carried over from the ICE donor. The change fits a rack-parallel EPS: BLDC motor, ball-screw and 6082-T6 rack tube in an AlSi10MnMg HPDC housing, deleting the entire hydraulic pump pack. Alternatives: dual-pinion EPS, A356-T6 cast housing, AM60B Mg cover. The BEV 12V/HV supply powers EPS directly with no parasitic pump drag, improving range. Deletes fluid, fill and leak test. ASIL-D dual sensing; assist maps tuned for the heavy BEV front axle. Recirculating-ball nut sized to 14kN.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes electric-pump assembly, reservoir, cooler and hydraulic line routing plus the oil-fill and pressure-decay leak station; alternatives dual-pinion or column EPS. Adds dry EPS sub-assembly (HPDC housing, ball-screw press). Net BOM down on pump pack and fluid; EOL fill cell removed. Tooling reuses extrusion and HPDC; assembly stations net reduced by one.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Pure EPS deletes the carried-over hydraulic pump pack and fill.

ideaData.riskNotes: Heavy BEV front axle demands high rack force; the ball-screw EPS is sized to 14kN for full-lock parking at GVW on 22in tyres, with thermal duty for repeated low-speed manoeuvres validated. Removing parasitic pump drag improves range. Premium on-centre feel and ADAS torque overlay tuned to match or beat the EHPS baseline. ISO 26262 ASIL-D fail-operational assist; off-road kickback and impact loads on the rack checked. NVH lower without pump whine.

title: PHEV blended braking deletes separate trailer-brake controller
description: [PHEV] Use the PHEV integrated brake-by-wire actuator's blended pressure control to manage trailer braking in software, deleting the standalone trailer-brake controller module. The one-box actuator meters pressure to the trailer gain output and blends regen, removing duplicated hardware on a tow-rated PHEV SUV.

ideaData.title: PHEV blended braking deletes separate trailer-brake controller
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline tow-rated trims add an aftermarket-style integrated trailer-brake controller with its own gain logic and connector. The change moves trailer-brake gain and proportional output into the one-box brake-by-wire ECU, which already meters master-cylinder pressure and blends e-axle regen, exposing a calibrated trailer output on the 7-pin/13-pin connector. Hardware: AlSi10MnMg HPDC actuator already present; deletes the standalone controller PCB and bracket. Alternatives: keep discrete controller, or app-based gain. Blending ensures regen and friction plus trailer braking act as one deceleration; ASIL rated.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC actuator (existing) / deleted controller PCB
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes the discrete trailer-brake controller module, its bracket, harness branch and dash aperture; alternative retains it. No new hardware tooling; reuses the one-box actuator and adds a trailer-output driver and software. Assembly removes a module mount and connector. BOM down a PCB and connector; calibration and validation are the spend, not tooling.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Software trailer gain in the brake-by-wire ECU deletes a module.

ideaData.riskNotes: Trailer braking blended with regen and friction must stay stable across split-mu, sway-control and ABS events, the core safety case, with proportional output validated against UN/ECE R13 trailer requirements and brake-controller standards at max towing GVW. Pedal feel held linear while trailer demand and regen arbitrate, preserving premium drivability. Fail-safe reverts to friction-only trailer braking on fault. Thermal duty for long-grade towing checked; connector pin-out and diagnostics meet regulatory trailer-lighting/brake norms.

title: Hybrid direct/indirect TPMS keeps sensors only on front axle
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] Adopt hybrid TPMS retaining direct sensors only on the front axle while inferring rear pressures from wheel-speed/vibration cross-referenced to the front. Halves direct-sensor count and battery replacements while still giving an absolute pressure reference and meeting detection regulation.

ideaData.title: Hybrid direct/indirect TPMS keeps sensors only on front axle
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline fits four direct TPMS modules. The change keeps two direct sensors (front axle) for absolute reference and infers rear pressures by indirect rolling-radius and natural-frequency analysis cross-calibrated against the fronts in the ESC ECU. Deletes two sensor modules, their lithium cells and RF load. Alternatives: full indirect, full direct, or BLE sensors. Front valves keep clamp-in metal valves; rear revert to snap-in rubber. Meets UN/ECE R64 with a documented detection strategy. Auto-location handled by the direct fronts plus indirect rears; relearn simplified.
ideaData.materialGrade: n/a 2x direct sensor + iTPMS / snap-in rear valve / clamp-in front valve
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Removes two sensor modules from BOM and halves the sensor pairing/relearn effort; alternative is full indirect. Rear valves simplify to snap-in rubber, easing tyre mounting. No new tooling; ESC software adds cross-axle calibration. Service replaces only two sensors over life, cutting parts logistics and battery-driven warranty roughly in half.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Front-only direct sensors halve module count while keeping a reference.

ideaData.riskNotes: Inferring rear pressure from indirect signals must meet UN/ECE R64 detection for simultaneous and individual deflation, the compliance risk, with the front direct reference improving robustness across loads and rims. Cross-axle calibration validated for SUV mass, trailering and varied surfaces. Premium displays still show front absolute pressures; rear shown as status. Reset procedure defined after inflation/rotation. Off-road airing-down handled by driver reset; no stopping-distance or stability regression at GVW.

title: Self-supporting run-flat delete via foam-seal LRR tyre
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace heavy self-supporting run-flat tyres with a self-sealing, foam-acoustic low-rolling-resistance tyre plus a compact mobility kit. Deletes the stiff reinforced sidewall, cutting ~3kg per corner of unsprung mass, improving ride and rolling resistance while retaining puncture mobility.

ideaData.title: Self-supporting run-flat delete via foam-seal LRR tyre
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline self-supporting run-flats (SSR) use thick reinforced sidewall inserts adding mass and harshness. The change fits a self-sealing tyre (inner butyl-based sealant layer that closes tread punctures) combined with a foam-acoustic liner and a silica LRR tread, plus a compact inflator kit. Deletes the heavy sidewall insert, cutting ~3kg/corner unsprung and lowering rolling resistance. Compounds: S-SBR/silica tread, butyl sealant, PU acoustic foam. Alternatives: keep SSR on high-speed trims, or seal-only. Ride and noise improve; mobility after puncture retained without a spare.
ideaData.materialGrade: Butyl self-seal layer / S-SBR/silica LRR tread / PU acoustic foam
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Tyre build adds a sealant-layer and foam-insert step but deletes the reinforced run-flat sidewall apex; alternative processes are SSR insert calendering versus seal-layer application. Lighter casing cuts rubber/steel mass per tyre. No OEM tooling change; supplier qualification dominates. Vehicle BOM swaps SSR for sealing tyre plus a low-cost inflator kit, no spare-wheel well needed.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Self-seal LRR tyre deletes the heavy run-flat sidewall mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: Removing run-flat capability changes the mobility case, so the self-sealing layer must reliably close common tread punctures and the inflator kit cover sidewall damage; validated against puncture and sealing standards. Lighter casing improves ride and rolling resistance, premium benefits, while load and high-speed (HL) durability at GVW are confirmed to UN/ECE R30/R117. Foam controls cavity noise. Customers lose drive-on-flat range; reset/repair guidance and TPMS warning timing tuned. Off-road sidewall cuts handled by kit.

title: Aluminium knuckle integrates caliper and steering-arm bosses
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace a cast-iron front steering knuckle with a forged or HPDC aluminium knuckle that integrates the caliper mount, steering-arm and ABS-sensor bosses. Consolidates brackets, cuts ~3.5kg unsprung per corner, and removes fasteners while carrying steering and braking loads.

ideaData.title: Aluminium knuckle integrates caliper and steering-arm bosses
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline iron knuckle plus bolted caliper bracket and steering-arm is heavy. The change forges or HPDC-casts an aluminium knuckle integrating caliper-mount lugs, steering-arm boss and ABS/wheel-speed sensor seat. Materials: 6082-T6 forged for fatigue, AlSi10MnMg HPDC for net-shape, or A356-T6 low-pressure cast. Cuts ~3.5kg/corner and deletes the bolted bracket and its fasteners. Alternatives by trim: forged for performance, HPDC for volume. Bearing bore and ball-joint taper machined; carries combined steering, braking and cornering loads. Corrosion-friendly aluminium reduces seizing of fasteners.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6082-T6 forged Al / AlSi10MnMg HPDC / A356-T6 low-pressure cast
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Process moves from iron casting plus separate bracket bolting to integrated aluminium forging or HPDC; alternatives A356-T6 low-pressure casting. Integration deletes a caliper bracket, 2-3 fasteners and an assembly step. Machining of bearing bore and tapers required. Forging/HPDC tooling is new capital, offset by mass and part-count savings at volume. Net assembly stations reduced.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Integrated aluminium knuckle deletes brackets, fasteners and mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: Aluminium knuckle must carry combined steering, braking and kerb-impact loads with iron-equivalent fatigue life, the governing concern, validated by multiaxial fatigue, caliper-lug pull and ball-joint pull-out at SUV GVW. Integrated bosses checked for casting porosity (HPDC) via X-ray; forged variant for performance trims. Reduced unsprung mass sharpens steering and ride, premium benefits. Galvanic corrosion at steel ball-joint/bearing interfaces managed by coatings. Off-road impact and ABS-sensor air-gap stability verified.

title: Brake-by-wire enables drag-free caliper for BEV range
description: [BEV] Exploit brake-by-wire active pad retraction to fit low-drag calipers with aggressive seal roll-back and lighter pistons on the BEV SUV. Eliminating residual pad drag recovers range and allows thinner rotors, since friction is rarely used under strong regen, trimming caliper and rotor mass.

ideaData.title: Brake-by-wire enables drag-free caliper for BEV range
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline calipers rely on passive seal roll-back, leaving slight residual drag that, on a regen-heavy BEV, persists for long periods. The change uses the brake-by-wire pressure source to command active pad retraction plus high-roll-back square-ring seals and lightweight phenolic or titanium pistons, eliminating drag. Caliper body forged 6082-T6; rotor thinned GG25 or AlSiC MMC. Alternatives: electric park-actuator retraction, knock-back springs. Drag-free operation recovers WLTP range; reduced friction duty allows lighter rotor. Pad-to-rotor clearance managed in software to keep first-stop response crisp.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6082-T6 forged caliper / phenolic or Ti piston / GG25 thin rotor
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Caliper machining adds a high-roll-back seal groove and lighter piston; alternatives are titanium or phenolic pistons versus steel, and active-retraction via the existing brake-by-wire pump (no new actuator). Thinner rotor cuts iron mass. No major new tooling; seal and piston are component swaps. Software clearance control needs validation, not assembly change. BOM trims piston and rotor mass.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Active retraction removes drag, recovers range and lightens hardware.

ideaData.riskNotes: Increased pad-to-rotor clearance risks a longer first-stop pedal travel, the key feel concern, mitigated by software pre-fill that closes clearance on brake intent or radar-predicted braking, preserving premium response. Drag-free seals validated for knock-back stability over rough/off-road sections so pads do not retract excessively. Thinner rotor thermal duty checked for descent. Corrosion of rarely-used rotors managed by periodic cleaning sweeps. Meets UN/ECE R13-H stopping at GVW; NVH unaffected.

title: Single-ECU EPS deletes redundant torque sensor via dual-MR
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] Replace twin discrete torque and angle sensors in the EPS with a single dual-die magnetoresistive sensor providing redundant channels in one package. Cuts component count and PCB area while retaining ASIL-D redundancy, lowering EPS sensor cost across all SUV powertrains.

ideaData.title: Single-ECU EPS deletes redundant torque sensor via dual-MR
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline EPS uses separate torque and steering-angle sensors plus a redundant torque sensor for ASIL-D. The change adopts a single dual-die TMR/AMR sensor package giving two independent torque channels and integrated angle on one PCB, halving sensor part count. Torsion bar stays 50CrV4 spring steel; target ring samarium-cobalt or NdFeB. Alternatives: inductive dual-channel sensor, or GMR. ECU consolidates inputs, shrinking PCB and connectors. Redundancy preserved in silicon for fail-operational assist. Applied across all SUV powertrains; calibration per variant.
ideaData.materialGrade: 50CrV4 torsion bar / dual-die TMR sensor / NdFeB target ring
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces two sensor sub-assemblies and their connectors with one dual-die package; alternatives inductive or GMR dual-channel. Smaller PCB and fewer solder joints cut SMT cost and a connector. No mechanical tooling change to the rack; sensor placement fixture simplified. BOM removes a sensor and harness; supplier consolidation lowers piece price. Calibration step unchanged.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One dual-die sensor replaces redundant discrete sensors.

ideaData.riskNotes: Single-package redundancy raises common-cause-failure scrutiny, the central safety question: dual independent dies, separate supplies and diverse signal paths within the package satisfy ISO 26262 ASIL-D fail-operational steering, validated by FMEDA and fault injection. Sensor accuracy and temperature drift must preserve precise on-centre feel and ADAS torque overlay expected in a premium SUV. EMC robustness checked. Off-road vibration and thermal cycling validated; no degradation of steering precision at high speed or full lock.

title: Composite leaf-style regen blend cuts PHEV brake hardware
description: [PHEV] Retune PHEV e-axle regen authority and blended-braking arbitration to cover more deceleration electrically, then downsize the rear caliper to a single-piston unit and thinner rotor. Higher regen share on the driven axle reduces friction duty, trimming rear brake mass and cost while keeping fade margin for towing.

ideaData.title: Composite leaf-style regen blend cuts PHEV brake hardware
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline rear brakes are sized for full friction duty despite a capable e-axle. The change raises rear-axle regen authority and refines blended arbitration so the e-axle handles more of the 0-0.35g range, cutting rear friction energy ~30%. The rear caliper downsizes from twin- to single-piston (forged 6082-T6) on a thinner GG25 rotor; pad area reduced. Alternatives: MMC rotor, NAO pad, smaller diameter. Front stays unchanged for stability. Blending ensures one linear deceleration; near-standstill handover to friction tuned. Towing duty retains fade headroom.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6082-T6 forged single-piston caliper / GG25 thinner rotor / NAO pad
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Process change downsizes the rear caliper casting/forging and rotor; alternatives MMC rotor or single- vs twin-piston forging. Fewer pistons cut bore machining and seal count. BOM trims caliper and rotor mass; pad area smaller. Calibration of regen/friction blend needs no tooling. Assembly station unchanged; supplier piece price drops on the simpler rear corner.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Higher rear regen lets the rear corner downsize calipers and rotor.

ideaData.riskNotes: Leaning on rear regen must not destabilise the vehicle: friction stays front-biased and ESC arbitration prevents rear over-braking on low-mu, validated for split-mu, ABS and trailer-sway at towing GVW. Downsized rear rotor thermal capacity checked for long descents where regen tapers as the battery fills, the key duty case, with friction fallback sized accordingly. Pedal feel linear through handover; premium refinement preserved. Meets UN/ECE R13-H fade and stability; NVH from smaller pads controlled.

title: Steer-by-wire steering-column delete frees BEV crash space
description: [BEV] On the BEV SUV, delete the steering column and intermediate shaft entirely with steer-by-wire, replacing them with a compact stowable feedback unit. Removes the column, U-joints, firewall seal and energy-absorbing collapse mechanism, simplifying LHD/RHD commonisation and freeing crash and packaging volume.

ideaData.title: Steer-by-wire steering-column delete frees BEV crash space
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline retains a collapsible column, intermediate shaft, two U-joints and a firewall seal even with assisted steering. The change deploys full steer-by-wire: a road-wheel actuator at the rack and a compact hand-wheel feedback unit, deleting the column entirely. LHD/RHD differ only by feedback-unit mounting and software, aiding commonisation. Feedback housing AlSi10MnMg HPDC; planetary 16MnCr5; redundant MR sensing; dual BLDC windings; ASIL-D. Alternatives: clutched backup column, A380 die-cast housing. Frees footwell crash crush space and removes the energy-absorbing collapse design.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC feedback housing / 16MnCr5 planetary / dual-MR
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes column tube, intermediate-shaft swaging, two U-joint presses, firewall grommet and the collapse mechanism; alternatives retain a clutched backup. LHD/RHD share hardware, halving column part numbers and commonising the line. Adds redundant feedback-unit wiring. Net part and weld/seal count down sharply; firewall sealing simplified, improving NVH and reducing foam. Assembly alignment of column to rack eliminated.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Deleting the column removes hardware and commonises LHD/RHD.

ideaData.riskNotes: Removing the mechanical column eliminates the conventional energy-absorbing collapse, so occupant crash protection is re-engineered, the central safety case, validated to UN/ECE R94 frontal and R12 steering-impact-equivalent with the new feedback-unit mount and airbag strategy. Fail-operational steering via dual windings, ECUs, power nets and sensing meets R79 SbW and ASIL-D. Premium feel synthesised with high-fidelity torque rendering including off-road texture. Freed crush space improves intrusion; redundancy fault-injection tested.
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN/ECE R79 SbW, R94 frontal, R12, ISO 26262 ASIL-D

title: Roll-formed steel wheel for entry BEV winter-tyre package
description: [BEV] Offer a roll-formed high-strength steel wheel with a full aero cover for the entry BEV SUV winter-tyre package, replacing a cast alloy. Cheaper raw material and roll-forming suit a value, range-focused winter wheel where the aero cover restores efficiency and styling at lower cost.

ideaData.title: Roll-formed steel wheel for entry BEV winter-tyre package
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline winter package uses a cast alloy wheel. The change fits a roll-formed/stamped high-strength steel wheel (HSLA S420/S550 rim, stamped disc) with a full plastic aero cover, for the value winter-tyre option. Steel raw cost is far below aluminium; roll-forming the rim and welding to a stamped disc is low-cost at volume. Alternatives: flow-formed alloy, basic cast alloy. Aero cover (PP/ABS) restores BEV efficiency and appearance. Heavier than alloy but acceptable for a seasonal value wheel; corrosion via cataphoretic coat. Load rating sized for BEV GVW.
ideaData.materialGrade: HSLA S420/S550 steel rim / stamped disc / PP-ABS aero cover
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Process is roll-forming the rim, stamping the disc and seam/flash welding versus aluminium casting; alternatives flow-formed alloy. Steel and roll-forming are low-capital and cheap per part at volume. Aero cover injection-moulded. BOM swaps alloy for steel plus cover, cutting material cost despite added cover. Welding and e-coat lines standard. Heavier wheel offset by seasonal/value positioning.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Roll-formed steel plus aero cover undercuts cast alloy on cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Added unsprung mass versus alloy slightly dulls ride and erodes range, accepted for a seasonal value wheel where the aero cover recovers most efficiency; range checked on WLTP winter cycle. Steel wheel passes radial-, cornering-fatigue and impact at BEV GVW, with corrosion controlled by cataphoretic coating for road-salt durability. Aero-cover retention and rattle validated for NVH. Premium appearance maintained via cover styling; bead-seat air retention and balance confirmed. Off-road not targeted.

title: Brake-by-wire deletes ABS pump for BEV via e-axle modulation
description: [BEV] On the dual-motor BEV SUV, use fast e-axle torque modulation plus the one-box brake-by-wire pressure source to perform low-g ABS/traction events, simplifying the hydraulic modulator valve count. Electric wheel-torque control handles many slip events without the full ESC valve block, trimming hydraulic hardware.

ideaData.title: Brake-by-wire deletes ABS pump for BEV via e-axle modulation
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline ESC uses a full 12-valve hydraulic modulator with pump for ABS/TCS/ESC. The change leverages dual e-axles for fast regen/traction torque vectoring to manage low-slip and traction events, while the one-box brake-by-wire pressure source provides wheel-pressure control, allowing a reduced-valve hydraulic block (fewer isolation/dump valves) for high-g friction ABS only. Block in A380 die-cast; alternatives AlSi9Cu3 or machined 6082-T6. Deletes some valves and simplifies the pump. ASIL-D arbitration blends electric and hydraulic slip control. Friction ABS retained for the worst case.
ideaData.materialGrade: A380 die-cast valve block / AlSi9Cu3 (option) / e-axle torque control
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Reduces valve count in the hydraulic modulator casting and simplifies the pump; alternatives AlSi9Cu3 die-casting or 6082-T6 machined block. Electric slip control needs software, not hardware. BOM removes several solenoid valves and seals; block machining simplified. EOL fill/bleed shortened with fewer circuits. Tooling for a simpler block; assembly seal count down.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: E-axle torque control offloads slip events, shrinking the valve block.

ideaData.riskNotes: Friction ABS authority must remain for high-g, low-mu and brake-blending failure cases, the safety boundary, so a reduced but compliant hydraulic modulator and the brake-by-wire source still deliver wheel-by-wheel pressure to UN/ECE R13-H ABS and Euro NCAP AEB at GVW. E-axle slip control validated for response latency versus hydraulic on ice/split-mu. Arbitration between electric and friction slip control verified fail-operational ASIL-D. Off-road traction and trailer stability checked; NVH of e-axle modulation refined.
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN/ECE R13-H ABS, Euro NCAP, ISO 26262 ASIL-D

title: Common steering-rack mounting across SUV and pickup platform
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Standardise the EPS rack mounting interface, tie-rod geometry and bolt pattern across the body-on-frame SUV and its pickup sibling, so one rack family and bracket set serves both. Commonising the mechanical interface consolidates tooling and supplier volume across two high-value programmes.
submittedBy: Toyota Land Cruiser 300 / Lexus LX benchmark

ideaData.title: Common steering-rack mounting across SUV and pickup platform
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline SUV and pickup siblings use distinct racks and crossmember mounts. The change defines a common EPS rack family: shared mounting bolt pattern, tie-rod inner geometry and 6082-T6 rack tube, with AlSi10MnMg HPDC housing, differing only in rack-force tune and tie-rod length. Crossmember bracket commonised in HSLA S420 stamping or A356-T6 cast. Alternatives: shared rack, variant brackets. Software sets assist for SUV refinement versus pickup load. One sensor cluster, one ECU family. Consolidates two body-on-frame programmes onto one steering interface, maximising volume leverage.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6082-T6 rack / AlSi10MnMg HPDC / HSLA S420 bracket
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Collapses two rack and bracket designs into one family, sharing extrusion, HPDC and stamping tooling; alternative shares rack only. PPAP and crash re-validation done once across two programmes. Assembly fixtures and torque sequences unified at both plants. BOM part numbers fall; supplier volume per part rises, lowering piece price and tooling amortisation across SUV plus pickup.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One rack interface across SUV and pickup maximises volume leverage.

ideaData.riskNotes: Pickup payload and SUV refinement impose different rack-force and feel targets, the integration risk, resolved by common hardware with variant assist maps and tie-rod lengths, validated for full-lock effort at each GVW and steering precision. Body-on-frame mount stiffness and NVH isolation tuned per programme via bushings, not the shared bracket. Off-road articulation clearance checked for both. ISO 26262 ASIL-D and UN/ECE R79 satisfied once for the shared rack; durability covers the higher pickup loads.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Toyota Land Cruiser 300 / Lexus LX (2021) steering

evidenceSources.title: LX vs Land Cruiser rack mounts

title: BLE battery-less TPMS via wheel-harvested energy on premium SUV
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Adopt battery-less direct TPMS that harvests energy from wheel rotation/vibration and reports over BLE, eliminating the lithium cell and its end-of-life sensor replacement. Removes the recurring battery-driven sensor swap and lowers lifetime service and warranty cost on the premium SUV.

ideaData.title: BLE battery-less TPMS via wheel-harvested energy on premium SUV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline direct TPMS modules rely on a non-replaceable lithium cell that ends sensor life at ~7-10 years, forcing whole-sensor replacement. The change uses an energy-harvesting sensor (piezoelectric or electromagnetic vibration harvester) powering a MEMS pressure sensor and BLE 5 transmitter, removing the battery. Valve clamp-in aluminium; harvester piezo PZT or electromagnetic coil. Alternatives: keep coin-cell direct, indirect, or hybrid. BLE links to the existing vehicle gateway, deleting a dedicated RF receiver. Reports pressure/temperature; auto-location via BLE RSSI. Sensor life extends to vehicle life.
ideaData.materialGrade: PZT piezo harvester / MEMS pressure die / clamp-in Al valve
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Sensor build adds a harvester and BLE radio but deletes the lithium cell and a dedicated RF receiver (reuses BLE gateway); alternatives coin-cell or indirect. EOL pairing via BLE simplifies relearn. No vehicle tooling change. BOM removes the receiver and the future replacement-sensor service event. Higher sensor unit cost offset by deleted receiver and lifetime warranty/service savings.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Battery-less harvesting deletes the sensor-replacement service event.

ideaData.riskNotes: Harvested power must guarantee reliable transmission at low speed and standstill, the functional risk, mitigated by a supercapacitor buffer and BLE low-duty scheduling so UN/ECE R64 detection timing is met across the speed range; cold-start and parked reporting validated. BLE security and coexistence with cabin BLE checked. Harvester durability over wheel vibration and temperature for vehicle life confirmed. Premium displays show per-wheel pressure. Off-road low-speed crawl reporting verified; no safety regression at GVW.

title: Regen-blended one-pedal mapping deletes BEV creep-brake hold valve
description: [BEV] Use brake-by-wire blended one-pedal control and motor creep-torque to hold the BEV SUV at standstill, deleting the dedicated auto-hold isolation valve and pressure-hold hydraulic circuit. Software hill-hold via the pressure source and e-axle removes hardware while keeping seamless stop-and-hold behaviour.

ideaData.title: Regen-blended one-pedal mapping deletes BEV creep-brake hold valve
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline auto-hold uses a dedicated isolation/hold valve and a pressure-maintenance circuit in the ESC block to keep the vehicle stationary. The change implements software hill-hold: the one-box brake-by-wire pressure source actively maintains clamp force at standstill, with the e-axle providing creep-torque hold and smooth release, deleting the dedicated hold valve and its circuit. Valve block in A380 die-cast simplifies by one solenoid. Alternatives: keep hold valve, EMB park hold. One-pedal regen blends to standstill; EPB latches for long holds. ASIL-rated; seamless transition for premium stop-and-go.
ideaData.materialGrade: A380 die-cast block (one fewer valve) / e-axle creep hold
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes a solenoid valve and its bore/seal from the modulator casting and the associated hydraulic circuit; alternative retains the valve. Software handles hold via the pressure source and e-axle. BOM removes a valve and seals; block machining simplified. EOL fill/bleed marginally shorter. No new tooling; calibration and validation are the spend. Assembly seal count down by one.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Software hill-hold via pressure source and e-axle deletes a hold valve.

ideaData.riskNotes: Standstill hold must be robust on grades and during electrical transients, the safety concern: the brake-by-wire source maintains clamp with energy buffering and the EPB latches for sustained or fault conditions, validated to UN/ECE R13-H park-on-grade at GVW including towing. One-pedal blend to zero and release tuned for premium smoothness without rollback. E-axle creep-hold thermal duty checked. Fail-safe falls back to EPB. Off-road hill-descent and crawl hold verified; ASIL-rated arbitration tested.

title: Hot-stamped 22MnB5 A-pillar with tailor-welded soft zone
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace two-piece cold-stamped DP780 A-pillar with single hot-stamped 22MnB5 1500MPa boron part carrying a laser-tailored soft zone for controlled folding. Cuts mass and spot welds while raising small-overlap crash performance, supporting both ICE BIW stiffness and BEV greenhouse intrusion targets across the premium SUV line.

ideaData.title: Hot-stamped 22MnB5 A-pillar with tailor-welded soft zone
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline A-pillar is a welded assembly of cold-stamped DP780 inner and DP980 outer with an HSLA S420 reinforcement, requiring 26 spot welds and adhesive. The change consolidates this into one press-hardened 22MnB5 1500MPa boron blank with a laser-heat-treated soft zone (~600MPa) at the hinge area to govern fold kinematics. Alternatives evaluated: DP980 AHSS roll-formed section, 7075-T6 aluminium extrusion, and CP1000 complex-phase. Physics: martensitic transformation in the press die gives high yield with thin gauge, while the engineered soft zone dissipates small-overlap energy without intruding the survival cell.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Shifts from cold stamping plus resistance spot welding to hot stamping with laser tailoring and laser trimming. Eliminates one inner stamping and ~14 spot welds, removing a sub-assembly weld fixture. Adds press-hardening line capacity and boron-tool wear management but nets lower BOM part count, fewer joins and reduced assembly cycle time.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Part consolidation plus gauge-down on a structural pillar.

ideaData.riskNotes: Press-hardened pillars risk hydrogen embrittlement and reduced bending ductility at welds, so soft-zone placement is validated by small-overlap and roof-crush FEA plus physical sled tests. NVH is preserved as torsional stiffness rises with the closed single-piece section. Durability is confirmed against corrosion via AlSi coating. Premium refinement is maintained because the stiffer greenhouse lowers door-aperture deflection and wind-noise paths.
ideaData.regulatoryContext: Euro NCAP frontal small-overlap; FMVSS 216 roof crush

title: Tailor-rolled blank B-pillar for graded crash strength
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Convert constant-gauge hot-stamped B-pillar into a tailor-rolled blank with thickness varying 1.0–2.2mm, removing the separate patch reinforcement. Optimises side-impact load path while shedding mass, benefiting BEV greenhouse intrusion limits and ICE BIW stiffness equally across the luxury SUV platform.

ideaData.title: Tailor-rolled blank B-pillar for graded crash strength
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline B-pillar uses a uniform 1.8mm 22MnB5 hot-stamped shell with an additional patchwork reinforcement spot-welded over the belt-line zone. The change adopts a tailor-rolled blank (TRB) of 22MnB5 with continuously variable thickness from 1.0mm at the rocker join to 2.2mm at the belt line, press-hardened in one shot. Alternatives: patchwork tailor-welded blank with DP1000, CP1200 roll-formed insert, and 7075-T6 aluminium hybrid. Physics: graded gauge places material exactly where bending moment peaks in side pole impact, lowering peak intrusion velocity while deleting redundant reinforcement mass and joints.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces blanking-plus-patch-welding with flexible cold rolling to produce variable-gauge coil, then hot stamping. Eliminates the patch stamping, its spot-weld station and adhesive bead. Tooling needs a TRB-capable die and revised quench. Net effect is fewer parts, lower weld count and simplified sub-assembly, offsetting TRB coil premium.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Deletes a reinforcement and grades steel to the load path.

ideaData.riskNotes: TRB transition zones can concentrate strain, so fillet radii and quench uniformity are tuned to avoid weld-line softening; side-pole and FMVSS 214 dynamic intrusion are verified by sled and CAE. Durability risk from gauge steps is mitigated with smooth thickness ramps. NVH and torsional rigidity improve with a one-piece pillar, preserving the quiet, planted on-road comfort expected of a premium SUV.

title: Multi-cell aluminium rocker extrusion for BEV pack protection
description: [BEV] Replace fabricated steel rocker box with a 6082-T6 multi-cell aluminium extrusion sized to absorb side-pole crush and protect the structural battery pack. Cuts mass and weld content while meeting stringent BEV intrusion limits, enabling pack edge-to-sill load sharing on the skateboard architecture.

ideaData.title: Multi-cell aluminium rocker extrusion for BEV pack protection
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline rocker is a multi-piece DP980/HSLA spot-welded box with internal bulkheads. The change is a continuous 6082-T6 multi-cell extrusion with engineered crush ribs spanning the wheelbase, bolted and bonded to the battery enclosure flange to share side-pole energy. Alternatives: 6014-T4 crash extrusion, AlSi10MnMg HPDC node castings at the ends, and 22MnB5 1500MPa roll-formed steel insert for the critical cell. Physics: progressive cell collapse caps intrusion velocity below the pack keep-out, while the closed multi-cell profile raises lateral bending stiffness and torsional rigidity of the lower body.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Moves from steel stamping and resistance spot welding to aluminium extrusion plus FSW/MIG of end nodes and structural adhesive bonding to the pack. Deletes multiple stampings and ~30 spot welds per side. Tooling shifts to extrusion dies and CNC fixtures; introduces HPDC end castings. Reduces part count and join variability and improves sealing.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Single extrusion replaces a fabricated steel box and shares pack load.

ideaData.riskNotes: Aluminium-to-pack galvanic corrosion and bolt-load relaxation are controlled with isolation coatings and bonded interfaces; side-pole, ground-impact and pack short-circuit cases are validated to UN ECE R100. Off-road jacking and stone-impact durability are addressed with thicker outer cells. NVH benefits from the closed section, preserving premium quietness, while the protected pack maintains long-term BEV range and safety margins.

evidenceSources.title: EX90 safety and battery protection brief

ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN ECE R100; Euro NCAP side pole

title: Roll-formed roof rail in CP1000 to delete reinforcement
description: [ICE,MHEV] Replace stamped multi-piece roof rail with a roll-formed CP1000 closed section that integrates the roof-crush reinforcement. Lowers tooling and part count while sustaining ICE BIW torsional stiffness and MHEV roof-rack load cases on the premium SUV body.

ideaData.title: Roll-formed roof rail in CP1000 to delete reinforcement
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline roof side rail is a stamped DP590 inner/outer pair plus a separate HSLA reinforcement, joined by spot welds along the roof ditch. The change is a continuous roll-formed CP1000 complex-phase closed section running the cant rail, integrating the reinforcement function. Alternatives: DP780 stamped section, 22MnB5 1500MPa hot-stamped rail, and 6082-T6 aluminium extrusion. Physics: a constant closed profile gives high section modulus for FMVSS 216 roof crush and roof-rack moments, while roll forming yields near-zero scrap versus stamping and removes the discrete reinforcement.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces tandem stamping dies and a reinforcement spot-weld station with a roll-forming line and laser cut-to-length. Eliminates one reinforcement stamping and reduces spot welds along the ditch. Lowers die investment and material scrap; sweep-bending tooling is added. Net lower BOM, fewer welds and improved dimensional repeatability for roof-bow fit.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Roll forming a closed rail removes stamping scrap and a reinforcement.

ideaData.riskNotes: Roll-formed high-strength sections risk springback and end-flare affecting roof-bow and windshield datum, mitigated by sweep calibration and laser trimming. Roof-crush strength-to-weight is verified to FMVSS 216a with the integrated reinforcement. Torsional stiffness improves with the continuous closed profile, preserving premium handling precision and low wind noise. Durability at sunroof cut-outs is validated against fatigue.

evidenceSources.title: Roll forming for automotive roof rails

title: Hot-formed roof bows commonised across SUV variants
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Standardise three bespoke stamped roof bows into one hot-formed 22MnB5 family carried across ICE, PHEV and BEV roof variants including panoramic glass. Cuts tooling and inventory while holding roof-crush and torsional targets on all premium SUV derivatives.

ideaData.title: Hot-formed roof bows commonised across SUV variants
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline uses three unique stamped DP590 roof bows differing by roof type (steel, panoramic, BEV). The change defines a single hot-formed 22MnB5 1500MPa bow family with shared attachment datums and optional local patchwork for the panoramic cut-out. Alternatives: DP780 stamped bow, 6082-T6 aluminium extruded bow, and AM60B magnesium die-cast bow. Physics: a common high-strength section provides the lateral diaphragm action stabilising the roof against crush and torsion; modular end brackets accommodate variant-specific roof rail offsets without new bow tooling.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates three stamping die sets into one hot-stamping tool plus shared weld fixtures, with laser-cut variant features. Reduces unique part numbers, logistics lanes and inspection plans. Tooling capital drops with one die family; hot-stamp cycle is added. Net lower part count, fewer fixtures and simplified BIW framing across derivatives.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One bow family replaces three variant-specific stampings.

ideaData.riskNotes: Commonising across roof types risks over-design for lighter variants and panoramic stiffness loss, mitigated by local reinforcement only where glass aperture demands it. Roof-crush, modal roof drumming and torsional stiffness are validated per variant by CAE and physical test. NVH is preserved through tuned bow spacing, and premium refinement is maintained because the stiffer common bow reduces panoramic-roof boom across the line.

title: Patchwork C-pillar blank to cut reinforcement count
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] Adopt a hot-stamped patchwork blank for the C-pillar, laser-welding a DP1000 patch onto a 22MnB5 base before forming. Removes two bolt-on reinforcements, trimming weld content and mass while securing rear side-impact and towing load paths on the premium SUV body.

ideaData.title: Patchwork C-pillar blank to cut reinforcement count
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline C-pillar is a 22MnB5 shell with two spot-welded HSLA reinforcements managing rear-door belt loads and towing/D-ring reactions. The change is a patchwork tailor-welded blank: a DP1000 patch is laser-welded onto the 22MnB5 1500MPa base sheet pre-forming, then co-pressed and quenched as one part. Alternatives: tailor-rolled 22MnB5 blank, CP1200 roll-formed insert, and 7075-T6 aluminium reinforcement. Physics: locally doubled gauge from the welded patch raises stiffness exactly at the towing and belt-load nodes while the surrounding single gauge stays light.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces two reinforcement stampings and their spot-weld stations with a single laser-welding cell feeding the hot-stamp press. Co-forming patch and base deletes downstream sub-assembly. Tooling adds a laser-weld fixture; hot-stamp die handles the stepped blank. Net fewer parts, fewer welds, reduced fixturing and improved dimensional stability of the rear aperture.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Welded patch deletes two bolt-on reinforcements.

ideaData.riskNotes: Laser weld-line ductility and patch edge fatigue under towing cycles are the key risks, controlled by weld-seam placement away from peak strain and post-quench inspection. Rear side-impact and trailer-hitch durability are validated to ECE R55 and Euro NCAP. Torsional contribution of the rear ring is preserved, and premium refinement is held as the stiffer pillar reduces tailgate-aperture shake and rear wind noise.
ideaData.regulatoryContext: ECE R55 towing; Euro NCAP side impact

title: BEV sill anti-intrusion insert in CP1200 roll form
description: [BEV] Add a roll-formed CP1200 anti-intrusion beam inside the aluminium sill to cap pack-edge deformation in side-pole impact. Replaces a heavier steel bulkhead pack and lets the outer extrusion gauge down, protecting the BEV battery while trimming lower-body mass.

ideaData.title: BEV sill anti-intrusion insert in CP1200 roll form
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline sill carries multiple stamped DP980 internal bulkheads to resist pole intrusion toward the pack. The change inserts one continuous roll-formed CP1200 complex-phase anti-intrusion beam into the 6082-T6 extruded sill cavity, bonded and riveted, allowing the outer extrusion wall to thin. Alternatives: 22MnB5 1500MPa hot-stamped insert, DP1180 martensitic strip, and pultruded CFRP plank. Physics: the high-strength steel beam acts as a stiff backstop limiting lateral intrusion while the aluminium cells absorb energy, decoupling crush absorption from final intrusion control to protect the pack keep-out zone.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces several stamped bulkheads and their welds with one roll-formed beam plus self-pierce riveting and adhesive into the extrusion. Reduces bulkhead stampings, weld stations and sealing operations. Tooling adds a roll-form line; assembly gains an SPR/bonding cell. Net lower part count, fewer joints, better seal integrity and reduced overall sill mass.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One roll-formed backstop replaces multiple steel bulkheads.

ideaData.riskNotes: Steel-aluminium galvanic coupling and SPR joint fatigue in the wet sill are mitigated with isolation primers and sealed rivets; side-pole, ground-strike and pack-deformation cases are validated to UN ECE R100 and GB 38031. Off-road sill loading and jacking durability are confirmed. NVH improves with the stiffer sill, preserving premium quietness, while the protected pack maintains BEV safety and warranty exposure stays low.

title: Mg AM60B D-pillar inner casting for tailgate ring
description: [MHEV,PHEV] Replace a multi-piece stamped D-pillar inner with an AM60B magnesium high-pressure die-casting consolidating brackets and the tailgate-hinge node. Sheds rear-mass to offset PHEV battery weight while maintaining tailgate-aperture stiffness on the premium SUV.

ideaData.title: Mg AM60B D-pillar inner casting for tailgate ring
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: MHEV/PHEV. Baseline D-pillar inner is an assembly of stamped DP590 panels with welded-on hinge and gas-strut brackets. The change is a single AM60B magnesium HPDC inner that integrates the tailgate hinge boss, strut mounts and harness clips. Alternatives: AE44 creep-resistant magnesium, AlSi10MnMg aluminium HPDC, and DP780 stamped assembly. Physics: casting permits ribbed topology placing material along principal stress paths of the tailgate ring, raising local stiffness per kg; magnesium's low density removes mass high and aft, lowering rear yaw inertia to help offset PHEV pack mass.
ideaData.materialGrade: AM60B Mg HPDC / AE44 Mg / AlSi10MnMg
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates five stampings and several welded brackets into one die-casting, deleting a spot-weld sub-assembly and bracket logistics. Tooling shifts to an HPDC die and trim tool; assembly gains flow-drill screws and adhesive to the steel body. Net major part-count reduction, fewer welds and integrated features, offset by Mg material and coating cost.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Casting consolidates the tailgate-ring inner and brackets.

ideaData.riskNotes: Magnesium galvanic corrosion and creep at the hinge boss are the principal risks, controlled with conversion coating, isolation washers and AE44 option for hot zones. Tailgate slam-durability, hinge fatigue and rear-impact integrity are validated by rig and CAE. NVH from the lighter ring is managed with tuned ribs to avoid tailgate boom, preserving the solid, premium close feel expected of a luxury SUV.

evidenceSources.title: Mg in automotive closures and pillars

title: Bonded roof rail to delete spot welds and seal labour
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Switch the roof-to-side-rail join from a welded ditch with applied moulding to a structurally bonded laser-brazed seam. Cuts weld count, sealer and trim cost while improving torsional stiffness and water management across the premium SUV roof.

ideaData.title: Bonded roof rail to delete spot welds and seal labour
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline roof panel attaches to the DP590 side rail via a spot-welded ditch sealed with PVC and hidden by a clip-on moulding. The change uses laser brazing with a CuSi3 filler plus structural adhesive to form a smooth, sealed seam, eliminating the ditch and moulding. Alternatives: remote laser welding, weld-bonding with DP780 rail, and roll-formed aluminium 6014-T4 rail with FDS. Physics: a continuous brazed/bonded line increases shear coupling between roof and rail, raising torsional stiffness and modal frequency versus discrete spot welds, while removing capillary water paths.
ideaData.materialGrade: DP590 rail + CuSi3 braze / DP780 / 6014-T4 Al
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces ~40 spot welds, PVC seam sealing and a moulding part with a laser-braze/bonding station. Deletes the moulding, clips and sealer robot. Tooling adds laser optics and clamping; cycle time competitive. Net lower part count, less consumable sealer, fewer finishing operations and a class-A seam without applied trim.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Continuous brazed seam deletes welds, sealer and a moulding.

ideaData.riskNotes: Brazed-seam porosity and zinc-coating burn-off can risk corrosion, mitigated by seam-tracking control and post-braze sealing; salt-spray and water-leak durability are validated. Roof modal and torsional stiffness improve, suppressing roof drumming for premium quietness. Crash load paths are unchanged as the seam is non-structural in crush; durability under thermal cycling of the brazed joint is confirmed to preserve long-term refinement.

title: Tailor-welded blank rocker for ICE BIW stiffness
description: [ICE,MHEV] Build the steel rocker from a tailor-welded blank pairing DP980 at the crash zones with HSLA S420 mid-span, deleting a separate reinforcement. Raises ICE BIW torsional stiffness while trimming mass on the premium SUV lower body.

ideaData.title: Tailor-welded blank rocker for ICE BIW stiffness
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline rocker outer is a uniform DP780 stamping with a welded-in DP980 reinforcement at the B-pillar foot. The change is a laser tailor-welded blank combining DP980 segments at the front/rear crash and B-pillar zones with HSLA S420 in the lower-stress mid-span, formed as one rocker. Alternatives: tailor-rolled DP1000 blank, 22MnB5 1500MPa hot-stamped rocker, and 6082-T6 aluminium extrusion. Physics: placing higher-strength grade only where bending and crush demand it lets mid-span gauge drop, cutting mass while the continuous section preserves torsional and bending stiffness of the body sides.
ideaData.materialGrade: DP980 + HSLA S420 TWB / DP1000 TRB / 22MnB5 1500MPa
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces stamping plus reinforcement spot-welding with laser blank welding feeding one stamping die. Deletes the reinforcement stamping and its weld station. Tooling reuses the stamping line with a TWB weld cell upstream. Net fewer parts, fewer welds, lower scrap from grade-matched nesting, and simplified rocker sub-assembly.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Grade placement by load path deletes a reinforcement.

ideaData.riskNotes: TWB weld-line formability and fatigue at the grade transition are risks, controlled by seam placement in low-strain zones and weld-quality monitoring. Side-impact, jacking and frame-mount durability are validated to FMVSS 214. Torsional stiffness is maintained or improved, supporting precise on-road handling and low NVH; corrosion of the welded seam is addressed with cathodic e-coat coverage to preserve premium durability.

title: Integrated roof-bow and rail node by FDS joining
description: [MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Join roof bows to the cant rail with flow-drill screws and adhesive instead of welded gusset brackets, removing parts and enabling mixed-material roofs. Maintains roof-crush and torsional targets while easing aluminium-roof adoption on premium SUVs.

ideaData.title: Integrated roof-bow and rail node by FDS joining
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline roof bow ties to the side rail through a stamped steel gusset spot-welded both sides. The change uses flow-drill screws (FDS) with structural adhesive to fasten bow ends directly to the rail, deleting the gusset and enabling steel-to-aluminium roofs. Alternatives: self-pierce riveting, weld-bonding with DP590, and laser welding. Physics: FDS forms a load-bearing threaded boss in single-sided access, giving repeatable shear/peel capacity for roof-crush diaphragm loads while permitting dissimilar 6014-T4 aluminium roof panels without thermal weld incompatibility.
ideaData.materialGrade: DP590 rail / 6014-T4 Al roof / AlSi10MnMg node
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces a stamped gusset and two spot-weld stations with single-sided FDS plus adhesive, suiting closed sections and mixed materials. Deletes the gusset stamping and its fixture. Tooling adds FDS spindles; assembly access simplifies. Net lower part count, fewer welds, and material flexibility that supports later aluminium-roof variants without reworking the join concept.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Single-sided fastening deletes gussets and enables mixed roofs.

ideaData.riskNotes: FDS joint loosening under thermal cycling and galvanic corrosion at steel-aluminium interfaces are mitigated by adhesive co-cure and isolation coatings; roof-crush and torsional diaphragm loads are validated to FMVSS 216a. Durability of the threaded boss under fatigue is rig-tested. NVH benefits from the bonded line damping roof drumming, preserving the calm, premium cabin character across powertrain variants.

title: AlSi10MnMg HPDC rocker node for BEV pack mounts
