description: [BEV] Replace fabricated rocker-end fittings with AlSi10MnMg high-pressure die-cast nodes that integrate pack mounts, jack points and crash load paths. Cuts part count and joins while securing battery attachment and side-pole loads on the BEV skateboard.

ideaData.title: AlSi10MnMg HPDC rocker node for BEV pack mounts
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline rocker-to-floor corners are built from several stamped DP980 brackets and a cast jack-point, spot-welded and bolted. The change is one AlSi10MnMg high-pressure die-cast node per corner integrating the sill extrusion socket, pack mounting bosses, jack point and crossmember interface. Alternatives: A356-T6 low-pressure casting, 6082-T6 forged node, and AM60B magnesium casting. Physics: a single ribbed casting routes side-pole and pack-attachment loads through optimised paths, raising local stiffness per kg and giving precise, repeatable pack mounting datums versus a welded bracket stack.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates multiple stampings and a separate jack-point into one casting, deleting spot welds and bolted brackets. Tooling shifts to a large HPDC die and trim; assembly uses FSW/SPR to the extrusion. Net major part-count and weld reduction, integrated functions and tighter pack-mount tolerances, offset by casting tool capital and porosity control.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One casting integrates pack mounts and crash load paths.

ideaData.riskNotes: HPDC porosity and low elongation can risk crash-zone cracking, mitigated by vacuum die-casting, T7 heat treatment and self-pierce-friendly alloy selection. Side-pole, pack pull-off and jacking durability are validated to UN ECE R100. Galvanic isolation protects steel fasteners. NVH improves with the stiff node, preserving premium isolation, and repeatable pack datums maintain BEV crash margins and warranty performance.
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN ECE R100; Euro NCAP side pole

title: Hot-stamped A-pillar lower commonised ICE/BEV
description: [ICE,BEV] Commonise the hot-stamped A-pillar lower across ICE and BEV bodies using a shared 22MnB5 blank with variant-specific local patches. Cuts unique tooling and inventory while holding small-overlap and dash-intrusion targets on both premium SUV architectures.

ideaData.title: Hot-stamped A-pillar lower commonised ICE/BEV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/BEV. Baseline uses separate A-pillar lower stampings for ICE (engine-bay load path) and BEV (front-pack and frunk load path). The change defines a common 22MnB5 1500MPa hot-stamped base with bolt-on or welded local patches in DP1000 tuned per architecture. Alternatives: tailor-welded 22MnB5 blank, CP1200 roll-formed reinforcement, and 7075-T6 aluminium variant part. Physics: the shared base carries the dominant dash-cross load path identical to both layouts, while small patches adapt to differing front-rail heights, preserving small-overlap performance without duplicating major tooling.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates two hot-stamp die families into one base tool plus low-cost patch tools, reducing capital and unique part numbers. Laser-cut variant features and shared weld fixtures cut complexity. Tooling adds patch dies; framing reuses common locating. Net fewer parts, lower tooling spend and simplified logistics across ICE and BEV lines.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Shared hot-stamp base with patches replaces two unique parts.

ideaData.riskNotes: Commonising risks compromise between ICE and BEV load paths and patch-interface fatigue, mitigated by tuning patches to each crash mode and validating small-overlap and dash intrusion per variant to Euro NCAP and FMVSS 208. Torsional contribution of the cowl ring is preserved. NVH and refinement are maintained as the stiff common base lowers cowl shake, sustaining the premium driving feel of both architectures.

title: MHEV lightweight aluminium roof to offset 48V mass
description: [MHEV] Replace the steel roof panel and bows with a 6014-T4 aluminium roof on hydroformed aluminium bows, lowering centre-of-gravity mass to offset the 48V battery and belt-starter-generator. Holds roof-crush and torsional targets on the premium MHEV SUV.

ideaData.title: MHEV lightweight aluminium roof to offset 48V mass
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: MHEV. Baseline is a steel DP340 roof skin on DP590 stamped bows. The change adopts a 6014-T4 aluminium roof skin bonded to hydroformed 6082-T6 aluminium bows, fastened to the steel cant rail by FDS and adhesive. Alternatives: 6016-T4 outer panel, SMC composite roof, and magnesium AM60B bows. Physics: aluminium roof mass sits high in the body, so its reduction lowers CoG and roll inertia, directly offsetting added 48V system mass; hydroformed bows give closed sections that sustain roof-crush diaphragm action despite lower modulus by increasing section size.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6014-T4 Al roof / 6082-T6 Al bows / SMC option
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces steel stamping and welding with aluminium stamping, hydroforming and FDS/adhesive joining. Deletes spot-weld stations for the roof, adds bonding and fastening cells and an aluminium hem/seal process. Tooling adds hydroform dies. Net lower mass high in the body, fewer welds, but new joining and corrosion-isolation steps for the steel-aluminium interface.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Aluminium roof lowers CoG mass to offset 48V hardware.

ideaData.riskNotes: Steel-aluminium galvanic corrosion and differential thermal expansion at the roof seam are mitigated with bonded isolation and flexible adhesives; roof-crush and torsional stiffness are validated to FMVSS 216a despite lower modulus by section sizing. Roof modal drumming is controlled with damping and bow spacing. NVH and premium quietness are preserved, and lower CoG mass improves the refined, planted on-road comfort expected of the MHEV SUV.

title: Tailor-rolled rocker outer for graded BEV crush
description: [BEV] Form the steel rocker outer as a tailor-rolled DP1000 blank graded 1.2–2.4mm to manage side-pole crush ahead of the battery. Replaces patch reinforcements, trimming mass while protecting the BEV pack edge on the premium SUV.

ideaData.title: Tailor-rolled rocker outer for graded BEV crush
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline rocker outer is uniform DP780 with two welded DP980 patches near the pack-edge cells. The change is a tailor-rolled DP1000 blank graded 1.2mm in low-load runs to 2.4mm at side-pole impact zones, deleting both patches. Alternatives: tailor-welded DP980/HSLA blank, 22MnB5 1500MPa hot-stamped outer, and CP1200 roll-formed section. Physics: continuously variable gauge tunes the crush front so peak intrusion stays outside the battery keep-out, distributing energy along the sill while removing the mass and joints of discrete reinforcements.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces patch stamping and spot-welding with flexible rolling to variable-gauge coil feeding the stamping die. Deletes two patches and their weld stations. Tooling needs a TRB-capable stamping die and revised hemming. Net fewer parts, fewer welds, lower scrap and a cleaner sealing surface against the pack flange.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Graded gauge replaces welded crush patches at the pack edge.

ideaData.riskNotes: TRB gauge-transition strain and formability at thick steps are risks, controlled with smooth ramps and die radii; side-pole and pack-deformation cases are validated to UN ECE R100 and GB 38031. Corrosion at the pack-flange seam is addressed with e-coat and sealer. NVH improves with the stiffer rocker, preserving premium quietness, and the protected pack maintains BEV crash safety and range integrity.

title: Single-piece hot-stamped door ring for B/C-pillar zone
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace the welded A/B/C-pillar and rocker subframe with a single laser-welded hot-stamped door-ring blank. Slashes weld count and mass while improving side-impact and roof-crush load paths across the premium SUV family.

ideaData.title: Single-piece hot-stamped door ring for B/C-pillar zone
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline body side joins A-pillar, B-pillar, roof rail and rocker as separate stamped/hot-stamped parts with many flange spot welds. The change is a laser-welded tailor blank press-hardened into one 22MnB5 1500MPa door ring with graded sub-blanks (DP1000 soft zones) at energy-management areas. Alternatives: two-piece ring with DP980 nests, tailor-rolled 22MnB5 ring, and 7075-T6 aluminium hybrid frame. Physics: a continuous ring removes flange overlaps and weld discontinuities, giving a smooth load path for side-impact and roof-crush that is stiffer and lighter than a multi-part welded assembly.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces four-plus stampings and dozens of spot welds with laser-welded blanks formed in a large hot-stamp die. Deletes multiple framing welds and sub-assemblies. Tooling needs a large press-hardening die and blank-welding cell; assembly framing simplifies dramatically. Net major weld and part-count reduction with improved dimensional control of the door aperture.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One ring blank replaces a multi-part welded body side.

ideaData.riskNotes: Large hot-stamped rings risk quench distortion, weld-line softening and blanking yield loss, controlled with sub-blank grade tailoring, soft-zone placement and CAE-tuned springback. Side-impact, small-overlap and roof-crush are validated to Euro NCAP and FMVSS 214/216a. NVH and torsional stiffness improve markedly with the continuous ring, preserving the quiet, solid premium feel across all powertrain variants.

evidenceSources.title: Laser-welded door rings in premium SUVs

title: Patchwork hot-stamped A-pillar upper for thin section
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] Use a patchwork 22MnB5 blank for the A-pillar upper to keep the slim screen pillar strong for roof-crush while improving forward visibility. Removes an internal reinforcement and trims mass on the premium SUV.

ideaData.title: Patchwork hot-stamped A-pillar upper for thin section
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline A-pillar upper carries an internal DP780 reinforcement to meet roof-crush in a slim screen pillar. The change is a patchwork blank with a DP1180 martensitic patch laser-welded to a 22MnB5 1500MPa base, co-press-hardened, deleting the internal reinforcement. Alternatives: tailor-welded 22MnB5 blank, CP1200 roll-formed insert, and 7075-T6 aluminium tube. Physics: locally added patch gauge raises bending capacity in the high-moment crush zone while the base stays thin, enabling a slimmer pillar for visibility without sacrificing FMVSS 216a strength.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces a reinforcement stamping and its weld station with a laser-weld cell feeding the hot-stamp press. Co-forming patch and base deletes a sub-assembly. Tooling adds a weld fixture; the hot-stamp die handles the stepped blank. Net fewer parts, fewer welds and a thinner, lighter pillar with improved daylight opening.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Patch keeps a slim pillar strong and deletes a reinforcement.

ideaData.riskNotes: Weld-line ductility and patch-edge fatigue at the windshield header are risks, mitigated by seam placement and inspection; roof-crush is validated to FMVSS 216a with the patched section. A slimmer pillar must retain stiffness for windshield support and wind-noise control, confirmed by modal and aeroacoustic test. Premium refinement improves through better visibility and maintained low wind noise.

title: Multi-cell sill extrusion commonised across BEV wheelbases
description: [BEV] Standardise one multi-cell 6082-T6 sill extrusion profile cut to length across short- and long-wheelbase BEV SUVs. Shares pack-protection tooling and inventory while meeting side-pole intrusion on both variants of the premium BEV SUV.

ideaData.title: Multi-cell sill extrusion commonised across BEV wheelbases
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline uses two unique sill designs for short- and long-wheelbase BEV SUVs. The change defines one multi-cell 6082-T6 aluminium extrusion profile cut to length per wheelbase, with shared end-node castings. Alternatives: 6005A-T6 crash profile, AlSi10MnMg HPDC end nodes, and 22MnB5 1500MPa steel insert for the critical cell. Physics: extrusion geometry sets crush and bending behaviour independent of length, so a single profile satisfies side-pole intrusion for both wheelbases; only cut length and node interface change, eliminating duplicate die investment.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates two extrusion dies into one profile plus cut-to-length and shared end-node tools. Reduces unique part numbers, extrusion die capital and inventory lanes. Tooling reuses one die across plants; assembly nodes are common. Net lower tooling spend, simplified logistics and fewer fixtures while preserving identical pack-protection cross-section across variants.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One sill profile cut to length serves both wheelbases.

ideaData.riskNotes: A common profile risks over-mass on the short wheelbase and node-interface mismatch, mitigated by length-tuned crush initiators and shared node datums; side-pole and pack-deformation are validated per variant to UN ECE R100. Galvanic isolation protects steel fasteners. NVH and torsional stiffness are consistent across variants, preserving premium refinement, and shared tooling lowers warranty variability for the BEV line.
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN ECE R100; Euro NCAP side pole

title: Hydroformed A-pillar tube for ICE BIW stiffness
description: [ICE] Replace a stamped, welded A-pillar reinforcement assembly with a hydroformed DP780 tube giving a continuous closed section. Raises ICE BIW torsional stiffness and reduces weld count while protecting the screen-pillar load path on the premium SUV.

ideaData.title: Hydroformed A-pillar tube for ICE BIW stiffness
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE. Baseline A-pillar reinforcement is two stamped DP590 halves spot-welded into a closed box. The change is a single hydroformed DP780 tube forming a seamless closed reinforcement from cowl to roof rail. Alternatives: DP980 stamped box, 22MnB5 1500MPa hot-stamped reinforcement, and 6082-T6 aluminium extrusion. Physics: a seamless hydroformed tube has no flange spot welds, so its full perimeter resists torsion, giving higher section efficiency and torsional stiffness per kg than a welded box, improving the cowl-to-roof load path.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces two stampings and a flange spot-weld station with tube pre-bending and hydroforming, then trim. Deletes one stamping and the box-weld fixture. Tooling adds a hydroform die and high-pressure cell. Net fewer parts, fewer welds and a torsionally efficient closed section, offset by hydroform cycle time and tube cost.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Seamless tube replaces a welded box reinforcement.

ideaData.riskNotes: Hydroforming risks thinning at bends and burst at tight radii, controlled by pre-bend optimisation and pressure ramping; small-overlap and roof-crush load paths are validated to Euro NCAP and FMVSS 216a. The closed section raises torsional stiffness, sharpening on-road precision and lowering cowl shake. Corrosion inside the closed tube is managed with e-coat flooding and drain holes to preserve premium durability.

title: PHEV floor crossmember in 6082-T6 to offset pack mass
description: [PHEV] Replace stamped steel seat-crossmembers tying into the rockers with 6082-T6 aluminium extrusions, cutting floor mass to offset the PHEV battery. Maintains side-load transfer between rockers and floor on the premium SUV.

ideaData.title: PHEV floor crossmember in 6082-T6 to offset pack mass
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline seat and tunnel crossmembers are stamped DP590 boxes spot-welded between rockers, transferring side-pole load and seat anchorage. The change replaces them with 6082-T6 aluminium extrusions riveted and bonded to the rockers and tunnel. Alternatives: 6005A-T6 extrusion, AlSi10MnMg HPDC crossmember, and tailor-welded DP980 steel. Physics: extruded closed sections give high bending and shear stiffness per kg, sustaining rocker-to-rocker load transfer that protects the PHEV pack, while removing steel mass low in the body to offset battery weight and aid CoG.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces stamped steel crossmembers and spot-welding with aluminium extrusion plus SPR and adhesive to the body. Deletes stampings and weld stations; adds extrusion cut-to-length and a rivet/bond cell with corrosion isolation. Tooling adds extrusion dies. Net lower floor mass, fewer welds and integrated seat-mount features, offset by mixed-material joining steps.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Aluminium crossmembers cut floor mass to offset pack weight.

ideaData.riskNotes: Steel-aluminium galvanic corrosion and SPR fatigue under seat and crash loads are mitigated with isolation coatings and validated joints; side-pole transfer and seat-anchorage pull are confirmed to FMVSS 207/210 and ECE side impact. Floor modal stiffness is preserved to avoid boom, sustaining premium quietness. Lower floor mass aids ride and handling, offsetting PHEV battery weight for refined on-road comfort.

title: Hot-stamped B-pillar with laser-tailored hardness bands
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Apply laser-tailored tempering to the hot-stamped B-pillar, creating soft lower and hard upper bands to control side-impact folding without a separate reinforcement. Trims mass while improving occupant survival space across the premium SUV line.

ideaData.title: Hot-stamped B-pillar with laser-tailored hardness bands
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline B-pillar uses a uniform-hardness 22MnB5 shell plus a welded DP980 reinforcement to tune the fold line. The change applies in-die or laser tailored tempering to one 22MnB5 1500MPa pillar, leaving a soft ~600MPa band at the rocker join and full martensite at the belt line, deleting the reinforcement. Alternatives: tailor-rolled 22MnB5 blank, patchwork DP1180 blank, and 7075-T6 aluminium reinforcement. Physics: graded hardness fixes the plastic hinge low and keeps the upper occupant zone rigid, optimising intrusion shape in side-pole impact without redundant reinforcement mass.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces reinforcement stamping and spot-welding with tailored tempering integrated in hot stamping. Deletes the reinforcement and its weld station. Tooling adds tailored quench segments or a laser-temper cell. Net fewer parts, fewer welds and a single tuned pillar, offset by added process control for hardness banding and inspection.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Tailored hardness deletes the fold-tuning reinforcement.

ideaData.riskNotes: Hardness-band placement tolerance and soft-zone fatigue are risks, controlled by closed-loop temper control and CAE-correlated sled tests for side-pole intrusion to Euro NCAP and FMVSS 214. Eliminating the reinforcement must not reduce belt-line stiffness, verified by modal and crash analysis. NVH and torsional contribution are preserved, sustaining the quiet, solid premium feel across all powertrain variants.

title: Composite SMC roof panel to cut mass and tooling
description: [MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace the steel roof skin with a bonded SMC composite panel on steel bows, lowering high-CoG mass to offset electrified-driveline weight. Holds roof modal and crush targets while reducing roof tooling on the premium SUV.

ideaData.title: Composite SMC roof panel to cut mass and tooling
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline roof skin is a DP340 steel stamping welded to the bows. The change is a low-density SMC (glass-filled) roof panel bonded to the steel DP590 bows with structural adhesive and FDS at the perimeter. Alternatives: GFRP press-moulded panel, 6014-T4 aluminium skin, and CFRP for halo trims. Physics: replacing high-mounted steel with composite removes mass at the highest body point, lowering CoG and roll inertia to offset electrified mass; bonded perimeter transfers crush diaphragm loads to the steel bows, which still set roof-crush strength.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces steel stamping and roof spot-welding with SMC compression moulding and adhesive bonding. Deletes the roof-skin stamping die and weld station; adds a moulding press and bonding/curing cell. Net lower mass high in the body, fewer welds, no roof-ditch sealing, offset by composite cycle time and surface-prep for paint or unpainted-with-trim strategy.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Composite roof cuts high-CoG mass and a stamping die.

ideaData.riskNotes: Composite-to-steel thermal-expansion mismatch and bond durability over paint-oven and service cycles are risks, mitigated by flexible adhesives and read-through control for class-A. Roof crush is held by the steel bows, validated to FMVSS 216a. Roof drumming and hail-dent resistance are tuned in the laminate. NVH and premium refinement improve through lower CoG and added panel damping, preserving the calm cabin character.

title: Roll-formed roof bow family to delete stamping dies
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Produce all roof bows by roll forming DP780 closed sections cut to length, eliminating multiple stamping dies. Reduces tooling and scrap while maintaining roof-crush diaphragm action on the premium SUV across powertrains.

ideaData.title: Roll-formed roof bow family to delete stamping dies
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline roof bows are stamped DP590 open hat sections needing several dies. The change roll-forms a DP780 closed profile cut to length with end-feature laser cutting, serving all bow positions. Alternatives: 22MnB5 1500MPa hot-stamped bow, 6082-T6 aluminium extrusion, and DP980 stamped bow. Physics: a closed roll-formed section gives higher torsional and bending stiffness per kg than an open hat, sustaining roof-crush diaphragm and panoramic-roof support, while continuous forming removes per-part stamping dies and slashes scrap.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces multiple stamping dies with one roll-forming line plus laser end-features and cut-to-length. Deletes several die sets and reduces scrap from near-net forming. Tooling adds roll-form rollers and a sweep unit. Net lower tooling capital, fewer unique parts and a stiffer closed bow, with simple length changes covering all positions.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Roll forming replaces several stamping dies with one line.

ideaData.riskNotes: Roll-formed end-flare and length-cut burr can affect bow-to-rail fit, controlled by sweep calibration and laser trimming; roof-crush diaphragm and panoramic support are validated to FMVSS 216a. Closed sections raise stiffness, reducing roof drumming for premium quietness. Corrosion inside the closed bow is managed with e-coat flooding and drains, preserving long-term refinement across all powertrain variants.

title: BEV sill with integrated multi-cell crush and cooling rib
description: [BEV] Adopt a multi-cell 6082-T6 sill extrusion whose inner cell doubles as a coolant or fire-barrier channel, consolidating sill and pack-edge functions. Cuts part count and joints while meeting side-pole intrusion and thermal-propagation limits on the premium BEV SUV.

ideaData.title: BEV sill with integrated multi-cell crush and cooling rib
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline separates the steel sill, a crush bracket and a discrete pack-edge thermal barrier. The change is a single multi-cell 6082-T6 aluminium sill extrusion where outer cells manage side-pole crush and an inner sealed cell carries coolant or acts as a fire/thermal barrier toward the pack. Alternatives: 6005A-T6 crash profile with bolt-on barrier, AlSi10MnMg HPDC end nodes, and 22MnB5 1500MPa steel insert. Physics: cell topology decouples energy absorption (outer cells) from intrusion limiting and thermal isolation (inner cell), protecting the battery from both mechanical crush and propagation in one part.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces a steel sill, crush bracket and barrier with one extrusion plus FSW end nodes and bonding. Deletes stampings, brackets, the barrier part and their welds/fasteners. Tooling adds an extrusion die and FSW fixtures. Net major part-count and joint reduction with integrated thermal function, offset by extrusion and leak-test of the sealed cell.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One extrusion integrates crush, intrusion and thermal barrier.

ideaData.riskNotes: Sealing the inner coolant/barrier cell and FSW node leak-tightness are risks, controlled by weld-seam inspection and pressure testing; side-pole, ground-strike and thermal-propagation cases are validated to UN ECE R100 and GB 38031. Off-road sill loading and stone impact are confirmed. NVH improves with the stiff closed sill, preserving premium quietness, while integrated thermal protection enhances BEV safety and reduces warranty exposure.

title: Tailor-welded D-pillar blank for G-class style aperture
description: [ICE,MHEV] Form the D-pillar from a tailor-welded blank pairing DP980 at the roof-rail node with HSLA S420 mid-span, deleting a reinforcement. Trims mass while sustaining roof-crush and tailgate-aperture stiffness on the premium SUV.

ideaData.title: Tailor-welded D-pillar blank for G-class style aperture
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline D-pillar is a DP590 stamping with a welded DP980 reinforcement at the roof-rail and tailgate-hinge node. The change is a laser tailor-welded blank combining DP980 at the upper node with HSLA S420 in the mid-span, formed as one pillar. Alternatives: tailor-rolled DP1000 blank, 22MnB5 1500MPa hot-stamped pillar, and AM60B magnesium inner. Physics: concentrating high-strength grade at the moment-critical roof-rail node lets the mid-span gauge fall, cutting mass while preserving the rear-ring stiffness governing roof-crush and tailgate-aperture deflection.
ideaData.materialGrade: DP980 + HSLA S420 TWB / DP1000 TRB / 22MnB5 1500MPa
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces a reinforcement stamping and spot-welding with laser blank welding feeding one stamping die. Deletes the reinforcement and its weld station. Tooling reuses the stamping line with an upstream TWB cell. Net fewer parts, fewer welds and lower scrap from grade-matched nesting, with a stiffer single-piece rear pillar.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Grade placement at the node deletes a reinforcement.

ideaData.riskNotes: TWB weld-line formability and fatigue at the grade transition are risks, controlled by seam placement in low-strain zones and weld monitoring. Roof-crush, tailgate-slam and rear-impact integrity are validated to FMVSS 216a/301. Rear-ring torsional stiffness is preserved to limit tailgate-aperture shake, sustaining premium refinement and a solid tailgate close on this boxy, upright SUV body.

title: Patchwork rocker reinforcement to cut BEV pack-edge welds
description: [BEV] Use a patchwork hot-stamped rocker reinforcement, laser-welding DP1180 patches onto a 22MnB5 base at side-pole zones, deleting two bolt-on parts. Protects the BEV pack while reducing welds and mass on the premium SUV.

ideaData.title: Patchwork rocker reinforcement to cut BEV pack-edge welds
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline rocker reinforcement is a 22MnB5 shell with two spot-welded DP980 patches at the side-pole impact cells protecting the pack. The change is a patchwork blank: DP1180 martensitic patches laser-welded onto a 22MnB5 1500MPa base pre-forming, co-press-hardened as one part. Alternatives: tailor-rolled 22MnB5 blank, CP1200 roll-formed insert, and 7075-T6 aluminium reinforcement. Physics: locally doubled gauge from the welded patches creates a stiff backstop limiting lateral intrusion toward the battery keep-out, while the surrounding base stays light.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces two reinforcement stampings and their spot-weld stations with a laser-weld cell feeding the hot-stamp press. Co-forming patches and base deletes sub-assemblies. Tooling adds a weld fixture; the hot-stamp die handles the stepped blank. Net fewer parts, fewer welds and tighter dimensional control of the pack-flange sealing surface.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Welded patches replace two bolt-on pack-edge reinforcements.

ideaData.riskNotes: Weld-line ductility and patch-edge fatigue under side-load cycles are risks, controlled by seam placement and post-quench inspection; side-pole and pack-deformation cases are validated to UN ECE R100 and GB 38031. Corrosion at the pack-flange seam is addressed with e-coat and sealer. NVH improves with the stiffer rocker, preserving premium quietness, and the protected pack maintains BEV crash safety and range.

title: Commonised C-pillar hot-stamp across SWB/LWB SUVs
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Commonise the hot-stamped C-pillar between short- and long-wheelbase SUVs using a shared 22MnB5 blank with bolt-on glass-aperture patches. Cuts unique tooling and inventory while holding rear side-impact targets across the premium SUV family.

ideaData.title: Commonised C-pillar hot-stamp across SWB/LWB SUVs
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline uses unique C-pillars for SWB and LWB SUVs differing by quarter-glass aperture. The change shares one 22MnB5 1500MPa hot-stamped base with variant-specific DP1000 aperture patches and bracket sets. Alternatives: tailor-welded 22MnB5 blank, CP1200 roll-formed reinforcement, and 7075-T6 aluminium variant part. Physics: the dominant rear side-impact and belt-load paths are identical between wheelbases, so a common base satisfies both; only the glass-aperture local stiffness changes via patches, avoiding duplicate hot-stamp tooling.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates two hot-stamp die families into one base tool plus low-cost patch tools, cutting capital and unique part numbers. Laser-cut variant apertures and shared weld fixtures reduce complexity. Tooling adds patch dies; framing reuses common datums. Net fewer parts, lower tooling spend and simplified logistics across wheelbase variants.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Shared C-pillar base with patches replaces two unique parts.

ideaData.riskNotes: Commonising risks over-design for SWB and aperture-patch fatigue, mitigated by tuning patches to each glass cut-out and validating rear side-impact per variant to Euro NCAP and FMVSS 214. Rear-ring torsional stiffness is preserved to limit tailgate-aperture shake. NVH and premium refinement are maintained as the common stiff base reduces rear wind noise across both wheelbases.

title: ICE BIW rocker-to-floor bonded joint for stiffness
description: [ICE,MHEV] Add structural adhesive along the rocker-to-floor flange with reduced spot welds (weld-bonding), raising ICE BIW torsional stiffness and fatigue life. Cuts weld count and NVH sealer while improving the lower-body load path on the premium SUV.

ideaData.title: ICE BIW rocker-to-floor bonded joint for stiffness
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline rocker-to-floor flange uses dense DP590 spot welds plus separate NVH sealer. The change is weld-bonding: a continuous structural epoxy bead with a reduced spot-weld pitch along the flange. Alternatives: full structural bonding with rivets, laser stitch welding, and self-pierce riveting with adhesive. Physics: continuous adhesive shear coupling raises joint stiffness and damping versus discrete welds, increasing global torsional stiffness and fatigue life of the lower body while the remaining welds fixture the bond during cure.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Adds an adhesive-dispense pass and reduces spot-weld count along the flange, with welds acting as cure fixtures. Deletes a separate NVH sealer where adhesive seals the seam. Tooling adds a dispense robot; weld guns are reduced. Net fewer welds, integrated sealing and higher joint performance, offset by adhesive cost and oven cure time.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Weld-bonding raises stiffness and deletes separate sealer.

ideaData.riskNotes: Adhesive bondline thickness control and oil-surface adhesion are risks, mitigated by validated wash, bead-quality monitoring and crash-durable epoxy selection. Torsional stiffness and lower-body fatigue improve, sharpening on-road handling precision and reducing structural NVH for premium quietness. Crash load paths are preserved as welds plus cured adhesive exceed baseline weld-only strength, maintaining occupant protection and durability.

title: Tailor-rolled A-pillar reinforcement for PHEV lightweighting
description: [PHEV] Replace a constant-gauge A-pillar reinforcement with a tailor-rolled DP1000 blank graded 1.0–2.0mm, shedding mass to offset PHEV battery weight. Maintains small-overlap and roof-crush load paths on the premium SUV.

ideaData.title: Tailor-rolled A-pillar reinforcement for PHEV lightweighting
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline A-pillar reinforcement is uniform 1.8mm DP780. The change is a tailor-rolled DP1000 blank graded from 1.0mm at low-load runs to 2.0mm at the cowl and header nodes, cutting mass to help offset PHEV pack weight. Alternatives: tailor-welded DP980/HSLA blank, 22MnB5 1500MPa hot-stamped reinforcement, and 7075-T6 aluminium extrusion. Physics: continuously variable gauge matches the bending-moment distribution from cowl to header, removing surplus mass in low-stress runs while keeping section capacity at the small-overlap and roof-crush nodes.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces constant-gauge blanking with flexible rolling to variable-gauge coil feeding the stamping die. No new welds; gauge follows the load path. Tooling needs a TRB-capable die and revised hemming. Net lower mass, lower scrap from optimised nesting and no added joints, offset by TRB coil premium.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Graded gauge sheds A-pillar mass to offset PHEV pack weight.

ideaData.riskNotes: TRB gauge-transition strain and formability at steps are risks, controlled by smooth ramps and die radii; small-overlap and roof-crush are validated to Euro NCAP and FMVSS 216a. Lower pillar mass aids CoG and offsets PHEV battery weight without reducing node stiffness. Torsional contribution and cowl-shake control are preserved, sustaining the refined, planted on-road comfort expected of the premium SUV.

title: Hot-stamped roof rail outer to delete two reinforcements
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace a stamped roof-rail outer plus two reinforcements with one hot-stamped 22MnB5 rail integrating roof-crush strength. Reduces parts and welds while raising torsional stiffness across the premium SUV line.

ideaData.title: Hot-stamped roof rail outer to delete two reinforcements
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline roof-rail outer is a stamped DP590 shell with two spot-welded HSLA reinforcements for roof-crush and roof-rack loads. The change is a single hot-stamped 22MnB5 1500MPa rail outer that integrates both reinforcement functions. Alternatives: CP1000 roll-formed rail, DP980 stamped outer, and 6082-T6 aluminium extrusion. Physics: the press-hardened high-strength shell delivers the section capacity previously shared with reinforcements, so deleting them cuts mass and welds while a single closed-loop rail raises torsional stiffness of the greenhouse.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces a stamping and two reinforcement spot-weld stations with one hot-stamping operation and laser trim. Deletes two reinforcement stampings and their welds. Tooling adds a press-hardening die; framing simplifies. Net fewer parts, fewer welds and improved dimensional control of the roof-bow and windshield interfaces.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One hot-stamped rail replaces an outer plus two reinforcements.

ideaData.riskNotes: Press-hardened rails risk quench distortion and reduced weld ductility, controlled by die design, soft-zone placement at welds and CAE-tuned springback. Roof-crush and roof-rack moments are validated to FMVSS 216a and ECE roof-load. Torsional stiffness rises with the integrated rail, lowering door-aperture deflection and wind noise, preserving the premium quietness and handling precision across all powertrain variants.

title: BEV structural-pack sill flange for load-sharing stiffness
description: [BEV] Bolt-bond the structural battery pack to a stiffened 6082-T6 sill flange so the pack contributes to body torsion, letting the sill and floor gauge down. Cuts BIW mass while protecting the pack in side-pole impact on the premium BEV SUV.

ideaData.title: BEV structural-pack sill flange for load-sharing stiffness
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline treats the pack as non-structural, so the sill and floor carry all torsion, demanding heavy sections. The change makes the pack a stressed member: a stiffened 6082-T6 sill flange is bolted and structurally bonded to the pack lid, sharing torsional and side-pole loads, allowing the sill and floor cross-sections to thin. Alternatives: AlSi10MnMg HPDC sill flange, 22MnB5 1500MPa steel flange, and CFRP pack-lid skin. Physics: coupling the pack enclosure into the load path increases effective beam depth of the floor, raising torsional stiffness per kg and letting body gauge fall while the pack edge is shielded by the sill crush cells.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Adds a bonded, bolted structural interface between sill flange and pack lid, with SPR and adhesive, while thinning sill and floor stampings/extrusions. Deletes redundant floor reinforcements enabled by load-sharing. Tooling adds bonding and torque-sequence fixtures. Net lower BIW mass and part count, offset by pack-interface sealing, isolation and serviceability provisions.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Structural pack lets sill and floor gauge down.

ideaData.riskNotes: Making the pack structural risks load-path coupling into the battery and serviceability/sealing challenges, mitigated by validated bonded-bolted interfaces, isolation and crash decoupling so the pack is shielded by sill crush cells. Side-pole, torsion and pack-integrity cases are validated to UN ECE R100 and GB 38031. Torsional stiffness rises, sharpening handling and lowering NVH, preserving premium refinement and BEV crash safety.

title: Magnesium roof bow to cut high-CoG mass for MHEV
description: [MHEV,PHEV] Replace steel roof bows with AM60B magnesium die-castings, removing mass at the body's highest point to offset electrified mass. Maintains roof-crush diaphragm action and modal targets on the premium SUV.

ideaData.title: Magnesium roof bow to cut high-CoG mass for MHEV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: MHEV/PHEV. Baseline roof bows are stamped DP590 hat sections welded to the rails. The change is AM60B magnesium die-cast bows with integrated harness and trim attachment, fastened to the steel rails by FDS and adhesive. Alternatives: AE44 creep-resistant magnesium, 6082-T6 aluminium extrusion, and SMC composite bow. Physics: magnesium's low density removes the largest possible mass at the highest body point, cutting CoG and roll inertia to offset electrified hardware; ribbed casting topology recovers the stiffness needed for roof-crush diaphragm and modal control despite lower modulus.
ideaData.materialGrade: AM60B Mg HPDC / AE44 Mg / 6082-T6 Al
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces stamping and roof spot-welding with magnesium die-casting and FDS/adhesive joining. Deletes bow stampings and weld stations; adds an HPDC die, trim and a fastening cell with galvanic isolation. Tooling shifts to casting. Net lower high-CoG mass and integrated features, offset by magnesium material, coating and corrosion-management cost.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Magnesium bows cut the highest-CoG mass in the body.

ideaData.riskNotes: Magnesium galvanic corrosion at steel interfaces and creep under roof loads are risks, mitigated with conversion coating, isolation and AE44 in hot or high-load zones. Roof-crush diaphragm and roof modal drumming are validated to FMVSS 216a. Reduced high-CoG mass improves roll behaviour and refinement; bonded joints add damping, preserving premium quietness and the calm cabin character of the SUV.

title: Tailor-welded A/B/C reinforcement family for commonisation
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Rationalise A/B/C-pillar reinforcements into one tailor-welded blank family of DP980/HSLA strips, shared across body sides and powertrains. Cuts unique parts and tooling while holding side-impact and roof-crush targets on the premium SUV.

ideaData.title: Tailor-welded A/B/C reinforcement family for commonisation
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline uses distinct stamped reinforcements per pillar and per body variant, proliferating part numbers. The change defines one tailor-welded blank reinforcement family pairing DP980 high-load segments with HSLA S420 low-load runs, sharing weld layouts and grades across A/B/C pillars. Alternatives: tailor-rolled DP1000 blanks, 22MnB5 1500MPa hot-stamped reinforcements, and 7075-T6 aluminium inserts. Physics: a shared grade-and-geometry logic places strength by load path consistently, so pillar-specific needs are met by changing segment lengths within one blank concept, reducing unique tooling without sacrificing crash capacity.
ideaData.materialGrade: DP980 + HSLA S420 TWB family / DP1000 TRB / 22MnB5
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates many reinforcement stampings into a TWB family run on shared blank-weld and stamping tooling, cutting unique dies and part numbers. Common weld fixtures and grades simplify framing. Tooling reuses lines across pillars. Net major part-number and tooling reduction with consistent grade placement, offset by TWB weld-cell scheduling complexity.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One TWB reinforcement family replaces many unique parts.

ideaData.riskNotes: A common family risks over-design for light load paths and weld-line fatigue across varied duty, mitigated by segment-length tuning and seam placement in low-strain zones. Side-impact, small-overlap and roof-crush are validated per pillar to Euro NCAP and FMVSS 214/216a. Torsional contribution of each ring is preserved. NVH and premium refinement are maintained as consistent reinforcement stiffness controls aperture deflection across all powertrains.

title: Forged 6082 rocker end-fitting to delete welded brackets
description: [BEV] Replace a stack of welded steel rocker end-brackets with a forged 6082-T6 aluminium fitting integrating the jack point, tow-eye and pack-mount interface. Cuts part count and joints while securing side-pole and pack loads on the premium BEV SUV.

ideaData.title: Forged 6082 rocker end-fitting to delete welded brackets
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline rocker end is several stamped DP980 brackets plus a cast jack point, spot-welded and bolted, feeding side-pole and pack loads into the front/rear structure. The change is one forged 6082-T6 aluminium end-fitting integrating the jack point, tow-eye boss and pack-mount interface. Alternatives: AlSi10MnMg HPDC node, A356-T6 low-pressure casting, and 7075-T6 forged fitting. Physics: forging gives high, defect-free strength and fatigue capacity, letting a single fitting carry concentrated side-pole and pack-attachment loads through optimised flow lines, replacing a multi-bracket welded stack with one stiff, precise part.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates several stampings and a cast jack point into one forging, deleting spot welds and bolted brackets. Tooling shifts to forging dies and CNC finishing; assembly uses SPR/bonding to the sill extrusion. Net major part-count and weld reduction with superior fatigue strength and tight pack-mount datums, offset by forging tool and machining cost.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One forging replaces a welded multi-bracket end-fitting.

ideaData.riskNotes: Forged aluminium fatigue at the tow-eye and galvanic isolation to steel fasteners are key risks, controlled by flow-line-aligned design, T6 temper and isolation coatings; side-pole, towing and pack pull-off are validated to UN ECE R100 and ECE R55. NVH improves with the stiff precise fitting, preserving premium isolation, and repeatable pack datums maintain BEV crash margins and low warranty exposure.
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN ECE R100; ECE R55 towing

title: BEV pack cross-member integrated into floor as load path
description: [BEV] Delete redundant body floor cross-members by structurally sharing the battery-tray transverse beams as the primary lateral load path. The pack upper plate becomes a stressed member, removing two stamped floor cross-members and their welds while preserving side-pole intrusion limits and seat-mount stiffness for a premium skateboard SUV.

ideaData.title: BEV pack cross-member integrated into floor as load path
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline carries discrete stamped floor cross-members (HSLA S420) plus a separate aluminium battery tray with its own internal cross-beams, duplicating the lateral load path. The change bolts the body to the tray so the AlSi10MnMg HPDC tray cross-beams and a sealed upper plate carry side-impact load, deleting two body cross-members. Alternatives evaluated: 6082-T6 extruded tray beams, DP980 AHSS roll-formed body beams, and 22MnB5 1500MPa boron hot-stamped tunnel reinforcement retained for tying load into rockers. Physics: shared shear plane raises effective floor section modulus, holding B-pillar/rocker intrusion within Euro NCAP side-pole targets while shedding mass.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Combines HPDC tray cross-beams with tailor-welded blank floor; eliminates two stamped cross-members, ~14 spot welds and one structural adhesive bead. Alternatives: low-pressure cast tray vs 6082-T6 extrusion-welded frame. Reduces BOM part count, frees one stamping die set, simplifies sealer robot path and cuts body-shop cycle time.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Function-sharing the pack as a stressed floor member removes duplicate structure.

ideaData.riskNotes: Stressed-pack integration must preserve side-pole intrusion velocity and seat-cross stiffness; durability risk from tray fastener fretting under torsional fatigue is mitigated with sleeved M10 bolts and structural adhesive. NVH booming managed by tuned tray damping mat. Crash load path validated to Euro NCAP side-pole; premium ride retained as floor torsional stiffness rises 6%, preserving handling precision expected of a luxury BEV SUV.

title: PHEV pack-zone underbody torsion-box gauge optimisation
description: [PHEV] Re-gauge and tailor-weld the underfloor torsion box surrounding the PHEV battery to remove blanket over-thickness added for pack mass. Targeted DP780/DP980 blanks replace uniform HSLA panels, cutting mass and material spend while sustaining e-axle mounting stiffness and rear-crash pack protection on a luxury plug-in SUV.

ideaData.title: PHEV pack-zone underbody torsion-box gauge optimisation
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline underbody uses uniform 1.8mm HSLA S355 panels thickened globally to react ~120kg pack and e-axle loads. The change deploys tailor-welded blanks pairing DP780 (1.2mm field) with DP980 (1.6mm pack-rail zone) and 22MnB5 1500MPa boron at the rear longitudinal-to-torsion-box node. Alternatives: 6014-T4 aluminium floor (rejected on joining cost), CP1000 complex-phase for bend zones, HSLA S420 base. Physics: localising gauge where pack inertia loads peak keeps first torsion mode above 30Hz and maintains rear-crash crush stability while removing material from low-stress field areas.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Tailor-welded laser blanks replace single-gauge stampings; alternatives roll forming for rails vs hot stamping boron node. Cuts ~1.9kg/veh and trims coil spend. Adds one laser-weld blanking step but removes a reinforcement patch and 6 spot welds, lowering assembly and tooling amortisation.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Tailored gauge removes blanket over-thickness carried only for pack mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: Localised de-gauging must not lower pack-zone bending stiffness or rear offset-crash crush resistance; mitigated by DP980 reinforcement at e-axle mounts and boron node tuning. Fatigue at TWB weld line validated to 300k-cycle road load. NVH boom risk from thinner field panels offset by laser-welded patch and damping. Premium refinement preserved; rear-impact pack intrusion stays within UN/ECE R94/R95 limits.

title: ICE front longitudinal crush-initiator bead optimisation
description: [ICE,MHEV] Replace machined crush triggers and added doublers in the front longitudinal rails with stamped crush-initiator beads tuned for stable axial folding. Eliminates a welded trigger bracket on ICE/48V MHEV variants while holding ODB and full-frontal pulse, reducing part count and weld content on a luxury SUV front structure.

ideaData.title: ICE front longitudinal crush-initiator bead optimisation
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline front rails use DP780 with a separately welded machined crush-trigger and reinforcing doubler to force progressive folding. The change integrates rolled crush-initiator beads directly into the DP780 rail with a CP1000 cap at the engine-mount node. Alternatives: 22MnB5 1500MPa boron tip (rejected — too stiff for axial fold), 6082-T6 extruded crush can, HSLA S420 base rail. Physics: geometric imperfection beads set fold wavelength, achieving stable accordion collapse and target mean crush force without a discrete trigger, maintaining occupant compartment pulse and dash intrusion limits.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Bead forming integrated into existing stamping die; alternatives roll forming the rail vs hydroforming for closed section. Deletes one machined trigger, one doubler and ~8 spot welds per side. Lowers BOM, removes a machining op and reduces fixture complexity in front-end sub-assembly.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Stamped fold initiators replace machined triggers and doublers.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crush-bead geometry must guarantee axial (not bending) collapse across temperature and tolerance; validated by ODB 64km/h and full-frontal pulse plus dynamic component crush tests. Risk of fold instability mitigated with CP1000 node cap and bead-depth DOE. NVH/stiffness for 48V MHEV front mass retained via section tuning. Premium impact harshness and crash compatibility preserved within UN/ECE R94 and IIHS small-overlap intent.

title: Off-road skid-plate integrated into underbody load path
description: [ICE,MHEV] Convert the bolt-on aluminium skid plate into a structural load-sharing member tied to the front cross-member, deleting a separate underbody brace. On body-on-frame luxury off-roaders the skid plate then carries grounding and recovery loads, cutting a stamped brace and fasteners while improving approach-angle protection.

ideaData.title: Off-road skid-plate integrated into underbody load path
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline uses a sacrificial 6mm AlMg skid plate plus a separate HSLA S420 underbody brace for grounding loads. The change thickens and ribs the skid plate as a 6082-T6 structural member bolted to the front cross-member, deleting the brace. Alternatives: AM60B/AE44 magnesium die-cast plate (rejected on impact toughness), SMC/GFRP composite belly pan, 22MnB5 boron stamped pan. Physics: stiffened plate distributes point grounding loads into the frame cross-member, raising local crush resistance and protecting steering/sump while sharing the brace's structural function.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Structural skid plate via low-pressure casting or thick-gauge stamping; alternatives forging the mount lugs vs HPDC ribbed pan. Deletes one stamped brace, 8 M8 fasteners and a weld nut set. Reduces BOM and underbody assembly time; one extra machining op for mount faces.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Skid plate absorbs the brace's structural duty via function sharing.

ideaData.riskNotes: Plate must survive repeated grounding and recovery-point loads without transmitting damaging shock into the frame; mitigated by 6082-T6 ribbing and energy-absorbing mount bushes. Off-road durability validated to corrugation and rock-strike load cases; corrosion managed by anodising. On-road NVH from added stiffness controlled with isolating mounts. Premium off-road capability and approach-angle protection preserved; no degradation to ramp breakover.

title: Rear crash-box extrusion commonised across SUV variants
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Commonise a single 6082-T6 aluminium rear crush-can/crash-box design across ICE, PHEV and BEV derivatives by tuning wall gauge rather than redesigning per variant. One extrusion tool and one bumper-beam interface serve all powertrains, cutting tooling and complexity while meeting rear-impact and fuel/pack integrity requirements.

ideaData.title: Rear crash-box extrusion commonised across SUV variants
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline runs three rear crash-box variants with separate extrusion dies to suit differing rear masses (fuel tank vs PHEV pack vs BEV pack). The change defines one 6082-T6 multi-cell extrusion with gauge/length tuning per variant and a shared DP980 rear bumper-beam mount. Alternatives: 7075-T6 high-strength crush can (rejected — unstable folding), AlSi10MnMg HPDC corner casting, 22MnB5 boron beam. Physics: multi-cell extrusion gives consistent mean crush force; per-variant tuning via wall thickness preserves rear-crash energy management and BEV pack stand-off across the range.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Single extrusion die with variant gauge; alternatives roll-formed steel beam vs HPDC corner node. Collapses three crash-box part numbers to one family, removing two die sets. Standardises bumper-beam interface, cutting fixture variants and simplifying rear-end assembly logistics.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One crash-box family across powertrains removes duplicate tooling.

ideaData.riskNotes: Single design must satisfy the heaviest (BEV) rear mass for crush force while not over-stiffening lighter ICE pulse; mitigated by gauge tuning and validated rear 80km/h impact plus pack/fuel integrity. Tow-load fatigue at beam interface checked to durability spec. NVH from common mount tuned per variant. Premium rear refinement and crash performance preserved across all derivatives under UN/ECE R34/R32.

title: BEV floor cross-member mega-casting consolidation
description: [BEV] Replace a multi-piece stamped-and-welded floor cross-member assembly with a single AlSi10MnMg high-pressure mega-casting integrating seat mounts and pack attach points. On a structural-pack BEV SUV this removes numerous stampings and welds, simplifying the floor while maintaining side-impact stiffness and seat anchorage strength.

ideaData.title: BEV floor cross-member mega-casting consolidation
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline floor cross-member is a 9-piece DP780/HSLA stamped-and-spot-welded assembly with bolted seat brackets. The change casts it as one AlSi10MnMg HPDC node with integrated seat mounts and pack-bolt bosses. Alternatives: 6082-T6 extrusion-plus-node hybrid, AE44 magnesium casting (rejected on corrosion/cost), DP980 stamped baseline. Physics: monolithic casting removes weld-flange compliance, raising lateral stiffness and providing direct, stiff load paths from rocker to tunnel; cast-in bosses give repeatable seat anchorage and pack clamp load for side-pole protection.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Single HPDC replaces 9 stampings; alternatives low-pressure casting vs extrusion-welded hybrid. Eliminates ~30 spot welds, 4 bolted brackets and two stamping dies. Cuts body-shop weld stations and BOM lines; adds one large casting cell and machining of mount faces and datum.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Mega-casting consolidates a 9-piece weldment into one node.

ideaData.riskNotes: Cast ductility at seat-anchor and pack-bolt bosses must meet pull-out and side-pole loads; mitigated by AlSi10MnMg with controlled porosity and T7 temper plus local thickening. Castings reduce inspectable weld joints but require porosity NDT. Crash side-pole and seatbelt-anchor pull validated; NVH improved by removed flange compliance. Premium seat solidity and side-impact protection preserved per UN/ECE R95 and R14 anchorage.

title: PHEV front crash box re-tuned for added e-axle mass
description: [PHEV] Re-tune front crash boxes and lower load-path rails specifically for PHEV front-mass (engine plus front e-machine) instead of carrying a heavier ICE-derived box. DP980 roll-formed cans replace over-built stampings, matching crush force to the actual PHEV pulse and trimming mass while holding small-overlap and full-frontal performance.

ideaData.title: PHEV front crash box re-tuned for added e-axle mass
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline reuses the ICE front crash box, over-sized for a lighter front mass case, adding weight and crush force mismatch. The change roll-forms a DP980 multi-cell can sized to PHEV front inertia with a 22MnB5 1500MPa boron tip bracket. Alternatives: 6082-T6 extruded can, CP1000 stamped can, AlSi10MnMg cast crush node. Physics: matching mean crush force to PHEV deceleration profile keeps compartment pulse and dash intrusion within target while removing surplus material; boron tip stabilises initial fold under small-overlap loading.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Roll-formed crash can replaces deep-drawn stamping; alternatives hydroforming closed can vs aluminium extrusion. Trims ~1.4kg/side and reduces draw-die wear. Common bolt interface to rail retained, so no new fixtures; deletes one doubler and 4 welds per side.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Right-sizing crush force to PHEV mass removes surplus material.

ideaData.riskNotes: Down-tuned can must still manage worst-case loaded PHEV mass and trailer tongue effects; validated by small-overlap, full-frontal and ODB with pack/HV integrity checks. Risk of premature fold offset by boron tip and roll-form work-hardening. NVH/stiffness for front e-axle mounting preserved via rail section. Premium frontal refinement and crash compatibility maintained under UN/ECE R94 and IIHS small-overlap.

evidenceSources.title: PHEV front-end mass and crush tuning

title: Front bumper beam switched to hot-stamped boron
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace a roll-formed dual-phase steel front bumper beam with a hot-stamped 22MnB5 boron beam, cutting wall thickness and mass at equal bending strength. Lower mass at the vehicle extremity benefits all powertrains, improving low-speed crash and pedestrian targets while trimming material on a premium SUV.

ideaData.title: Front bumper beam switched to hot-stamped boron
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline is a 1.8mm DP780 roll-formed beam. The change hot-stamps a 1.3mm 22MnB5 1500MPa boron beam with tailored soft zones at the crush-can interface. Alternatives: 7075-T6 aluminium extrusion, DP980 roll-form, AlSi10MnMg cast end-caps. Physics: higher yield strength lets thinner section meet RCAR low-speed and bending-moment targets; tailored soft zones manage energy into crush cans, while reduced extremity mass lowers yaw inertia, aiding handling response valued in luxury SUVs.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Hot stamping with in-die soft-zone tailoring replaces roll forming; alternatives aluminium extrusion vs press hardening. Cuts ~1.6kg, reduces coil width. Adds press-hardening line time but removes a reinforcing insert and 6 welds; bolt interface to crush can unchanged.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Higher-strength boron enables thinner, lighter beam at equal strength.

ideaData.riskNotes: Thinner boron beam must hold RCAR 15km/h low-speed repairability and pedestrian leg-form loads; mitigated by tailored soft zones and crush-can tuning. Hydrogen-embrittlement and corrosion risk managed with AlSi coating. NVH from lighter extremity neutral. Premium handling improved by lower yaw inertia; pedestrian and low-speed crash targets preserved under UN/ECE R42, R127 pedestrian and Euro NCAP.
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN/ECE R42; R127 pedestrian; Euro NCAP

title: BEV battery-tray cross-members as roll-formed extrusions
description: [BEV] Replace cast battery-tray cross-members with 6082-T6 aluminium extrusions friction-stir welded to the tray rails, cutting casting cost and porosity scrap on a BEV SUV pack. Extruded cross-members give consistent crush behaviour for side-pole protection while lowering tooling spend versus dedicated casting dies.

ideaData.title: BEV battery-tray cross-members as roll-formed extrusions
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline tray uses AlSi10MnMg cast cross-members with variable porosity and high die cost. The change extrudes 6082-T6 multi-cell cross-members FSW-joined to tray side rails. Alternatives: 7075-T6 extrusion (rejected on weldability), AlSi10MnMg HPDC baseline, AM60B magnesium (rejected on corrosion). Physics: closed multi-cell extrusions provide uniform, predictable lateral crush force for cell-zone intrusion control; FSW gives porosity-free, fatigue-strong joints, improving pack structural repeatability under side-pole and crush-test loading.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Extrusion plus FSW replaces high-pressure casting; alternatives MIG-welded extrusion vs cast node. Removes casting porosity scrap and one large die. Cuts BOM machining of cast faces; FSW automation raises first-pass yield and lowers warranty leak risk versus cast trays.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Extrusion plus FSW removes casting porosity and die cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Extruded cross-members must equal cast crush strength for cell protection and resist coolant-side corrosion; mitigated by 6082-T6 temper control and FSW joint qualification. Pack side-pole and ground-impact crush validated; FSW fatigue checked to vibration durability. Sealing integrity for IP67 maintained. Premium BEV refinement and side-impact pack protection preserved under UN/ECE R100 and R95 with no range loss.

title: Front longitudinal rail tailor-welded blank gauge control
description: [ICE,MHEV] Convert single-gauge front longitudinal rails to tailor-welded blanks combining a soft crush front and stiff rear, removing a separately welded rear reinforcement. On ICE/48V luxury SUVs this manages frontal-crush progression in one part, cutting a reinforcement and welds while trimming mass.

ideaData.title: Front longitudinal rail tailor-welded blank gauge control
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline uses uniform DP780 rails with a bolted/welded DP980 rear reinforcement to stiffen the dash node. The change laser-welds a TWB rail: DP590 soft crush front, DP980 rear, 22MnB5 1500MPa boron dash-node insert integrated. Alternatives: 6082-T6 extruded rail, CP1000 uniform rail, HSLA S420 base. Physics: graded strength sets a controlled fold-then-resist sequence so energy dissipates in the front while the dash node resists intrusion, deleting the discrete reinforcement and its joints.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Laser tailor-welded blank replaces uniform stamping plus reinforcement; alternatives hydroformed rail vs roll-formed graded section. Removes one reinforcement panel and ~10 welds per side, one stamping die. Adds laser-weld blanking; net BOM and assembly-station reduction.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One graded blank replaces a rail plus welded reinforcement.

ideaData.riskNotes: Graded fold sequence must reliably crush front-first while resisting dash intrusion across the small-overlap and full-frontal cases; TWB weld-line fatigue and fold initiation validated by component and full-vehicle crash. Risk of weld-line crack at transition mitigated by laser-weld quality control. NVH/stiffness for 48V front loads retained. Premium frontal refinement and intrusion targets preserved under UN/ECE R94 and IIHS.

title: Off-road frame cross-member hydroformed to cut welds
description: [ICE,MHEV] Hydroform the body-on-frame transmission/transfer-case cross-member as one closed section, replacing a stamped-and-welded box with internal gussets. On premium off-road SUVs this raises torsional stiffness for articulation loads while deleting weld seams and gussets, cutting part count and warranty leak/fatigue paths.

ideaData.title: Off-road frame cross-member hydroformed to cut welds
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline cross-member is a two-shell DP780 stamped box with welded internal gussets to react transfer-case and articulation loads. The change hydroforms a single CP1000 closed tube with formed mount bosses. Alternatives: 22MnB5 1500MPa boron stamped box, 6082-T6 extruded member, AlSi10MnMg cast node. Physics: closed hydroformed section has higher torsional constant than an open welded box, improving frame twist stiffness under off-road articulation and reducing stress concentrations at former weld lines.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Hydroforming replaces two-shell stamping plus gusset welding; alternatives roll forming vs tube bending. Eliminates internal gussets and ~16 welds, two stamping dies. Adds a hydroform cell; lowers BOM, weld-station load and improves dimensional repeatability for frame assembly.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Closed hydroformed section replaces a welded gusseted box.

ideaData.riskNotes: Hydroformed member must sustain transfer-case mount fatigue and articulation torsion without wall thinning at bends; mitigated by CP1000 formability mapping and FEA-tuned wall thickness. Off-road durability validated to articulation and corrugation load cases. Weld-line removal cuts fatigue initiation sites. NVH improved by stiffer closed section. Premium off-road articulation and on-road refinement preserved with no loss of ground clearance.

title: BEV structural pack lid as load-bearing floor panel
description: [BEV] Make the battery pack upper lid a structural floor panel that reacts seat and cross-car loads, deleting a separate stamped floor pan above the pack. On a cell-to-body BEV SUV this removes a full floor stamping and its welds while preserving torsional rigidity and occupant floor stiffness.

ideaData.title: BEV structural pack lid as load-bearing floor panel
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline has a stamped HSLA floor pan spot-welded above an independent pack with its own lid, duplicating panels. The change uses a structural 6082-T6/composite pack lid bonded to the body as the floor, deleting the separate pan. Alternatives: AlSi10MnMg cast lid, GFRP/SMC sandwich lid, DP780 steel structural lid. Physics: a bonded structural lid closes the floor shear field, sustaining torsional stiffness and seat-load paths while removing redundant panel mass and the seam between pan and lid.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Structural bonded lid via stamping or composite moulding replaces stamped floor pan; alternatives HPDC lid vs SMC compression mould. Deletes one large floor stamping, ~24 welds and a die. Shifts joining to structural adhesive line; reduces BOM and body-shop welds, adds bond-cure station.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Structural pack lid doubles as floor, deleting a redundant pan.

ideaData.riskNotes: Bonded structural lid must carry seat-anchor and torsional loads and survive thermal/fire events from cells below; mitigated by adhesive selection, mechanical back-up fasteners and thermal barrier layer. Floor stiffness and seatbelt anchorage validated to R14; pack fire-propagation to GB 38031 / R100. Bond durability tested to ageing. Premium floor solidity and NVH preserved; serviceability addressed with bonded-panel repair process.

title: Front crush can commonised between ICE and BEV platforms
description: [ICE,MHEV,BEV] Commonise the front crush can and rail interface between ICE/MHEV and BEV SUV variants using a shared 6082-T6 extrusion tuned by length, despite different front masses. One can family and one mounting plate cut tooling and logistics while meeting each variant's frontal pulse and BEV front-crash pack stand-off.

ideaData.title: Front crush can commonised between ICE and BEV platforms
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/BEV. Baseline runs distinct crush cans for ICE (heavy engine) and BEV (front-frunk, lighter). The change adopts one 6082-T6 multi-cell extrusion family with variant length/gauge and a common DP980 mounting plate. Alternatives: 7075-T6 can, AlSi10MnMg cast can, DP980 roll-formed steel can. Physics: extrusion mean crush force scales with section and length, so a single profile retuned per variant manages each frontal pulse; common plate preserves repair interface and BEV front structure stand-off to the pack.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One extrusion die family with variant cut length; alternatives roll-form steel vs cast can. Collapses two can part numbers and mounting plates into shared tooling, removing one die set. Standard repair-can interface lowers service-part SKUs and assembly fixturing.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Shared crush-can family across ICE and BEV removes duplicate tooling.

ideaData.riskNotes: Common can must satisfy both heavy-ICE and light-BEV pulses without compromising either; mitigated by length/gauge tuning and validated full-frontal/ODB plus BEV pack stand-off. Repair-can crush repeatability checked to RCAR. NVH from shared mount tuned per variant. Premium frontal refinement and crash compatibility preserved across powertrains under UN/ECE R94 and R100 for BEV.

title: Rear longitudinal rail re-gauged for PHEV pack mass
description: [PHEV] Re-gauge rear longitudinal rails for the actual PHEV under-floor pack mass rather than a conservative shared section, using DP980 to trim wall thickness. Rear-crash energy management and pack protection are held while removing surplus steel on a luxury plug-in SUV.

ideaData.title: Rear longitudinal rail re-gauged for PHEV pack mass
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline rear rails reuse a heavy shared section sized for worst-case BEV mass, over-built for PHEV. The change adopts DP980 rails gauged to PHEV rear inertia with a 22MnB5 1500MPa boron pack-mount node. Alternatives: 6082-T6 extruded rail, CP1000 rail, HSLA S420 base. Physics: matching section to PHEV rear mass keeps mean rear-crush force and pack stand-off within target while removing surplus material; boron node localises the pack-mount load path for rear-impact integrity.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Re-gauged stamping with boron node insert; alternatives roll forming rail vs tailor-welded blank. Trims ~1.5kg/side, reduces coil spend. Retains existing mount interface so no new fixtures; deletes one doubler and 6 welds per side.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Right-sizing rear rail to PHEV mass removes shared-section surplus.

ideaData.riskNotes: Down-gauged rails must protect the rear pack in offset and full-width rear impact and resist tow/fatigue loads; mitigated by DP980 strength and boron mount node, validated to UN/ECE R34 and pack integrity. Fatigue at re-gauged section checked to road-load durability. NVH for rear pack mass retained via section tuning. Premium rear refinement and crash protection preserved.

title: Floor cross-members switched from stamping to roll forming
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Roll-form the straight floor seat cross-members instead of deep-draw stamping, cutting tooling and scrap while enabling high-strength CP1000 sections too brittle to draw. Applicable across all powertrains, it lowers material and tooling spend while raising side-impact bending strength on a luxury SUV floor.

ideaData.title: Floor cross-members switched from stamping to roll forming
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline draws DP590 floor cross-members, limited in strength by formability and high die wear. The change roll-forms CP1000 complex-phase straight sections with end-tab cut-outs for rocker tie-in. Alternatives: DP980 roll-form, 22MnB5 boron stamping, 6082-T6 extrusion. Physics: roll forming permits higher-strength CP1000 at thinner gauge, raising lateral bending strength for side-pole load transfer from rocker to tunnel while reducing draw scrap and forming energy.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Continuous roll forming replaces deep-draw stamping; alternatives press hardening vs aluminium extrusion. Cuts blank scrap ~12% and removes a large draw die. Enables high-strength thin gauge; end conditions added by post-cut. Lowers BOM and tooling amortisation; minor added secondary trim op.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Roll forming enables high-strength thin section at lower tooling cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Roll-formed straight section must integrate with rocker/tunnel for side-pole load transfer and resist springback variation; mitigated by CP1000 process control and FEA-validated section. Side-impact intrusion and seat-cross stiffness validated to R95. Fatigue at end cut-outs checked. NVH neutral with stiffer section. Premium side-impact protection and floor solidity preserved across all powertrain floors.

title: BEV rocker/sill extrusion integrating pack side crush
description: [BEV] Combine the body rocker and the battery side-crush member into one multi-cell 6082-T6 extrusion, deleting the separate steel sill reinforcement. On a skateboard BEV SUV this single side member manages side-pole intrusion and pack protection, cutting parts and mass while raising side stiffness.

ideaData.title: BEV rocker/sill extrusion integrating pack side crush
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline has a steel rocker (DP980) plus a separate aluminium pack side-crush rail, overlapping in function. The change merges them into one 6082-T6 multi-cell extrusion bonded/bolted to floor and pack. Alternatives: 7075-T6 extrusion, AlSi10MnMg cast sill nodes, 22MnB5 boron steel rocker baseline. Physics: a deep multi-cell side member provides progressive lateral crush and a continuous load path to floor cross-members, controlling cell-zone intrusion in side-pole impact while deleting the duplicate steel reinforcement.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Single extrusion with cast end nodes replaces steel rocker plus separate rail; alternatives FSW vs MIG-welded assembly. Deletes one steel reinforcement, ~20 welds and a stamping die. Mixes joining to bonding/bolting; reduces BOM and body-shop welds, adds extrusion and node-casting cells.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One side extrusion serves rocker and pack crush, deleting steel reinforcement.

ideaData.riskNotes: Combined sill must meet side-pole intrusion velocity for occupant and cell protection plus jacking/towing loads; mitigated by multi-cell tuning and cast end nodes, validated to R95 and R100 pack tests. Galvanic isolation at steel-to-aluminium joints addressed. NVH improved by stiffer side. Premium side-impact protection and body solidity preserved; serviceability via bolted pack interface.

title: Rear bumper beam commonised across SUV body styles
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Commonise the rear bumper beam and crush-can interface across standard and coupe SUV body styles using one DP980 roll-formed beam with bracketed fascia adapters. This removes a body-style-specific beam variant, cutting tooling and SKUs while meeting rear low-speed and tow-load requirements across powertrains.

ideaData.title: Rear bumper beam commonised across SUV body styles
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline carries two rear-beam variants for SUV and coupe-SUV rear fascia geometry. The change uses one DP980 roll-formed beam with style-specific fascia adapter brackets and common 6082-T6 crush cans. Alternatives: 22MnB5 boron beam, 7075-T6 aluminium beam, HSLA roll-form. Physics: structural rear load path is body-style independent; only fascia attachment differs, so one beam plus adapters meets RCAR low-speed and tow-tongue loads while removing a redundant beam tool.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One roll-formed beam with adapter brackets replaces two stamped/rolled variants; alternatives hot stamping vs aluminium extrusion. Removes one beam die and a crush-can variant. Adds small adapter stamping; net tooling and service-SKU reduction with simpler rear assembly logistics.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Body-style-independent beam plus adapters removes a variant tool.

ideaData.riskNotes: Common beam plus adapters must meet rear low-speed repairability and tow-tongue fatigue for both body styles; mitigated by DP980 strength and validated RCAR rear and trailer load tests. Adapter bracket fatigue checked to durability spec. NVH from common beam tuned per fascia. Premium rear appearance and crash/repair performance preserved across body styles and powertrains under UN/ECE R34/R42.

title: BEV pack cross-members structurally bonded not bolted
description: [BEV] Replace bolted battery-tray cross-member joints with structural adhesive bonding plus self-pierce rivets, cutting fasteners and sealing parts while improving fatigue life on a BEV SUV pack. Bonded cross-members give a continuous load path for side-pole protection and remove leak-prone bolted interfaces.

ideaData.title: BEV pack cross-members structurally bonded not bolted
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline bolts AlSi10MnMg cross-members to tray rails with sealed fasteners, adding weight and leak paths. The change structurally bonds 6082-T6 cross-members with SPR back-up to the tray. Alternatives: FSW joint, AlSi10MnMg cast-in cross-members, MIG-welded extrusion. Physics: continuous adhesive bond distributes shear over the full flange rather than discrete bolts, raising joint fatigue strength and lateral stiffness for cell-zone intrusion control while sealing the coolant/IP67 boundary without separate gaskets.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Structural bonding with SPR replaces bolted joints; alternatives FSW vs MIG welding. Removes ~24 fasteners, weld nuts and gaskets per pack. Adds adhesive dispense and cure; reduces BOM, sealing parts and warranty leak claims, with cleaner automated assembly flow.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Bonded joints replace bolts, sealing and stiffening in one step.

ideaData.riskNotes: Bonded cross-members must hold side-pole crush load and IP67 sealing across thermal cycling and coolant exposure; mitigated by qualified epoxy, SPR back-up and surface pre-treatment. Joint fatigue and peel validated to pack vibration and ageing. Repair strategy defined for bonded pack. NVH and lateral stiffness improved. Premium BEV refinement and side-impact pack protection preserved under UN/ECE R100 and R95.

title: Front rail commonised across ICE and MHEV with mount inserts
description: [ICE,MHEV] Commonise the front longitudinal rail between pure-ICE and 48V MHEV variants using shared stampings with variant-specific bolt-in mount inserts for the belt-starter-generator. One rail tool serves both, cutting tooling and complexity while preserving frontal crush and powertrain-mount stiffness on a luxury SUV.

ideaData.title: Front rail commonised across ICE and MHEV with mount inserts
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline uses two rail variants to suit different engine/BSG mount loads. The change shares one DP780 rail stamping with bolt-in 22MnB5 1500MPa boron mount inserts per variant. Alternatives: 6082-T6 extruded rail, CP1000 rail, AlSi10MnMg cast mount node.
