Physics: crush behaviour is set by the rail section, common to both; only local mount stiffness differs, handled by interchangeable boron inserts, so one tool meets frontal pulse while supporting the heavier 48V BSG loads.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One rail stamping with variant bolt-in inserts; alternatives roll forming vs hydroforming. Collapses two rail part numbers to one, removing a die. Adds small insert stamping shared as service part; net BOM, tooling and assembly-fixture reduction across variants.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Shared rail with mount inserts removes a variant-specific tool.

ideaData.riskNotes: Shared rail must meet frontal crush for both variants and carry heavier 48V BSG mount loads via inserts; mitigated by boron insert strength and validated full-frontal/ODB plus mount durability. Insert-to-rail fatigue checked at bolt holes. NVH from BSG vibration controlled by insert stiffness. Premium frontal refinement and powertrain-mount solidity preserved under UN/ECE R94 across ICE and MHEV.

title: BEV rear underbody torsion-box mega-casting
description: [BEV] Replace the multi-piece stamped rear underbody and torsion box with a single AlSi10MnMg rear mega-casting integrating rear-rail, shock towers and pack-mount nodes. On a BEV SUV this removes dozens of stampings and welds, simplifying assembly while sustaining rear-crash and pack-protection load paths.

ideaData.title: BEV rear underbody torsion-box mega-casting
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline rear underbody is a ~20-piece DP780/HSLA stamped-and-welded structure with separate shock towers and pack brackets. The change casts a single AlSi10MnMg HPDC rear node. Alternatives: 6082-T6 extrusion-plus-node hybrid, AE44 magnesium casting (rejected on cost/corrosion), DP980 stamped baseline. Physics: a monolithic casting removes weld-flange compliance, giving stiff, direct rear-crash and pack-mount load paths and consistent shock-tower stiffness, while consolidating part count and removing weld variability.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One large HPDC replaces ~20 stampings; alternatives extrusion-welded hybrid vs low-pressure casting. Removes ~60 welds, multiple brackets and several dies. Adds a giga-casting cell and machining of datums/mounts; large BOM and body-shop weld-station reduction, with porosity NDT added.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Rear mega-casting consolidates ~20 stampings into one node.

ideaData.riskNotes: Cast rear node must meet rear-crash crush, shock-tower fatigue and pack-mount pull loads with adequate ductility; mitigated by AlSi10MnMg crash-grade alloy, T7 temper and local thickening, validated to R34/R94 and R100. Repairability of large casting addressed via bolt-on crash horns. NVH improved by stiffness. Premium rear refinement and crash/pack protection preserved; casting porosity controlled by NDT.

title: Off-road belly skid-plate composite with steel load ribs
description: [ICE,MHEV] Replace a heavy aluminium belly skid plate with an SMC/GFRP composite pan carrying bonded steel load-distribution ribs, cutting mass and grounding-noise while protecting the underbody. On premium off-road SUVs this trims weight at the lowest point, improving centre of gravity and corrosion resistance.

ideaData.title: Off-road belly skid-plate composite with steel load ribs
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline uses a 6mm 6082-T6 aluminium skid plate, heavy and prone to galling. The change moulds an SMC/GFRP pan with bonded DP980 steel load-spreading ribs at mount points. Alternatives: 22MnB5 1500MPa boron stamped plate, AM60B magnesium plate (rejected on impact), thicker 6082-T6 baseline. Physics: composite pan absorbs abrasion and spreads moderate grounding loads while bonded steel ribs channel point impacts to frame mounts, protecting sump and steering at lower mass and improved low-frequency grounding NVH.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Compression-moulded composite with bonded steel ribs replaces machined aluminium plate; alternatives RTM composite vs stamped boron. Cuts ~2.2kg, removes machining of aluminium. Adds bonding step; lowers BOM mass and corrosion warranty, with simpler mount interface.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Composite pan with steel ribs cuts mass at the lowest point.

ideaData.riskNotes: Composite pan must survive rock strikes, abrasion and thermal exposure near exhaust while ribs protect critical components; mitigated by GFRP impact grade, steel rib reinforcement and heat shielding. Off-road impact and corrugation durability validated. Lower mass aids CoG and ride. NVH grounding noise reduced. Premium off-road protection and capability preserved with no clearance loss; serviceable as a bolt-on unit.

title: Crush-can mounting bracket additive topology-optimised
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace a multi-piece welded crush-can mounting bracket with a topology-optimised additively manufactured or cast aluminium node, cutting mass and welds at the rail-to-can interface. Applicable across powertrains, it gives a stiffer, lighter load introduction into the crush can on a luxury SUV.

ideaData.title: Crush-can mounting bracket additive topology-optimised
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline crush-can mount is a 4-piece DP780 stamped/welded bracket. The change uses a topology-optimised AlSi10MnMg cast node (or AM for low volume) tuned to load paths. Alternatives: 6082-T6 forged bracket, 7075-T6 machined node, 22MnB5 boron stamping. Physics: topology optimisation places material only along principal crash-load paths, raising stiffness-to-mass and ensuring clean axial load introduction into the crush can, removing weld-flange compliance and improving crush initiation repeatability.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Casting or additive replaces 4-piece weldment; alternatives forging vs machining from billet. Removes ~10 welds and three stampings. For volume, HPDC node; AM for ramp/low volume. Reduces BOM, weld stations and fixturing; adds casting/AM cell and node machining.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Topology-optimised node replaces a welded multi-piece bracket.

ideaData.riskNotes: Optimised node must introduce crush load axially without local buckling and meet fatigue at bolt joints; mitigated by AlSi10MnMg ductility, T7 temper and load-path-aligned ribs, validated by component crush and full-frontal. AM/cast porosity controlled by NDT. NVH from stiffer mount neutral-to-improved. Premium frontal crash repeatability and front-end solidity preserved across all powertrains under UN/ECE R94.

title: BEV battery cross-members commonised across pack sizes
description: [BEV] Commonise battery-tray cross-member extrusions across standard and long-range pack sizes by adding modular cells rather than redesigning, sharing one extrusion die. On BEV SUV derivatives this cuts tooling and complexity while maintaining side-pole crush and module clamping across both pack capacities.

ideaData.title: BEV battery cross-members commonised across pack sizes
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline has separate cast cross-members for standard and long-range packs, doubling tooling. The change uses one 6082-T6 extrusion profile cut to length and arrayed in modular pitch for both packs, with common AlSi10MnMg end nodes. Alternatives: 7075-T6 extrusion, AlSi10MnMg cast cross-member, AM60B magnesium (rejected on corrosion). Physics: identical extruded cross-section gives the same crush force per member regardless of pack length; only count and pitch change, so one tool protects cells in side-pole across both capacities.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One extrusion die cut and arrayed by pack size; alternatives FSW assembly vs casting. Removes one casting die and a cross-member variant. Standardises module clamp interface; cuts BOM SKUs and service parts, with shared FSW/bonding assembly across packs.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Modular cross-member array spans pack sizes from one tool.

ideaData.riskNotes: Common cross-member must clamp modules and meet side-pole crush for both pack masses; mitigated by extrusion crush tuning and validated to R95/R100 across capacities. Module clamp-load fatigue checked over thermal cycling. NVH and pack stiffness consistent. Premium BEV refinement and side-impact pack protection preserved across both range variants with no thermal-management compromise.

title: Rear crush can switched from steel to aluminium extrusion
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV] Replace a roll-formed steel rear crush can with a 6082-T6 aluminium multi-cell extrusion, cutting rear-overhang mass and improving crush consistency. Lower rear extremity mass benefits handling and efficiency across ICE, MHEV and PHEV SUVs while meeting rear low-speed and full rear-impact targets.

ideaData.title: Rear crush can switched from steel to aluminium extrusion
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV. Baseline rear crush can is a DP780 roll-formed steel box. The change extrudes a 6082-T6 multi-cell aluminium can bolted to a DP980 rear beam. Alternatives: 7075-T6 extrusion (rejected — unstable fold), AlSi10MnMg cast can, 22MnB5 boron steel can. Physics: aluminium multi-cell gives stable, repeatable mean crush force at lower mass; reduced rear-overhang inertia lowers yaw moment, aiding agility, while the can tunes rear-impact energy and pack/fuel stand-off across powertrains.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Aluminium extrusion replaces roll-formed steel can; alternatives casting vs steel hot stamping. Cuts ~1.3kg at the rear; mixes joining to bolted aluminium-steel. Removes a roll-form line; adds extrusion and isolation washers. Net BOM mass and rear-assembly simplification.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Aluminium crush can cuts rear-overhang mass at stable crush force.

ideaData.riskNotes: Aluminium can must match steel crush consistency and survive tow-load fatigue and galvanic corrosion at the steel beam joint; mitigated by 6082-T6 tuning, isolation washers and sealing. Rear 80km/h impact and RCAR low-speed validated with fuel/pack integrity. NVH neutral; lower rear inertia aids handling. Premium rear refinement and crash performance preserved under UN/ECE R34/R32 across powertrains.

evidenceSources.title: Aluminium vs steel rear crush-can study

title: Floor tunnel reinforcement deleted on BEV via cross-member tie
description: [BEV] Delete the central tunnel reinforcement on a BEV SUV (no driveshaft) by tying floor cross-members directly across a shallow tunnel, recovering the load path through the pack and cross-members. This removes a stamped reinforcement and welds while preserving torsional stiffness and side-pole load transfer.

ideaData.title: Floor tunnel reinforcement deleted on BEV via cross-member tie
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline retains a tall tunnel and DP980 tunnel reinforcement carried over from ICE architecture, redundant without a driveshaft. The change lowers the tunnel and ties DP780 floor cross-members directly to the pack and rockers, deleting the reinforcement. Alternatives: 6082-T6 cross-car extrusion, 22MnB5 boron shallow tunnel, AlSi10MnMg cast cross-node. Physics: with the pack as a stiff floor, cross-car load transfers through cross-members and pack rather than the tunnel, sustaining torsion and side-pole paths while removing the tall-tunnel reinforcement and freeing cabin space.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Cross-member-to-pack tie replaces tunnel reinforcement; alternatives extruded cross-car member vs cast node. Deletes one stamped reinforcement, ~12 welds and a die. Lowers BOM and body-shop welds; minor change to cross-member ends. Frees packaging for centre console.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Pack and cross-members replace the redundant tunnel reinforcement.

ideaData.riskNotes: Removing tunnel reinforcement must not reduce torsional stiffness or side-pole cross-car load transfer; mitigated by direct cross-member-to-pack/rocker ties validated to R95 and body-stiffness targets. Fatigue at cross-member ends checked to road load. NVH floor boom controlled with damping and pack stiffness. Premium body solidity and side-impact protection preserved; cabin packaging improved for luxury console.

title: PHEV underfloor pack shield integrated with skid-plate ribs
description: [PHEV] Integrate the PHEV under-floor battery shield with off-road skid-plate ribs into one stamped boron protection panel, deleting a separate pack underplate and brace. On a luxury PHEV off-roader this single panel manages ground-strike loads and pack protection, cutting parts and mass.

ideaData.title: PHEV underfloor pack shield integrated with skid-plate ribs
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline carries a thin pack underplate plus a separate aluminium skid plate and brace. The change forms one 22MnB5 1500MPa boron stamped panel with crush-rib geometry covering pack and grounding zones. Alternatives: 6082-T6 ribbed plate, AlSi10MnMg cast shield, SMC/GFRP composite pan. Physics: a press-hardened ribbed panel resists point ground strikes and distributes load to frame mounts while shielding the under-floor pack, combining two protection functions in one structure and removing the redundant underplate.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Hot-stamped ribbed panel replaces pack underplate plus skid plate; alternatives aluminium stamping vs composite moulding. Deletes one plate, a brace and ~8 fasteners. Adds press-hardening; reduces BOM, mass and underbody assembly time, with a single bolt-on protection module.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One ribbed boron panel shields the pack and resists grounding.

ideaData.riskNotes: Combined panel must protect the pack from ground strikes and resist deformation into cells while surviving corrosion and articulation loads; mitigated by boron strength, rib tuning and AlSi coating, validated to ground-impact and pack-integrity tests. Off-road durability checked to rock-strike cases. NVH grounding noise managed. Premium off-road capability and PHEV pack safety preserved under UN/ECE R100 and GB 38031.

title: Front rail commonised across BEV pack capacities
description: [BEV] Commonise the front longitudinal crash rail across BEV pack-capacity variants by sharing the rail and tuning only the front cross-member tie to the pack. One rail tool serves all capacities, cutting tooling while preserving frontal crush and front-pack stand-off on a BEV SUV range.

ideaData.title: Front rail commonised across BEV pack capacities
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline tweaks the front rail per pack capacity due to differing front masses. The change shares one DP980 rail and tunes the 6082-T6 front cross-member tie to the pack per variant. Alternatives: 6082-T6 extruded rail, CP1000 rail, 22MnB5 boron dash node. Physics: the rail crush behaviour is mass-tolerant within range, and remaining differences are absorbed by the tunable cross-member tie, so one rail tool meets frontal pulse and maintains front-crash stand-off to the pack across capacities.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One rail stamping with tunable cross-member tie; alternatives roll forming vs extrusion. Removes a rail variant and a die. Tie tuned by gauge, shared across line; cuts BOM SKUs and assembly fixturing while keeping front-end logistics simple across capacities.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Shared front rail with tunable tie spans BEV pack capacities.

ideaData.riskNotes: Shared rail must meet frontal crush for the heaviest front-mass capacity without over-stiffening the lightest, and protect the front pack edge; mitigated by mass-tolerant rail design and tunable cross-member tie, validated full-frontal/ODB plus R100 pack stand-off. Fatigue at tie checked. NVH consistent. Premium frontal refinement and crash protection preserved across the BEV range under UN/ECE R94 and R100.

title: Bumper beam roll-formed to delete reinforcement insert
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Roll-form a closed-section front bumper beam with integral stiffening flutes, deleting a separately welded reinforcement insert. Across all powertrains this simplifies the beam, cutting a part and welds while meeting bending-moment and low-speed crash targets on a premium SUV.

ideaData.title: Bumper beam roll-formed to delete reinforcement insert
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline uses an open DP780 beam with a welded DP980 reinforcement insert. The change roll-forms a closed-section DP980 beam with rolled stiffening flutes, deleting the insert. Alternatives: 22MnB5 1500MPa boron hot-stamped beam, 7075-T6 aluminium extrusion, CP1000 roll-form. Physics: a closed, fluted roll-formed section raises bending stiffness and section modulus intrinsically, meeting RCAR low-speed and bending targets without an added reinforcement, removing the insert and its welds.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Roll forming a closed fluted section replaces open beam plus insert; alternatives hot stamping vs aluminium extrusion. Deletes one insert and ~8 welds. Removes a stamping die; continuous roll forming lowers scrap. Net BOM and weld-station reduction with stable section quality.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Closed fluted roll-form beam deletes the welded reinforcement.

ideaData.riskNotes: Closed beam must meet bending-moment, low-speed repairability and pedestrian leg-form loads without the insert; mitigated by DP980 strength, flute geometry and crush-can tuning. Roll-form weld-seam integrity validated; fatigue at fascia mounts checked. NVH neutral. Premium low-speed crash, pedestrian and repairability targets preserved across all powertrains under UN/ECE R42, R127 and Euro NCAP.

title: BEV pack cross-member hybrid steel-aluminium load tuning
description: [BEV] Use a hybrid 22MnB5 boron-and-aluminium battery cross-member where boron carries side-pole crush peaks and aluminium handles the field, optimising mass-cost trade versus an all-aluminium member. On a BEV SUV pack this targets material where strength is needed while trimming cost and mass.

ideaData.title: BEV pack cross-member hybrid steel-aluminium load tuning
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline uses an all-AlSi10MnMg cast cross-member, costly and mass-heavy for uniform strength. The change pairs a 22MnB5 1500MPa boron central crush spine with bonded 6082-T6 aluminium outer cells. Alternatives: full DP980 steel member, 7075-T6 extrusion, AM60B magnesium (rejected on corrosion). Physics: boron spine carries peak side-pole crush load at minimal thickness while aluminium cells provide energy absorption and stiffness at lower density, optimising the mass-cost curve and protecting cells under lateral intrusion.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Hot-stamped boron spine bonded to aluminium extrusion replaces full casting; alternatives FSW hybrid vs all-aluminium extrusion. Mixes processes (press hardening plus extrusion plus bonding). Removes a large casting die; adds bonding line. Net material-cost and mass reduction with managed multi-material joining.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Boron-aluminium hybrid puts strength only where crush peaks.

ideaData.riskNotes: Hybrid member must meet side-pole crush and resist galvanic corrosion at steel-aluminium bonds under coolant exposure; mitigated by isolation, qualified adhesive and AlSi coating on boron. Side-pole and pack-integrity validated to R95/R100; joint fatigue checked over thermal cycles. NVH and pack stiffness maintained. Premium BEV side-impact protection and refinement preserved with no range or sealing compromise.

title: Rear torsion-box de-gauged for ICE without rear pack
description: [ICE,MHEV] De-gauge the rear underfloor torsion box on ICE/MHEV SUV variants that carry no rear battery pack, removing thickness added only for BEV/PHEV pack mass. DP780 tailored panels replace heavy shared gauge, trimming mass and material while holding rear-crash and tow-load performance.

ideaData.title: Rear torsion-box de-gauged for ICE without rear pack
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline shares a heavy rear torsion-box gauge across the range, over-built for ICE which has no rear pack. The change uses tailored DP780 panels gauged to ICE rear loads with a CP1000 tow-mount reinforcement. Alternatives: 6082-T6 aluminium floor, DP980 tailored blank, HSLA S420 base. Physics: removing pack-driven over-thickness on ICE keeps first torsion mode and rear-crush force within target while shedding mass; CP1000 reinforcement localises the tow-hitch load path retained for trailer capability.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Tailored de-gauged stamping with local tow reinforcement; alternatives tailor-welded blank vs roll forming. Trims ~2.0kg on ICE variants, cuts coil spend. Retains floor interfaces; deletes one shared doubler. Adds tow-mount patch; net BOM and mass reduction on ICE derivatives.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Removing pack-driven over-gauge sheds mass on ICE variants.

ideaData.riskNotes: De-gauging must not reduce rear-crash crush or tow-tongue/fatigue capability on ICE; mitigated by DP780 strength and CP1000 tow-mount reinforcement, validated to R34 rear impact and trailer load durability. First torsion mode checked for NVH boom. Premium rear refinement and towing capability preserved on ICE/MHEV variants, with structure right-sized to actual loads rather than the heaviest pack case.

title: Crush can manufacturing switched to tailor-welded blank
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Build the front crush can from a tailor-welded blank with a soft initiation zone and stiff base, deleting a separately welded trigger and base reinforcement. Across powertrains this controls fold initiation in one part, cutting welds and parts on a luxury SUV front crash structure.

ideaData.title: Crush can manufacturing switched to tailor-welded blank
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline crush can is a DP780 box with a welded soft trigger and a DP980 base doubler. The change folds a TWB pairing DP340 soft front and DP980 base, deleting both add-ons. Alternatives: 6082-T6 extruded can, CP1000 uniform can, 22MnB5 boron base insert. Physics: graded blank sets fold initiation softly and stiffens the base for clean reaction into the rail, achieving target mean crush force and stable accordion folding without discrete trigger and reinforcement parts.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Tailor-welded blank folded into the can replaces box plus trigger plus doubler; alternatives roll forming vs aluminium extrusion. Deletes two parts and ~8 welds per side. Adds laser-weld blanking; removes a stamping die. Net BOM and weld-station reduction with consistent crush quality.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Graded TWB sets fold initiation, deleting trigger and doubler.

ideaData.riskNotes: Graded can must initiate axial folding reliably and react cleanly into the rail across tolerance and temperature; mitigated by TWB weld placement, DP340 soft zone and DP980 base, validated by component crush and full-frontal/ODB. Weld-line fatigue at transition checked. NVH neutral. Premium frontal crash repeatability and impact harshness preserved across all powertrains under UN/ECE R94 and RCAR.

title: BEV floor shear panel replaces discrete cross-members
description: [BEV] Replace several discrete floor cross-members with a single bonded aluminium shear panel acting with the pack as a stressed-skin floor, cutting parts and welds. On a BEV SUV this distributes side-pole and torsion loads continuously rather than through spaced members, simplifying the floor while raising stiffness.

ideaData.title: BEV floor shear panel replaces discrete cross-members
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline uses four spaced DP980 floor cross-members spot-welded between rockers. The change bonds a 6082-T6 aluminium shear panel to the floor and pack, working as stressed skin and deleting two cross-members. Alternatives: AlSi10MnMg cast cross-floor, GFRP/SMC shear panel, DP780 steel shear plate. Physics: a continuous bonded shear panel carries in-plane side-pole and torsional loads across the whole floor, replacing discrete bending members; combined with the pack it raises floor shear stiffness while reducing part count.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Bonded shear panel replaces discrete cross-members; alternatives composite panel vs steel shear plate. Deletes two cross-members and ~16 welds. Shifts joining to adhesive line; removes a stamping die. Reduces BOM and body-shop welds; adds bond-cure station and surface prep.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Continuous shear panel replaces spaced bending cross-members.

ideaData.riskNotes: Shear panel must carry side-pole and torsional loads continuously and bond durably to mixed substrates over thermal cycling; mitigated by qualified adhesive, surface pre-treatment and SPR back-up at edges. Side-pole intrusion and torsion stiffness validated to R95 and body targets. Bond ageing and repair addressed. NVH improved by continuous panel. Premium body solidity and side-impact protection preserved under UN/ECE R95.

title: Off-road recovery-point cross-member integrated into crash rail
description: [ICE,MHEV] Integrate the rear recovery/tow-point cross-member into the rear crash rail structure, deleting a separate bolted recovery bracket. On premium off-road SUVs this shares the crash-rail load path for recovery loads, cutting a heavy bracket and fasteners while preserving rear-impact and recovery strength.

ideaData.title: Off-road recovery-point cross-member integrated into crash rail
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV. Baseline bolts a heavy HSLA recovery bracket separately to the frame behind the rear crash structure. The change integrates a 22MnB5 1500MPa boron recovery node into the rear crash cross-member with formed shackle bosses. Alternatives: CP1000 stamped node, 6082-T6 forged recovery eye, DP980 box bracket. Physics: routing recovery loads through the rear crash cross-member reuses an existing stiff load path to the frame, meeting recovery pull strength while deleting the separate bracket, provided the crash rail's energy-absorbing function is preserved away from the node.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Integrated recovery node in the crash cross-member replaces a bolted bracket; alternatives forging the eye vs stamped box. Deletes one bracket and ~6 fasteners. Adds boron node forming; removes a part and weld-nut set. Net BOM, mass and assembly reduction with shared load path.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Recovery node shares the crash rail's load path, deleting a bracket.

ideaData.riskNotes: Integrated node must meet recovery/snatch pull loads without compromising rear-crash crush behaviour; mitigated by locating the boron node outside the primary crush zone and validating recovery pull plus R34 rear impact. Fatigue under repeated recovery loads checked to durability spec. NVH neutral. Premium off-road recovery capability and rear crash protection preserved, with the bracket function absorbed into existing structure.

title: BEV rear underbody one-piece gigacast replaces 79-part assembly
description: [BEV] Replace the stamped-and-welded rear underbody (79 parts, 180+ welds) with a single AlSi10MnMg high-pressure gigacasting on a 6,100t press. Eliminates SPR, RSW and adhesive operations in the rear crash structure, cutting BIW part count, tooling spend and dimensional stack-up for the skateboard architecture.
submittedBy: Tesla Model Y / NIO ES8 benchmark

ideaData.title: BEV rear underbody one-piece gigacast replaces 79-part assembly
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline rear underbody is a multi-material stamped sub-assembly of HSLA S420, DP590 and 22MnB5 boron rails joined by 180+ resistance spot welds plus SPR and structural adhesive. The change consolidates this into one AlSi10MnMg HPDC gigacasting (Fe<0.15% for ductility, T7 temper). Alternatives evaluated: 6082-T6 extrusion-intensive space-frame, AM60B Mg HPDC for the heel area, and a tailor-welded DP980/CP1000 steel design. Casting in self-hardening AlSi10MnMg removes weld-induced distortion and gives isotropic load paths into the structural pack ring. Wall thickness 2.5–4mm with cast-in mounting bosses replaces 79 discrete pressings.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus hot stamping plus tailor-welded blanks, a single HPDC gigacasting on a 6,100t machine deletes ~79 stampings, ~6 sub-assembly weld jigs and 180+ RSW spots. Alternative low-pressure casting was rejected for cycle time. BOM lines fall ~70%; assembly takt drops; SPR/adhesive consumables eliminated in zone. Offsetting tooling: one large die plus vacuum system.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Massive part/weld consolidation collapses BIW BOM and assembly labour in the rear crash zone.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: cast node tuned via T7 elongation >10% to meet UN/ECE R34 rear impact; ductile alloy plus cast-in crush initiators preserve energy absorption. NVH improved by monolithic stiffness raising rear-end modes ~8%. Durability risk from casting porosity mitigated by vacuum HPDC and CT sampling. Repairability addressed with bolt-on rear crash cassette so premium warranty exposure stays bounded.

title: BEV front underbody gigacasting integrates shotgun and shock towers
description: [BEV] Cast the front underbody as one AlSi10MnMg HPDC node integrating shock towers, shotguns and front rails, replacing a 71-part welded steel/aluminium hybrid. Reduces SPR/flow-drill-screw count, removes braze seams and stabilises hardpoint tolerances feeding the front motor cradle on the BEV skateboard.

ideaData.title: BEV front underbody gigacasting integrates shotgun and shock towers
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline front structure mixes 22MnB5 boron rails, DP780 shotguns and 6014-T4 stamped shock towers joined by flow-drill screws, SPR and RSW. The change integrates these into one AlSi10MnMg vacuum HPDC (T7). Alternatives: 6082-T6/7075-T6 extrusion space-frame, AE44 Mg creep-resistant tower casting, and CP1000 roll-formed rails with cast corners. Monolithic casting fixes suspension hardpoints within ±0.3mm, removing weld spring-back. Cast-in ribs route crash load into the rocker and pack ring; local 4mm pours at tower bearings carry strut loads while 2.5mm fields save mass.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One HPDC node replaces ~71 parts and ~4 weld jigs versus a hot-stamped/extruded hybrid. Flow-drill screw and SPR counts drop sharply; laser braze of the upper rail is deleted. Alternative forged towers rejected on cost. Net: fewer fixtures, lower scrap, simpler logistics; offset by one large die, melt loop and CT inspection cell.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Hardpoint integration removes weld stack-up and slashes joining operations up front.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: front node engineered with graded wall and crush initiators for UN/ECE R94 frontal; T7 ductility >10% prevents brittle fracture at towers. NVH: integrated towers raise front-end stiffness, improving steering precision expected of premium SUVs. Durability validated by strut-load fatigue and porosity CT. Casting repair limited, mitigated by sacrificial bolt-on crush cans preserving warranty exposure.

title: Self-piercing rivet count reduction via adhesive co-cure
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Re-balance the multi-material body joining recipe so structural adhesive carries shear and SPR density drops 22% in non-peel joints between 6014-T4 aluminium and DP780 steel. Cuts rivet consumables, gun cycle time and electrode-free joining cost across all powertrain body variants.

ideaData.title: Self-piercing rivet count reduction via adhesive co-cure
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline aluminium-steel joints use ~2,400 SPR plus epoxy in a belt-and-braces recipe. The change uses crash-durable epoxy (toughened) to carry static/fatigue shear, retaining SPR only where peel or dynamic peel governs, cutting rivet count 22%. Alternatives: flow-drill screws for single-sided access, RSW with aluminium-clad electrodes, and laser braze for steel-steel seams. Joint physics: adhesive distributes stress over bond area lowering peak SPR load; remaining rivets handle process-robustness and crash peel. Mixed stack 6014-T4 (2.0mm) to DP780 (1.2mm) validated by lap-shear and KSII.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus all-SPR joining, co-cured adhesive plus reduced rivets cuts ~530 rivets/body and gun cycles, lifting line takt. Alternatives flow-drill screw and laser braze retained selectively. Lower fastener BOM and tip wear; oven adhesive cure already in process. Offset: bondline control and adhesive QA. Tooling largely unchanged; robot programs simplified.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Shifting load to adhesive removes redundant rivets and consumable cost per body.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: KSII and peel testing confirm joints meet Euro NCAP loadcases; SPR retained at crash-critical peel zones preserves energy path. Durability: cyclic corrosion (galvanic Al/steel) controlled by epoxy isolation and sealing, protecting premium long-life warranty. NVH: continuous bondline improves body damping versus discrete rivets. Process risk from bondline porosity mitigated by inline dispensing monitoring.

title: Flow-drill screws replace SPR on closed-section bodyside rail
description: [ICE,MHEV,BEV] Convert single-sided-access bodyside closed-section joints from blind rivet/SPR to flow-drill screws (FDS) into 6082-T6 extrusion and DP590 stampings. Removes the need for backing tooling, simplifies fixturing and lowers joining cost on the A/B-pillar reinforcement load path.

ideaData.title: Flow-drill screws replace SPR on closed-section bodyside rail
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/BEV. Baseline closed bodyside sections need expensive C-frame access or blind fasteners. The change applies FDS, which forms its own thread without back access, joining 6082-T6 extrusion (2.5mm) to DP590 (1.5mm). Alternatives: SPR with re-engineered access, RSW with welding adhesive, and structural rivet bonding. Physics: FDS friction-heats and extrudes a collar, forming load-bearing threads with high pull-out; combined with adhesive gives sealed, fatigue-robust joint. Enables fully closed extruded sections for torsional stiffness without split-and-reweld.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus SPR needing two-sided access, FDS is single-sided, removing anvil tooling and enabling closed extrusions. Alternatives RSW and rivet-bonding retained elsewhere. Cuts fixture complexity and cycle time; fastener cost higher per joint but offset by tooling/access savings. Adhesive dispense added for sealing. Robot-friendly, scalable across variants.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Single-sided FDS unlocks closed sections and deletes backing tooling cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: pillar load path validated for Euro NCAP side pole; FDS pull-out and adhesive give ductile joint behaviour. Durability: thread-forming pre-heat avoids Al micro-cracking; sealed joint resists galvanic corrosion preserving premium warranty. NVH: closed torsional sections raise bodyside modes improving refinement. Process risk from screw stripping mitigated by torque/angle monitoring and pilot-hole-free validation.

title: Laser-brazed roof-to-bodyside replaces solder and trim molding
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace the leaded roof ditch joint and applied trim with a CuSi3 laser-brazed roof-to-side seam between galvanised DP grade and the roof panel. Deletes the roof molding part, sealing operation and finishing, giving a class-A zero-gap seam on all powertrain variants.

ideaData.title: Laser-brazed roof-to-bodyside replaces solder and trim molding
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline roof joins via RSW under a clip-on molding requiring sealing and trim. The change uses CuSi3 laser brazing to create a smooth visible seam, eliminating the molding component and ditch sealant. Alternatives: remote-laser welding, RSW plus molding (baseline), and adhesive roof bonding with hem. Physics: low-heat braze wets galvanised steel without melting base metal, minimising distortion on the thin roof skin (0.7mm) and giving a polishable bead. Removes a visible part and several finishing steps while improving torsional continuity of the roof rail.
ideaData.materialGrade: CuSi3 braze wire / HX220 galvanised / DP590
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus RSW-plus-molding, laser braze deletes the molding part, clips, sealant and trim labour. Alternatives remote-laser weld and adhesive hem considered. One braze cell replaces spot guns plus a trim station. BOM loses a visible part; finishing reduced to light polish. Offset: laser cell capex and wire feed control; class-A inspection added.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Brazed seam removes a trim part and multiple finishing operations.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: roof crush per FMVSS 216a maintained; continuous braze raises roof-rail shear capacity versus discrete welds. Durability: braze plus seam sealer resists corrosion at the most exposed body seam, protecting premium appearance warranty. NVH: continuous joint stiffens roof, lifting boom modes. Aesthetic risk of bead defects mitigated by vision-guided brazing and inline class-A scanning.

title: Hydroformed A-pillar/cowl node consolidates seven stampings
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace a seven-part welded A-pillar-to-cowl junction with a single hydroformed DP590 tubular node. Removes weld flanges and overlaps, improving A-pillar bending strength for roof crush while cutting part and weld count across all powertrain body variants.

ideaData.title: Hydroformed A-pillar/cowl node consolidates seven stampings
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline A-pillar/cowl junction is seven stampings (HSLA and DP grades) with ~40 welds and flange overlaps. The change uses tube hydroforming of DP590 (later DP780) into a closed node carrying continuous load from rocker to roof rail. Alternatives: 22MnB5 hot-stamped multi-piece, 6082-T6 hydroformed aluminium tube, and roll-formed closed section. Physics: closed hydroformed section gives higher bending/torsional stiffness per mass than open welded channels and removes flange stress risers, improving A-pillar moment capacity for roof crush and reducing slim-pillar deflection.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus a seven-piece stamped/welded node, one hydroformed tube removes ~6 stampings, their dies and ~40 welds. Alternatives hot-stamping and roll forming evaluated. Fewer fixtures and reduced weld distortion improve dimensional control feeding the windscreen aperture. Offset: hydroform press, tube pre-bend and laser trim of ends.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Closed hydroformed node deletes stampings, welds and flange mass at once.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: closed section improves roof crush (FMVSS 216a) and reduces A-pillar intrusion; continuous tube removes weld-line fracture risk. NVH: torsional continuity raises front body modes aiding premium refinement. Durability: no flange crevices reduces corrosion initiation, protecting long warranty. Manufacturing risk of wall thinning at corners mitigated by pre-bend optimisation and wall-thickness CT sampling.

title: PHEV multi-material rocker offsets battery mass with aluminium extrusion
description: [PHEV] Re-architect the rocker as a 6082-T6 multi-cell aluminium extrusion bonded/riveted to a DP980 inner, offsetting PHEV battery and e-axle mass while protecting the side battery from pole intrusion. Mixed-material design recovers payload and range otherwise lost to powertrain mass.
submittedBy: Audi Q8 / Volvo XC90 PHEV benchmark

ideaData.title: PHEV multi-material rocker offsets battery mass with aluminium extrusion
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline rocker is an all-steel multi-stamping (DP590/HSLA) that is heavy and intrudes under pole load. The change is a 6082-T6 multi-void extrusion (crush-tuned webs) joined to a DP980 inner via SPR and crash adhesive, saving ~3.2kg/side to offset PHEV battery mass. Alternatives: 7075-T6 extrusion, AlSi10MnMg cast rocker ends, and CP1000 roll-formed steel. Physics: multi-cell extrusion absorbs pole energy progressively, shielding the cells, while the steel inner anchors cross-members. Mixed Al/steel cuts mass where it most affects PHEV payload and CO2.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus all-steel stamped rocker, an Al extrusion plus steel inner removes several stampings and adds SPR/adhesive joining. Alternatives roll forming and casting at ends considered. Extrusion is a continuous die, low tooling; cut-to-length and CNC notch. Net BOM down; mixed-material joining cell added. Offsets payload/range, easing battery cost per km.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Aluminium extrusion offsets PHEV mass and shields cells in pole impact.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: multi-cell extrusion tuned for Euro NCAP side pole protects PHEV side cells; DP980 inner anchors load path. Durability: galvanic Al/steel interface isolated by adhesive and e-coat compatibility, preserving premium warranty. NVH: stiff closed extrusion improves rocker modes and squeak/rattle margin. Off-road: rocker sill strength validated for jacking and rock-rail loads. Mass offset restores PHEV payload and refinement.

title: Dash-panel sub-assembly consolidation cuts twelve stampings
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Consolidate the dash/firewall sub-assembly from 19 to 7 parts using tailor-welded blanks and larger nested stampings in DP590/HSLA. Reduces weld flanges, sealing length and fixture count across all powertrain firewalls while improving NVH sealing of the cabin bulkhead.

ideaData.title: Dash-panel sub-assembly consolidation cuts twelve stampings
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline dash sub-assembly uses 19 small pressings with many overlaps and sealant seams. The change uses tailor-welded blanks (HSLA S340 + DP590) and larger draw stampings to cut to 7 parts, reducing weld and sealing length. Alternatives: hydroformed cowl tube, one-piece hot-stamped bulkhead, and composite SMC dash insert for ICE heat zones. Physics: fewer joints lower leak paths and stress risers; TWB places thickness only where crash and NVH demand, trimming mass and tuning bulkhead stiffness for powertrain isolation.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus 19 discrete stampings, TWBs and larger nested draws cut to 7 parts, deleting dies, welds and sealant runs. Alternatives hot-stamped one-piece and hydroformed cowl considered. Fewer fixtures and shorter seal length speed takt and improve quality. Offset: TWB laser welding and larger press tonnage.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Larger nested and tailor-welded parts collapse the bulkhead BOM.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: bulkhead integrity for frontal (UN/ECE R94) maintained via TWB thickness placement; fewer welds reduce fracture initiation. NVH: shorter seal path and continuous panels cut airborne/structure-borne noise into the cabin, key for premium quietness. Durability: reduced crevices lower corrosion; sealing simplified. Process risk from TWB weld-line forming mitigated by formability mapping and draw-bead tuning.

title: Cast aluminium pack-ring integrates seat cross-members for BEV
description: [BEV] Replace the welded steel pack-perimeter ring and separate seat cross-members with an AlSi10MnMg cast/extruded hybrid ring that also forms seat mounts and pack seal land. Integrates the structural battery interface, cutting parts and joints on the skateboard floor.
submittedBy: NIO ES7 / Li Auto L9 benchmark

ideaData.title: Cast aluminium pack-ring integrates seat cross-members for BEV
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline pack ring is stamped DP980 segments welded to separate seat cross-members and a sealing frame. The change combines AlSi10MnMg cast corners with 6082-T6 extruded straight runs (FSW/bolted), integrating seat mounts and the pack gasket land. Alternatives: all-extruded ring, roll-formed CP1000 steel ring, and Mg AM60B corner castings. Physics: integrated ring carries side-crash load around the cells and provides a flat, machinable sealing surface, while cast seat bosses transfer occupant loads directly into the pack structure improving floor stiffness.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus welded steel ring plus separate cross-members, the cast/extruded hybrid integrates seat mounts and seal land, removing stampings and many welds. Alternatives all-extruded and roll-formed steel considered. FSW corners and machined seal face replace weld-and-seal. Offset: casting dies, extrusion, FSW cell and CNC seal machining.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Integrated pack ring merges seat structure, seal land and crash perimeter.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: ring tuned for side pole and barrier to shield cells (UN/ECE R100/R94); FSW and cast corners give continuous load path. Durability: machined seal land improves pack ingress (IP67) reliability, protecting battery warranty. NVH: stiff integrated floor ring lifts global body modes for premium refinement. Risk of casting porosity at seal land mitigated by vacuum HPDC and leak-test gating.

title: Remote laser welding replaces spot welds on floor pan
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Convert ~900 resistance spot welds on the steel floor pan to remote laser stitch welds, raising joint stiffness per unit and freeing flange width. Cuts cycle time and electrode cost across all powertrain floor structures while improving sealing continuity.

ideaData.title: Remote laser welding replaces spot welds on floor pan
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline floor uses ~900 RSW with wide flanges for gun access. The change applies remote (scanner) laser welding with short stitches on HSLA S340 and DP590, raising weld continuity and allowing narrower flanges. Alternatives: laser braze, hybrid laser-arc, and adhesive-bonded floor. Physics: laser stitches give higher shear stiffness per length than spot welds and remove gun-access flange penalty, trimming mass; non-contact scanning lifts throughput. Galvanised coating handled via gap control for degassing.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus RSW, remote laser welding deletes electrode dressing/tip wear, narrows flanges and raises takt with one scanner per zone. Alternatives hybrid laser-arc and bonding considered. Fixturing must hold tight gaps for laser. Net: lower consumable and cycle cost, modest mass save from flange reduction. Offset: laser cell and clamping precision.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Laser stitches replace spot welds, cutting cycle and consumable cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: stitch pattern engineered to match RSW shear/peel for floor cross-member loads (Euro NCAP); continuous stitches reduce unzipping risk. NVH: stiffer continuous joints improve floor panel modes and road-noise isolation valued in premium SUVs. Durability: sealed laser seams plus sealer resist corrosion. Process risk from zinc-induced porosity mitigated by gap control and inline weld monitoring.

title: Aluminium HPDC shock towers replace eight-part welded assemblies
description: [ICE,MHEV,BEV] Replace welded eight-part steel shock towers with AlSi10MnMg HPDC towers tied into the front rails, cutting part and weld count and stiffening the strut hardpoint. Applicable across ICE, MHEV and BEV front structures for consistent steering precision.

ideaData.title: Aluminium HPDC shock towers replace eight-part welded assemblies
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/BEV. Baseline towers are 8 stampings (DP590/HSLA) welded with strut-mount reinforcements. The change casts each tower in AlSi10MnMg (T7) with integrated brace and rail interface. Alternatives: AE44 creep-resistant Mg casting, 7075-T6 forged tower, and 6082-T6 fabricated tower. Physics: monolithic casting raises strut-mount local stiffness, improving camber stability and steering feel while removing weld stack-up; cast ribs distribute strut loads into the rail and plenum. T7 ductility ensures crash-load tolerance at the tower.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus 8-part welded steel, HPDC towers cut ~7 stampings and ~30 welds each side, plus their dies and jigs. Alternatives forging and Mg casting evaluated. One die per tower with vacuum HPDC; CNC of mount faces. Net BOM and weld reduction; offset by casting tooling and machining of strut interface.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Cast towers consolidate parts and stiffen the strut hardpoint.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: T7 ductility and graded walls keep front crash path intact (UN/ECE R94). NVH: stiffer strut towers improve steering precision and reduce road-noise transfer, key premium attributes. Durability: strut-load fatigue validated; cast porosity at mount controlled by vacuum HPDC and CT. Repair limited to bolt-on brace, bounding warranty. On-road comfort improved by stable camber under load.

title: Tailor-welded boron B-pillar removes separate reinforcement
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace a B-pillar with bolt-on reinforcement by a tailor-welded/tailor-rolled 22MnB5 hot-stamped blank with soft lower zone. Deletes the reinforcement part and welds while tuning intrusion and pelvis-load response across all powertrain side structures.

ideaData.title: Tailor-welded boron B-pillar removes separate reinforcement
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline B-pillar is a 22MnB5 outer plus a separate inner reinforcement spot-welded in. The change uses a tailor-welded blank (22MnB5 + softer 22MnB5 lower) or tailor-rolled blank, hot-stamped to one part with a tough lower crush zone, deleting the reinforcement. Alternatives: DP980/CP1000 cold-stamped multi-piece, press-hardened MnB with laser-decoated soft zones, and 7075-T6 cast/forged insert. Physics: graded strength gives 1500MPa upper intrusion resistance and ductile lower fold for pelvis/abdomen loads, removing the separate part and its 20+ welds.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus outer-plus-reinforcement, a tailor-welded/rolled hot-stamped blank yields one pillar, deleting a part, its die and 20+ welds. Alternatives cold-stamped multi-piece and forged insert considered. One press-hardening line with TWB laser weld or TRB rolling. Offset: TWB welding/rolling and blanking complexity; net assembly simplification.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Graded hot-stamped pillar removes the reinforcement part and its welds.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: tailored hardness meets Euro NCAP side pole intrusion up top and tuned pelvis load below; single part removes weld-line failure risk. Durability: hot-stamp Al-Si coating plus e-coat resists corrosion, protecting premium warranty. NVH: stiffer one-piece pillar improves side-aperture modes. Mass save offsets powertrain weight across variants. Process risk in soft-zone control mitigated by tool temperature mapping.

title: Hybrid front-end carrier merges crash, cooling and pedestrian functions
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace a steel front-end module and separate cooling/crash brackets with a plastic-metal hybrid carrier (GFRP over DP590 inlays) integrating cooling mounts, crash cans and pedestrian beam. Cuts part count and mass across all powertrains while tuning low-speed damageability.

ideaData.title: Hybrid front-end carrier merges crash, cooling and pedestrian functions
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline front-end is steel stampings plus many brackets for cooling, lamps and crash cans. The change overmoulds GFRP (PA6-GF30) onto DP590 metal inlays, integrating cooling-module mounts, lamp datums and a pedestrian lower stiffener. Alternatives: AlSi10MnMg cast carrier, 6082-T6 fabricated frame, and SMC composite carrier. Physics: metal inlays carry crash-can and tow loads while ribbed GFRP handles stiffness and mounting, slashing bracket count and mass forward of the front axle where it aids weight distribution and damageability.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus steel module plus brackets, hybrid overmoulding integrates ~10 brackets into one carrier, removing stampings, fasteners and welds. Alternatives cast and SMC carriers considered. Injection over-moulding with metal inlay insert; crash cans bolt on. Offset: moulding tool and inlay handling. Net BOM, fastener and mass reduction up front.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Function integration into one hybrid carrier removes brackets and fasteners.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: metal inlays carry crash-can loads for low-speed (RCAR) and pedestrian (UN/ECE R127) requirements; GFRP tuned for lower-leg. Durability: thermal cycling of metal/plastic interface validated; corrosion of inlays e-coated. NVH: stiff carrier supports cooling module, cutting front-end shake. Mass reduction forward improves premium ride balance. Repair via bolt-on crash cans bounds warranty.

title: Extruded aluminium battery floor sandwich replaces stamped steel
description: [BEV] Replace the stamped steel BEV floor over the pack with a 6082-T6 multi-void extruded sandwich that doubles as pack lid and floor stiffener. Saves mass and parts while improving torsional stiffness of the skateboard and side-impact protection of the cells.

ideaData.title: Extruded aluminium battery floor sandwich replaces stamped steel
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline floor is stamped HSLA/DP steel bolted above a separate pack lid. The change uses 6082-T6 multi-void extrusions (FSW-joined) that serve as both structural floor and pack top, removing the duplicate lid. Alternatives: AlSi10MnMg cast floor, CFRP sandwich lid, and roll-formed CP1000 steel floor. Physics: closed extruded voids give high bending/torsional stiffness per mass and a flat sealing interface; integrating floor and lid removes a stack of parts and seals, raising body torsional rigidity that defines premium BEV handling.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus stamped floor plus separate lid, integrated extruded sandwich deletes the lid, fasteners and a seal interface. Alternatives cast and CFRP considered. FSW of extrusions and CNC seal land replace stamping and bolting. Offset: extrusion dies, FSW cell, machining. Net mass, part and seal reduction on the skateboard.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Dual-function extruded floor/lid removes a part stack and seal.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: extruded voids absorb side-impact energy protecting cells (UN/ECE R100); FSW seams continuous and inspectable. Durability: machined seal land secures IP67 pack sealing, protecting battery warranty. NVH: high torsional rigidity improves premium ride/handling and reduces structure-borne noise. Thermal: floor doubles as cooling interface. Risk of FSW defects mitigated by force-controlled welding and phased-array inspection.

title: Commonised floor cross-members across ICE/PHEV/BEV variants
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Standardise seat and tunnel cross-member sections so one set of roll-formed CP800 parts serves ICE, PHEV and BEV floors via common datums. Cuts unique part numbers, tooling and logistics across the SUV family while keeping crash performance per powertrain.

ideaData.title: Commonised floor cross-members across ICE/PHEV/BEV variants
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline floors carry powertrain-specific cross-members with unique tooling. The change defines common roll-formed CP800 sections with shared mounting datums, locally reinforced per variant (tunnel for ICE/PHEV, flat for BEV). Alternatives: hot-stamped 22MnB5 common beams, 6082-T6 extruded commons, and TWB-tuned commons. Physics: a common section sized for the worst-case load case is locally patched, so one profile covers all powertrains; roll forming gives long, constant sections cheaply and consistently, slashing unique parts and dies.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus powertrain-unique cross-members, commonised roll-formed sections cut unique part numbers and dies markedly. Alternatives hot stamping and extrusion considered. Roll forming yields continuous sections cut to length with local patches. Offset: patch parts and validation per variant. Net: tooling, inventory and logistics reduction across the family.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One cross-member family covers all powertrains, cutting unique tooling.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: worst-case sizing plus local patches keeps each variant compliant with Euro NCAP frontal/side; common datums protect load paths. Durability: roll-formed sections corrosion-treated consistently. NVH: shared stiff sections maintain floor modes; local tuning preserves powertrain-specific isolation. Logistics: fewer SKUs lowers complexity risk. Validation effort per variant mitigated by reusing common base CAE models.

title: Roll-formed roof rail replaces stamped two-piece weldment
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace a two-piece stamped roof rail with a single roll-formed CP800 closed section, deleting a part, weld flanges and dies. Improves roof crush and torsional continuity across all powertrain bodies at low investment.

ideaData.title: Roll-formed roof rail replaces stamped two-piece weldment
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline roof rail is two stampings welded into a closed box. The change roll-forms a single CP800 closed section to length, deleting the second part and its welds. Alternatives: 22MnB5 hot-stamped rail, 6082-T6 extruded rail, and hydroformed tube. Physics: roll forming produces a uniform high-strength closed section ideal for the long, straight roof rail, giving continuous load path for roof crush and torsion without weld lines, while constant section reduces mass variability.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus two-piece stamped/welded rail, one roll-formed section removes a stamping, its die and the closing welds. Alternatives hot stamping and extrusion considered. Roll forming is low-tooling and high-rate for long parts; ends laser-trimmed. Offset: roll-form line setup. Net die, weld and mass-variability reduction.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Single roll-formed section deletes a part and the closing welds.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: continuous closed section improves roof crush (FMVSS 216a) versus welded box; no weld-line fracture. NVH: torsional continuity lifts roof modes aiding refinement. Durability: fewer flanges reduces corrosion sites, protecting warranty. Off-road: stiff roof rail supports roof-rack and rollover loads. Process risk of springback at high strength mitigated by roll-pass design and end-of-line check fixtures.

title: Magnesium AM60B cross-car beam replaces steel/plastic assembly
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace a steel-and-plastic cross-car beam (cockpit) with a single AM60B magnesium HPDC integrating HVAC, steering-column and airbag mounts. Cuts mass and parts behind the dash across all powertrains, improving steering-column NVH.

ideaData.title: Magnesium AM60B cross-car beam replaces steel/plastic assembly
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline cross-car beam is a welded steel tube with many brackets and plastic ducts. The change casts an AM60B Mg beam with integrated column, HVAC and knee-bolster mounts. Alternatives: AE44 creep-resistant Mg, AlSi10MnMg aluminium casting, and hydroformed steel tube with plastic carrier. Physics: Mg casting integrates dozens of brackets, cutting mass ~40% behind the dash and raising the steering-column first mode to reduce shake. T-rated Mg with corrosion isolation carries airbag and column loads with high specific stiffness.
ideaData.materialGrade: AM60B Mg HPDC / AE44 Mg / AlSi10MnMg
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus welded steel tube plus brackets, one Mg casting integrates ~20 brackets, deleting stampings, welds and fasteners. Alternatives Al casting and hydroformed steel considered. HPDC with vacuum and CNC of mount faces; isolation coatings applied. Offset: Mg casting cell, corrosion protection. Net large bracket, fastener and mass reduction.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Magnesium casting integrates cockpit brackets and cuts dash mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: beam carries airbag reaction and knee-bolster loads validated to UN/ECE R94/R12; ductile AM60B avoids brittle failure. NVH: higher column first mode reduces steering shake, a premium quality cue. Durability: galvanic isolation (coatings, isolation washers) protects Mg from corrosion, preserving warranty. Risk of Mg creep at mounts mitigated by AE44 in hot zones and bolt-load management.

title: Laser-welded door-ring blank consolidates A/B-pillar and rocker
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Adopt a one-piece laser-welded door-ring blank (22MnB5 + DP grades) hot-stamped to merge A-pillar, roof rail, B-pillar and rocker outer. Removes flange welds and overlaps, cutting parts and improving side-crash continuity across all powertrains.

ideaData.title: Laser-welded door-ring blank consolidates A/B-pillar and rocker
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline bodyside outer is several stampings welded along pillars and rocker. The change laser-welds a tailored blank (22MnB5 1500MPa pillars + DP590 transitions + softer rocker) then hot-stamps one door ring. Alternatives: cold-stamped multi-piece DP980/CP1000, roll-formed pillars with cast nodes, and 7075-T6 fabricated ring. Physics: continuous ring removes overlap welds at the highest side-crash load paths, giving uninterrupted strength from rocker through B-pillar to roof, while tailored thickness/grade tunes intrusion and fold zones, trimming part count and mass.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus multi-piece welded bodyside, a laser-welded door-ring blank cuts several stampings and dozens of perimeter welds. Alternatives cold-stamped and roll-formed considered. One large press-hardening line plus blank laser welding; large transfer die. Offset: blank welding cost and press tonnage. Net major part/weld reduction and dimensional gain.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One-piece door ring deletes pillar overlaps and perimeter welds.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: continuous ring meets Euro NCAP side pole and barrier with tailored hardness; no weld-line failures along load path. Durability: Al-Si coated press-hardened steel resists corrosion; fewer crevices, protecting warranty. NVH: stiffer one-piece side aperture lifts global modes for refinement. Process risk of soft-zone and weld-line formability mitigated by formability mapping and tool-temperature control.

title: Bonded-and-hemmed aluminium tailgate replaces welded steel
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace a welded steel tailgate with a bonded-and-hemmed 6014-T4 aluminium inner/outer, cutting closure mass at the vehicle rear and lowering gas-strut and hinge loads. Mass save aids rear weight balance across all powertrains and range for BEV.

ideaData.title: Bonded-and-hemmed aluminium tailgate replaces welded steel
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline tailgate is steel inner/outer spot-welded with reinforcements. The change uses 6014-T4 outer and 6082-T6 inner, hem-flanged and structurally bonded with toughened epoxy, plus a few SPR at hinge/latch. Alternatives: SMC composite tailgate, GFRP-SMC hybrid, and DP590 lightweight steel. Physics: aluminium plus bonding cuts mass ~40% at the extreme rear, reducing hinge/strut loads and improving polar balance; adhesive distributes load and isolates galvanic contact at steel fasteners. Stiffness met by bonded inner ribs.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus welded steel tailgate, bonded-and-hemmed aluminium removes spot welds and steel reinforcements, adding hemming and adhesive cure. Alternatives SMC and steel considered. Hemming press plus adhesive dispense and oven cure replace spot welding. Offset: aluminium cost and cure time. Net mass cut and hinge/strut downsizing.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Aluminium bonded closure cuts rear mass and downsizes hinges/struts.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: rear closure meets rear-impact and latch retention (FMVSS 206); bonded inner carries latch loads. Durability: hemmed/bonded edges sealed against corrosion; galvanic isolation at steel fasteners protects premium paint warranty. NVH: bonded panel damps better than welded steel, cutting tailgate boom. Strut/hinge downsizing improves ease-of-use. Adhesive durability validated by KSII and ageing.

title: Rear gigacast integrates tow bracket and rear crash rails
description: [BEV] Extend the rear gigacasting to integrate tow-hitch reinforcement, rear crash rails and damper mounts in AlSi10MnMg, removing a bolt-on tow sub-frame and welded rail set. Consolidates the BEV rear structure and stabilises trailer and crash load paths.

ideaData.title: Rear gigacast integrates tow bracket and rear crash rails
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline rear has a cast/stamped underbody plus a separate welded tow sub-frame and rail set. The change integrates tow reinforcement, crash rails and rear damper mounts into the AlSi10MnMg gigacasting (T7) with locally thickened tow boss. Alternatives: 6082-T6 extruded rear frame, AE44 Mg damper-mount casting, and DP980 fabricated tow module. Physics: integrated cast bosses carry static tow and dynamic trailer loads while graded-wall rails absorb rear crash; consolidation removes a sub-frame, its fasteners and welds, stiffening the rear for premium towing-rated SUVs.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus separate tow sub-frame plus rails, integrating into the gigacasting deletes a module, its fasteners and welds. Alternatives extruded frame and fabricated module considered. Larger die with local thick sections; CNC of tow and damper interfaces. Offset: casting/inspection. Net sub-frame deletion and assembly simplification.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Tow and crash integration removes the rear sub-frame and its joints.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: graded rails meet UN/ECE R34 rear; cast tow boss validated for 2.5–3.5t trailer fatigue load. NVH: integrated stiff rear lifts modes and reduces trailer-induced shake. Durability: tow-load fatigue and porosity CT control casting risk. Off-road: rear recovery-point loads validated. Repair via bolt-on bumper crash cans bounds warranty exposure on the casting.

title: Steel-aluminium hybrid C-pillar balances strength and mass
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Build the C-pillar as a DP980 inner with 6082-T6 aluminium outer joined by SPR and adhesive, cutting upper-body mass for a lower roll centre while meeting roof-crush. Mixed-material design benefits handling across all powertrains and lowers BEV centre of gravity contributions.

ideaData.title: Steel-aluminium hybrid C-pillar balances strength and mass
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline C-pillar is all DP/HSLA steel, heavy high in the body. The change keeps a DP980 structural inner for roof-crush while using a 6082-T6 outer joined by SPR plus crash adhesive, trimming upper mass. Alternatives: 22MnB5 hot-stamped inner, AlSi10MnMg cast node at the belt, and CFRP outer. Physics: putting steel where peak intrusion strength is needed and aluminium on the lower-stress outer cuts mass high in the structure, lowering CG and roll inertia for sharper handling, while adhesive isolates the galvanic couple.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus all-steel C-pillar, a steel-inner/aluminium-outer hybrid removes steel mass and adds SPR/adhesive joining. Alternatives hot-stamped inner and cast node considered. SPR plus adhesive cell replaces some spot welds. Offset: mixed-material joining and corrosion control. Net upper-body mass reduction with maintained crash strength.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Steel-where-needed, aluminium-elsewhere cuts upper-body mass and CG.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: DP980 inner secures roof crush (FMVSS 216a) and side intrusion; aluminium outer non-structural in peak path. Durability: SPR/adhesive isolates Al/steel galvanic couple; sealed against corrosion, protecting warranty. NVH: bonded outer damps panel resonance. Handling: lower CG and roll inertia improve premium agility. Process risk in mixed-material fatigue mitigated by KSII and corrosion validation.

title: Wheelhouse sub-assembly consolidation via deep-draw nesting
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Consolidate the rear wheelhouse sub-assembly from 11 to 5 deep-drawn DP340/HSLA parts, deleting weld flanges and sealing seams. Improves dimensional control of the rear suspension mounts and reduces road-noise leak paths across all powertrains.

ideaData.title: Wheelhouse sub-assembly consolidation via deep-draw nesting
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline wheelhouse is 11 small pressings with overlaps near suspension mounts. The change uses deeper-draw DP340/HSLA stampings nesting several functions into 5 parts. Alternatives: AlSi10MnMg cast wheelhouse, 6082-T6 fabricated arch, and TWB-tuned stampings. Physics: fewer, larger panels reduce weld stack-up and improve mount-datum repeatability, tightening rear axle kinematics and lowering structure-borne noise; deeper draws place material continuously around the arch, raising local stiffness for damper-top loads.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus 11 small stampings, deeper-draw nesting cuts to 5 parts, removing dies, welds and sealant seams. Alternatives casting and fabrication considered. Larger draw dies and binder control needed. Offset: higher press tonnage. Net die, weld and seal reduction with better suspension-mount dimensional control.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Deeper-draw nesting collapses wheelhouse parts and weld seams.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: rear structure load paths preserved (UN/ECE R34); fewer welds reduce fracture risk near mounts. NVH: tighter mount datums and fewer leak paths cut road noise, valued in premium SUVs. Durability: fewer crevices lower corrosion; suspension-load fatigue validated. On-road comfort improved by stable axle kinematics. Process risk in deep draw (splits/wrinkles) mitigated by formability simulation and draw-bead tuning.

title: Friction-stir-welded aluminium pack tray replaces bolted assembly
description: [BEV] Replace a bolted/sealed multi-part aluminium battery tray with a friction-stir-welded 6082-T6 extruded tray, removing fasteners, gaskets and leak paths. Improves pack sealing and structural integration of the BEV underbody.

ideaData.title: Friction-stir-welded aluminium pack tray replaces bolted assembly
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline pack tray bolts extruded rails to stamped floors with gaskets, creating many leak paths. The change friction-stir-welds 6082-T6 extrusions into a sealed tray with integral cooling channels. Alternatives: AlSi10MnMg cast tray, MIG-welded aluminium tray, and adhesive-bonded tray. Physics: FSW gives full-penetration, hermetic, low-distortion joints in 6xxx aluminium without filler, eliminating gasketed bolt joints and improving long-term IP sealing; integral void channels carry coolant, integrating thermal and structural functions in the BEV floor.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus bolted/sealed tray, FSW deletes fasteners and gaskets and yields hermetic joints. Alternatives casting and MIG welding considered. FSW cell with phased-array NDT replaces bolting/sealing stations. Offset: FSW fixturing and inspection. Net fastener/gasket reduction and sealing-quality gain.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: FSW removes fasteners and gaskets, hermetically sealing the pack tray.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: FSW tray integrated into floor protects cells in side/underbody loads (UN/ECE R100); continuous welds avoid bolt-joint unzip. Durability: hermetic seal improves IP67 reliability and corrosion resistance, cutting battery warranty risk. NVH: rigid welded tray raises floor modes. Thermal: integral channels manage cell temperature. Risk of FSW root defects mitigated by force control and 100% phased-array inspection.

title: PHEV tunnel reinforcement uses aluminium extrusion for mass offset
description: [PHEV] Replace heavy stamped steel tunnel reinforcements with a 6082-T6 extruded tunnel beam bonded to the steel floor, offsetting PHEV high-voltage battery and cabling mass while housing wiring and coolant lines. Recovers payload lost to electrification on the SUV.
submittedBy: Mercedes GLE PHEV / BMW X5 benchmark

ideaData.title: PHEV tunnel reinforcement uses aluminium extrusion for mass offset
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: PHEV. Baseline tunnel uses stacked steel stampings (DP590/HSLA) and brackets to carry torsion and route services. The change bonds a 6082-T6 multi-void extrusion along the tunnel, carrying torsional load and integrating cable/coolant routing, saving ~2kg. Alternatives: 7075-T6 extrusion, AlSi10MnMg cast tunnel ends, and CP1000 roll-formed steel. Physics: closed extruded section gives high torsional stiffness per mass and integral channels for HV cabling, removing brackets; bonding to steel floor isolates galvanic contact and damps panels, offsetting PHEV mass centrally.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus stacked steel tunnel reinforcements, an extruded beam removes stampings and routing brackets, adding bonding/SPR. Alternatives roll forming and casting considered. Extrusion cut-to-length plus bonding cell. Offset: galvanic isolation and adhesive cure. Net stamping/bracket reduction and central mass offset for PHEV.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Extruded tunnel beam offsets PHEV mass and integrates service routing.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: tunnel beam maintains torsional and frontal load paths (UN/ECE R94); bonded joints validated by KSII. Durability: galvanic Al/steel isolation and sealing protect against corrosion, preserving warranty. NVH: stiff bonded tunnel damps and raises floor torsion mode improving refinement. Packaging: integral channels protect HV cabling routing. Mass offset restores PHEV payload and central balance for handling.

title: Hydroformed aluminium front cradle replaces fabricated steel
description: [ICE,MHEV,BEV] Replace a multi-piece fabricated steel front cradle with a hydroformed 6082-T6 aluminium tubular cradle, cutting unsprung-adjacent mass and welds while tuning powertrain-mount stiffness. Serves ICE/MHEV engine cradles and BEV motor cradles via shared geometry.

ideaData.title: Hydroformed aluminium front cradle replaces fabricated steel
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/BEV. Baseline cradle is fabricated steel stampings and tubes (HSLA/DP) heavily welded. The change hydroforms 6082-T6 aluminium tubes into a closed cradle with cast AlSi10MnMg corner nodes (MIG/FSW). Alternatives: AlSi10MnMg full HPDC cradle, 7075-T6 forged arms, and DP980 fabricated steel. Physics: closed hydroformed tubes give high bending/torsional stiffness per mass for powertrain-mount and suspension loads, cutting mass low in the vehicle; node castings localise mount stiffness, tuning NVH isolation across ICE engine and BEV motor applications via common architecture.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus fabricated steel cradle, hydroformed aluminium tubes plus cast nodes cut stampings and many welds. Alternatives full HPDC and forging considered. Tube pre-bend, hydroform press and node welding replace heavy stamping/welding. Offset: hydroform tooling and aluminium welding. Net weld/mass reduction with common ICE/BEV geometry.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Hydroformed aluminium cradle cuts mass and welds with shared ICE/BEV geometry.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: cradle deforms predictably for frontal (UN/ECE R94) and detaches as designed; closed tubes carry crash and tow. NVH: node-tuned mount stiffness isolates engine/motor vibration for premium quietness. Durability: suspension and mount fatigue validated; aluminium corrosion via coatings. Handling: lower mass low in body aids agility. Risk of tube thinning at bends mitigated by pre-bend and CT wall checks.

title: Commonised bodyside outer across ICE and BEV derivatives
description: [ICE,MHEV,BEV] Engineer one bodyside outer with shared apertures and hardpoints serving ICE, MHEV and BEV derivatives, with powertrain-specific reinforcements added inboard. Slashes large stamping tools and unique parts across the SUV family while preserving crash and NVH per variant.

ideaData.title: Commonised bodyside outer across ICE and BEV derivatives
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/BEV. Baseline carries powertrain-specific bodyside outers with separate expensive dies. The change defines a common DP590/22MnB5 door-ring outer with shared apertures, locally reinforced inboard for BEV rocker/pack loads or ICE exhaust packaging. Alternatives: separate cold-stamped outers, fully cast bodyside nodes, and roll-formed common pillars. Physics: outer skin and apertures are largely powertrain-agnostic; tailoring inboard reinforcements meets each variant's crash/NVH while reusing the most expensive large dies, cutting tooling and unique part numbers dramatically.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus per-powertrain bodyside outers, one common outer reuses the largest dies; only inboard reinforcements differ. Alternatives separate outers and cast nodes considered. Shared transfer dies plus variant patch tooling. Offset: reinforcement validation per variant. Net massive large-die and unique-part reduction across family.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Shared bodyside outer reuses the most expensive dies across powertrains.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: common apertures plus variant reinforcements meet Euro NCAP side pole for each derivative; shared hardpoints protect kinematics. Durability: common corrosion treatment across variants. NVH: variant inboard tuning preserves powertrain-specific isolation while common outer keeps modes. Logistics: fewer SKUs reduce complexity. Validation per variant mitigated by shared base CAE and crash carryover.

title: Magnesium rear damper-tower casting cuts mass and parts
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace welded steel rear damper towers with AE44 magnesium castings tied into the wheelhouse, cutting mass high and rearward while stiffening the damper top. Improves rear-axle NVH and weight balance across all powertrains.

ideaData.title: Magnesium rear damper-tower casting cuts mass and parts
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline rear damper towers are welded steel stampings with reinforcements. The change casts AE44 creep-resistant magnesium towers integrating the damper mount and wheelhouse interface. Alternatives: AM60B Mg, AlSi10MnMg aluminium casting, and DP980 fabricated steel. Physics: Mg casting gives high specific stiffness at the damper top, raising local mode and reducing road-noise transfer, while cutting mass high and rearward for better balance; AE44 resists creep under sustained bolt and thermal load at the mount.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus welded steel towers, Mg castings integrate reinforcements, removing stampings and welds. Alternatives Al casting and steel considered. HPDC with vacuum plus CNC of damper face and isolation coatings. Offset: Mg casting and corrosion protection. Net stamping/weld reduction and rearward mass cut.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Magnesium damper towers cut rearward mass and stiffen the mount.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: rear structure loads preserved (UN/ECE R34); ductile Mg avoids brittle failure at tower. NVH: higher damper-top mode reduces road noise, a premium attribute. Durability: AE44 creep resistance and galvanic isolation protect the mount, preserving warranty. Handling: lower rearward mass improves balance.
