Risk of Mg corrosion mitigated by isolation washers, coatings and sealed e-coat-compatible interfaces.

title: Structural-adhesive floor stiffeners replace welded brackets
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Replace spot-welded floor stiffener brackets with bonded top-hat stiffeners using crash-durable epoxy, raising floor panel stiffness without weld distortion. Cuts welds and brackets and lowers road noise across all powertrain floors.

ideaData.title: Structural-adhesive floor stiffeners replace welded brackets
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline floor uses spot-welded steel brackets and beads for stiffness, causing weld distortion. The change bonds DP340 top-hat stiffeners with crash-durable toughened epoxy, raising panel stiffness with no thermal distortion. Alternatives: laser-stitch-welded stiffeners, expandable structural foam baffles, and rivet-bonded stiffeners. Physics: continuous adhesive bondline increases composite section stiffness and damps the panel, reducing booming and road noise, while removing welds that warp thin floor panels and require rework.
ideaData.materialGrade: DP340 stiffener / toughened epoxy / structural foam
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus welded brackets, bonded stiffeners delete spot welds and reduce distortion rework. Alternatives laser stitch and structural foam considered. Adhesive dispense plus fixturing and oven cure replace welding. Offset: bondline control and cure time. Net weld reduction, less rework and better panel quality.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Bonded stiffeners replace welds, removing distortion and damping the floor.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: crash-durable epoxy retains stiffener function in floor loadcases (Euro NCAP); KSII-validated. Durability: bondline sealed against corrosion and aged-validated, protecting warranty. NVH: continuous bonded stiffener damps floor booming and road noise, valued in premium quietness. Quality: no weld warpage improves dimensional consistency. Process risk from bondline voids mitigated by inline dispense monitoring and ageing tests.

title: Front gigacast with replaceable crash horns limits repair cost
description: [BEV] Cast the front underbody in AlSi10MnMg but decouple the crushable front horns as bolt-on segments, capturing gigacast consolidation savings while bounding collision-repair cost. Balances part-count reduction against insurability on the BEV front structure.

ideaData.title: Front gigacast with replaceable crash horns limits repair cost
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline either a fully welded steel front (repairable but many parts) or a monolithic gigacast (cheap but costly to repair). The change casts the load-bearing front underbody in AlSi10MnMg (T7) yet bolts on replaceable crush horns in 6082-T6 extrusion or DP980. Alternatives: full one-piece gigacast, all-steel fabricated front, and extrusion space-frame. Physics: the casting carries integrated hardpoints and stiffness while sacrificial bolt-on horns absorb low/medium-speed crash, so a collision damages only replaceable parts, preserving consolidation savings without a high insurance/repair penalty.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus a monolithic gigacast, decoupling crush horns keeps most part-count savings while making the crash zone replaceable. Alternatives full cast and steel fabricated considered. One casting die plus extruded/stamped horns and bolt interfaces. Offset: horn parts and fasteners. Net consolidation retained with far lower repair cost.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Bolt-on crush horns preserve gigacast savings while bounding repair cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: bolt-on horns absorb RCAR low-speed and contribute to UN/ECE R94 frontal; casting carries residual load. Durability: horn interfaces fatigue-validated; casting porosity CT-controlled. Insurability: replaceable crash zone lowers collision-repair cost, a key premium-SUV ownership metric. NVH: stiff casting maintains front modes. Risk of bolt-joint loosening mitigated by validated preload and locking fasteners.

title: Tailor-rolled floor blanks tune thickness for all powertrains
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Use tailor-rolled DP590 floor blanks to place thickness only where each powertrain needs it, cutting mass versus uniform-gauge stampings while serving ICE tunnel and BEV flat floors from one rolling process. Saves mass across the family.

ideaData.title: Tailor-rolled floor blanks tune thickness for all powertrains
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline floors use uniform-gauge stampings sized for the worst local load, wasting mass elsewhere. The change uses tailor-rolled blanks (DP590, continuously varying 1.0–1.8mm) so thickness follows load, with profiles tuned per powertrain (tunnel vs flat). Alternatives: tailor-welded blanks, patchwork reinforcements, and 6082-T6 sheet. Physics: continuously variable gauge removes over-thickness, cutting mass while maintaining crash and stiffness where needed; one rolling line feeds all variants by changing the thickness profile, supporting commonised processing across powertrains.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus uniform-gauge floors, tailor-rolled blanks remove excess gauge and consolidate processing. Alternatives TWB and patchwork considered. Tailor-rolling mill plus existing stamping; profile change per variant. Offset: rolling setup and formability validation. Net mass reduction and common processing across powertrains.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Variable-gauge blanks cut floor mass and serve all powertrains from one line.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: thickness placed for frontal/side loadcases (Euro NCAP) maintains performance; thin fields validated for buckling. Durability: corrosion-treated consistently; fatigue of thickness transitions validated. NVH: gauge tuning manages floor modes and road-noise transfer. Mass save offsets powertrain weight across variants. Process risk in transition-zone forming mitigated by formability mapping and draw control.

title: Laser-brazed steel-aluminium roof joint enables mixed structure
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Join an aluminium roof panel to the steel bodyside via laser braze with a zinc-aluminium filler and engineered interlayer, enabling a lightweight mixed-material roof without galvanic failure. Cuts roof mass high in the body across all powertrains.

ideaData.title: Laser-brazed steel-aluminium roof joint enables mixed structure
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline roof is steel welded to steel sides. The change uses a 6014-T4 aluminium roof brazed to the galvanised DP bodyside using a Zn-Al filler with an interlayer to control intermetallics, enabling a lightweight roof high in the structure. Alternatives: SPR/adhesive roof hem, cold-metal-transfer welding, and full aluminium upper body. Physics: controlled laser braze limits brittle Fe-Al intermetallic thickness while the interlayer and adhesive bead manage galvanic corrosion; aluminium roof cuts ~3kg high up, lowering CG and roll inertia for sharper premium handling.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus steel-to-steel roof welding, steel-aluminium laser braze enables a lighter roof but needs intermetallic and galvanic control. Alternatives SPR/adhesive hem and CMT considered. Laser braze cell with filler and interlayer plus sealing replaces spot welds. Offset: process control and sealing. Net mass cut high in the body.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Steel-aluminium braze enables a light roof, lowering CG.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: roof crush (FMVSS 216a) maintained; braze plus adhesive carries roof-rail shear. Durability: intermetallic control and galvanic isolation prevent joint corrosion, protecting premium warranty at an exposed seam. NVH: lighter roof lowers CG, aiding handling and reducing roof boom with damping. Process risk of brittle intermetallics mitigated by tight thermal control, interlayer and inline seam inspection.

title: Common gigacast underbody scaled across BEV SUV wheelbases
description: [BEV] Design the rear gigacast with modular inserts so one base AlSi10MnMg casting scales across short- and long-wheelbase BEV SUVs, sharing the largest die. Commonises the most expensive tool and consolidates parts across the BEV family.

ideaData.title: Common gigacast underbody scaled across BEV SUV wheelbases
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: BEV. Baseline would need a unique gigacast die per wheelbase, a very large investment. The change designs a common rear AlSi10MnMg gigacast core with bolt-on/insert extensions (6082-T6) for wheelbase and suspension variants, sharing the base die. Alternatives: separate dies per variant, extruded modular rear frame, and steel fabricated rear. Physics: the load-bearing casting core is wheelbase-agnostic; modular extensions adapt length and mounts, so the costliest die is amortised across the BEV family while retaining part consolidation and stiffness benefits.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus per-wheelbase gigacast dies, a common core plus inserts shares the largest, most expensive tool across variants. Alternatives separate dies and extruded frames considered. One base casting die plus modular extension tooling and CNC interfaces. Offset: insert parts and validation. Net huge die-investment commonisation and part consolidation.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One gigacast core scales across wheelbases, sharing the costliest die.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: common core plus variant inserts validated for UN/ECE R34/R100 per wheelbase; shared load paths protect cells. Durability: casting porosity CT-controlled; insert interfaces fatigue-validated. NVH: consistent stiff rear core maintains modes across variants. Investment: die sharing transforms gigacast business case for premium BEV families. Risk of insert-joint loosening mitigated by validated preload and locking fasteners.

title: Multi-material upper body mixes steel, aluminium and magnesium
description: [ICE,MHEV,PHEV,BEV] Re-architect the upper body as an optimised steel-aluminium-magnesium mix: 22MnB5 pillars, 6082-T6 roof/cantrail, AM60B header, joined by SPR, FDS, adhesive and laser braze. Cuts mass high in the structure across all powertrains for handling and efficiency.

ideaData.title: Multi-material upper body mixes steel, aluminium and magnesium
ideaData.technicalDescription: Powertrain: ICE/MHEV/PHEV/BEV. Baseline upper body is steel-dominant and heavy high up. The change applies multi-material optimisation: 22MnB5 1500MPa hot-stamped pillars for intrusion, 6082-T6 aluminium roof bow and cantrail for mass, and AM60B magnesium header/cross-members for specific stiffness, joined by SPR, flow-drill screws, structural adhesive and laser braze. Alternatives: all-aluminium upper body, CFRP roof, and DP980/CP1000 all-steel. Physics: matching material to local load and putting light alloys high up lowers CG and roll inertia for premium handling while steel guards crash zones; mixed joining manages galvanic couples.
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Versus steel-dominant upper body, a steel-aluminium-magnesium mix needs multi-material joining (SPR, FDS, adhesive, laser braze) replacing spot welds. Alternatives all-aluminium and CFRP considered. Mixed-material assembly cell with galvanic isolation and sealing. Offset: joining complexity and corrosion control. Net significant upper-body mass reduction with maintained crash.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Material-optimised mix cuts upper-body mass, lowering CG and roll inertia.

ideaData.riskNotes: Crash: 22MnB5 pillars meet Euro NCAP side pole and roof crush (FMVSS 216a); light alloys sit out of peak path. Durability: SPR/FDS/adhesive isolate Al/steel and Mg galvanic couples; sealed and coated to protect long premium warranty. NVH: lighter, stiffer upper body lowers CG and improves refinement. Handling: reduced roll inertia sharpens agility. Multi-material fatigue validated by KSII and corrosion cycling.
